title: atypical cartilaginous tumor.
content:
atypical cartilaginous tumor.

Microscopic description:
Sections show fragmented tissue composed of lobulated growth of tumor with abundant hyaline cartilaginous matrix and low cellularity. The tumor cells show small and condensed nuclei with binucleation. Mitoses and necrosis are absent. Entrapment of lamellar bone trabeculae is seen.

Histologic type: atypical cartilaginous tumor
Histologic Grade: grade I
Tumor Size: cannot be evaluated due to fragmentation
Mitotic Rate: not identified.
Necrosis: absent
Margins: The specimen consists of multiple unoriented
  fragments and the surgical margins cannot be assessed.
Lymphovascular Invasion: not identified
Treatment Effect: no known presurgical therapy

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: Brunner gland hamartoma with focal atrophy and residual NE cells
content:
Brunner gland hamartoma with focal atrophy and residual NE cells

The main lesion is a Brunner galnd hamartoma. The main problem is there is a fibromuscular tissue with some neuroendocrine cells in the submucosa. If you look at other part of the lesion, you can seen lesions transitional between Brunner galnd hamartoma and this type of lesion, such as that shown in Attached file, which shows Brunner gland, inflammation, fibrosis, and some NE cell nests. NE cells are relative resistant to injury. So in the process of inflammation and compression, such as chronic pancreatitis oand GVHD, NE cells are often survive even other cells are destructed.

title: chondroblastoma
content:
chondroblastoma.

title: Chondrogenic tumor (see description)
content:
Chondrogenic tumor (see description).
Microscopic description:
Sections show fragmented hypocellular hyaline cartilage admixed with bony fragments. The tumor cells show small nuclei with occasional binucleation. Cytological atypia and mitoses are absent. Equivocal entrapment is seen, and encasement is focally noted.
The distinction between benign chondrogenic tumor and atypical cartilaginous tumor is difficult based on histology and also due to fragmentation of specimens. Clinico-radiological correlation is essential for the diagnosis.

title: Langerhans cell histiocytosis
content:
Langerhans cell histiocytosis
Portions of the excisional biopsy show fragments of native bone. The lesion consists of sheets of loosely cohesive cells with hypochromatic nuclei with irregular contours, distinctive nuclear grooves, and occasional “kidney bean” clefted morphology. The cytoplasm is pale. Mitotic figures are readily apparent. The background is notable for a prominent mixed inflammatory infiltrate with an abundance of eosinophils. Immunostains show the lesional cells to be positive for CD1a and S100

title: Abscess
content:
Section shows mammary tissue cores exhibiting a heavy infiltrate of acute and chronic
inflammatory cells, and scattered histiocytes and multinucleated giant cells. The GMS and PAS stains are negative for fungal organism.

Immunohistochemically, no invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by AE1/AE3.

title: Adenosis and stromal fibrosis
content:
Section shows florid proliferation of mammary glands with preserved myoepithelial cells that are demonstrated by p63 and SMMHC immunostainings. The stroma surrounding
the proliferative glands is fibrotic and shows spindled fibroblasts. Focal aggregate of foamy histiocytes along with lymphocytic infiltrate is also seen.

No evidence of malignancy is observed in these materials. Clinico-radiological correlations are recommended.

title: ADH [ADF] [F]
content:
Atypical ductal hyperplasia.

Microscopic description:
There is focal population of monotonous cells arranged in solid and cribriform patterns (_ mm in maximum dimension), suggestive of atypical ductal hyperplasia.

Microcalcifications are present inside the ADH and in benign mammary acini.

Immunohistochemically, the proliferative ductal epithelial cells show focal loss of CK5/6. P63 highlights the surrounding myoepithelial cells.

title: ALH
content:
Atypical lobular hyperplasia.

Microscopic description:
Sections show expended acini featuring proliferation of discohesive cells with intracytoplasmic vacuoles, involving less than half of the acini of a lobular unit.

Atypical lobular hyperplasia.
There is solid, discohesive proliferation of cells that are monomorphic, small, with pale pink cytoplasm, uniform oval nuclei, and indistinct nucleoli, but it does not distend the acini
An immunohistochemical stain for E-cadherin is negative in the atypical focus, supporting the diagnosis of ALH.

title: Benign mammary tissue, core biopsy
content:
各位主任、醫師：

今天乳癌團隊會議討論決議，core biopsy 結果僅是normal mammary tissue時，病理報告能加註警示，以免臨床醫師誤以為是benign lesion，而錯失進一步處理。

雖然於2015年1月已有發通知給大家，但科內仍有不一致的做法，在此重發通知，並統一電腦系統中的格式。若報告用手寫的同仁，也請用一樣的診斷字句(Normal mammary tissue)，及加註警語 (Note: The specimen may be not representative for the lesion. Re-biopsy is recommended.)。
謝謝！

1.病人通常是在有懷疑病變的情況下做core biopsy，例如palpable mass 或影像下看到病變。所以當我們在core biopsy的切片看到”良性組織”，應該分出是”normal mammary tissue” 或”benign lesion (e.g. Sclerosing adenosis, DH…)”。
2.如果是”normal mammary tissue”，core biopsy很可能沒扎到可疑的病變。臨床或影像懷疑時應做進一步處理。若我們在這樣的情況下，診斷打”benign mammary tissue”，臨床醫師容易會誤以為是benign lesion，而錯失進一步處理。
3.請統一使用電腦報告格式代碼BRE33 (Normal mammary tissue)

Diagnosis:
Normal mammary tissue, right/left breast (,), core biopsy.
Note: The specimen may not be representative of the lesion. Re-biopsy is recommended.
Microscopic Description:
The core biopsy samples contain normal breast ducts and lobules, along with fibrous and adipose stroma. No pathological changes, including microcalcifications or signs of malignancy, are present in the specimens. Based on imaging findings, the specimen may not be representative of the lesion. Re-biopsy is recommended.

李文瑛醫師

title: Benign mammary tissue.
content:
Benign mammary tissue.

Microscopic description:
The section reveals benign mammary acini and ductules within a fibrotic stroma, accompanied by focal cystic dilatation. Myoepithelial cells are highlighted by p63 immunostain. No microcalcifications or signs of malignancy are present.
Re-biopsy is advised if there is strong clinical suspicion of malignancy.

title: Benign phyllodes tumor
content:
Benign phyllodes tumor

Microscopic description:
Sections show a well-circumscribed tumor revealing leafy architecture with epithelium-lined clefts. The stromal elements are mildly cellular with low mitotic activity (<5 per 10 HPF). No evidence of malignancy is observed.

Benign phyllodes tumor

Microscopic description:
Sections show a lobulated tumor composed of clefts lined by benign ductal epithelia and myxoid cellular stroma. The stromal cells do not exhibit pleomorphism and mitoses are rare. Benign phyllodes tumor is considered. The section margin is free of tumor but very closed. Follow-up for local recurrence is recommended.
    
Benign phyllodes tumor
Microscopic description:
Sections show a well-circumscribed tumor revealing leafy architecture with epithelium-lined clefts. The stromal elements are mildly cellular with peri-epithelial accentuation. The stromal nuclei are monomorphic with low mitotic activity (1 per 10 HPF). No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: Cystic neutrophilic granulomatous mastitis with abscess formation. See Note.
content:
Cystic neutrophilic granulomatous mastitis with abscess formation. See Note.
Granulomatous inflammation witn CNGM-pattern. See Note.

Microscopic description:
Sections show non-caseating granuloma, accompanied by formation of round to oval cystic spaces rimmed by neutrophils (microabscesses).
No microorganisms are identified on special stains (PAS, AFS). Lactational change is seen in the surrounding mammary parenchyma. No malignancy is observed.

Note: The CNGM (cystic neutrophilic granulomatous mastitis)-pattern is possible associated with gram positive bacilli such as Corynebacterium species.

title: DCIS_檢體不夠評估大小
content: Sections A and B show mainly non-comedo type ducatal carcinoma in situ with one and two small foci (all mearsuring around 1 mm in diameter) of invasive lesion, respectively. One of the small invasive lesion in Section B is at the surgical margin with cauterization artifact, making the determination of tumor size impossible. In addition, there might be residual tumor left behind. The pathologic tumor stage is at least at T1a. Final tumor staging will be determined by total excisional specimen.

title: desmoid fibromatosis.
content:
desmoid fibromatosis.

Microscopic description:
Sections show spindle cells in long sweeping fascicles or
loose storiform pattern infiltrating mammary lobuldes, duct
and adipose tissue. These cells are uniform with small
nuclei and absence of nuclear pleomorphism or mitotic
figures. Infiltrate of lymphoplasmacytic cells is focally
seen. Small to medium-sized stromal blood vessels with
perivascular edema is discerned. Usual ductal hyperplasia is
also noted.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for SMA
but not p63, AE1/AE3, ALK or 34betaE12. Weakly
staining for CD34 is focally seen. Nucelar beta-catenin
expression is noted.
Taken together, this is a desmoid fibromatosis.
The surgical margin is involved.

Note: Desmoid fibromatosis is locally aggressive but non-
metastasizing neoplasm with propensity for local recurrence.

title: FEA, flat epithelial atypia
content:
Flat epithelial atypia.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a picture of flat epithelial atypic, which is characterized by variably dilated acini lined by several layers of columnar epithelial cells with monomorphic nuclei. 

Radiological-clinical correlation is recommended for determining further management.

title: Fibroadenoma [FA2] pericanalicular
content:
Fibroadenoma.

Microscopic description:
Sections show breast tissue with a well-defined nodule composed of
admixture of stromal and epithelial proliferation, which features stromal growth around patent ducst.

title: Fibroadenoma [FA]
content:
Fibroadenoma.

Microscopic description:
Sections show breast tissue with a well-defined nodule composed of admixture of stromal and epithelial proliferation, which features compressed glandular spaces in a fibrohyalinized stroma.

title: Fibroadenomatous changes (fibroadenomatoid hyperplasia)
content:
Fibroadenomatous changes (fibroadenomatoid hyperplasia)

Microscopic description: 
Section shows changes similar to those seen in fibroadenomas but lacks the sharp circumscription.

title: Fibrocystic change with microcalcifications [FCC]
content:
Fibrocystic change with microcalcifications

Microscopic description:
Sections show round to ovoid cystically dilated spaces lined by epithelial and myoepithelial cells in a sclerotic stroma. Microcalcifications are present in the lumens. Adenosis is also seen. No evidence of malignancy is observed.
            

usual ductal hyperplasia
There is focal proliferation of epithelial cells being positive for CK5/6 with heterogeneous staining pattern, consistent with usual ductal hyperplasia.
No malignancy is o    bserved.

title: Fibroepithelial lesion
content:
Fibroepithelial lesion

Microscopic description:
Sections show myxofibrous stroma admixed with compressed glandular structures with focal usual ductal hyperplasia. Immunohistochemically, the myoepithelial cells are highlighted by SMMHC. Staining for CK5/6 is heterogeneously positive in the proliferative epithelial cells.

title: Fibroepithelial lesion [no IHC]
content:
Fibroepithelial lesion.

Microscopic description:
Section shows fibrous stroma with low cellularity, admixed with compressed glandular structures.

The above-mentioned features can be consistent with a fibroadenoma or a benign phyllodes tumor.

title: fibroepithelial lesion, suspicious for phyllodes tumor. see NOTE.
content:
fibroepithelial lesion, suspicious for phyllodes tumor. see NOTE.

Microscopic description:
Section shows proliferation of ductal epithelial cells in focal papillary pattern in a fibromyxoid stroma. The stroma shows focally increased cellularity with mild to moderate nuclear atypia and increased mitotic figures.
Immunohistochemically, the ductal epithelial cells are heterogeneously positive for ER and CK5/6. P63 and SMMHC highlight the myoepithelial cells.

NOTE: The overall features are highly suspicious for phyllodes tumor. The categories cannot be established in this biopsy specimen, as the tumor is not fully sampled.
Wide excision of the tumor is recommended.

title: Granulomatous mastitis
content:
Granulomatous mastitis

Microscopic description:
Sections shows mammary tissue exhibiting a heavy infiltrate
of inflammatory cells within fibroadipose tissue and around
mammary lobules. The infiltrate is composed of acute and
chronic inflammatory cells, histiocytes, accompanied by
granulation tissue formation. Focal granuloma formation
composed of epithelioid histiocytes and multinucleated giant
cells are also seen. A subset of the granulomas show cystic
space or microabscess formation.

The PAS stain is negative for fungal organisms. No
mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast
stain. No invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by CK(AE1/AE3).

title: IDC, biopsy (D.D lobular)
content:
Section shows breast tissue infiltrated by neoplastic cells arranged in nests and focal Indian filing patterns. The tumor cells show plemorphic nuclei with occasional nucleoli.
Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells express E-cadherin and P120 (membranous pattern), consistent with the diagnosis.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDY: 
1. ER (Ab): Positive /Low Positive (1-10% of cells with nuclear positivity)/ Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
2. PR(Ab): positive /Negative ( %).
3. HER-2/neu (Ab): Positive (3+) /Equivocal (2+) /Negative (1+/0
4. Ki-67

title: IDC, biopsy (glandular differentiation)
content:
Invasive carcinoma of no special type (ductal).

Microscopic description:
Section shows breast tissue cores infiltrated by nests of neoplastic cells with tubuloglandular differentiation and focal cribriform architecture. The tumor cells show slightly enlarged nuclei with occasional prominent nucleoli.

Histologic type: Invasive carcinoma of no special type (ductal).
Histologic grade (Nottingham histologic score):
  Grade I (score 3-5) / Grade II (score 6,7) / Grade III (score 8,9)
- Tubule formation: score 1/ 2/ 3
Majority of tumor greater than 75% (score = 1)
Moderate 10% to 75% (score = 2)
Minimal less than 10% (score = 3)   
- Nuclear pleomorphism: score 1/ 2/ 3
Small regular nuclei (score = 1)
Moderate increase in size, etc (score = 2)
Marked variation in size, nucleoli, chromatin clumping, etc (score = 3)
- Mitotic count: score 1/ 2/ 3
0- 3 mitoses per mm2 (score = 1)
4-7 mitoses per mm2 (score = 2)
>= 8 mitoses per mm2 (score = 3)

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDY:
1. ER (Ab): Positive /Low Positive (1-10% of cells with nuclear positivity)/ Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
2. PR (Ab): Positive /Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
3. Her-2/neu (Ab): Positive (3+) /Equivocal (2+) /Negative (1+/0)
4. Ki-67
5. P63 immunostaining demonstrates the absence of myoepithelial cells at the periphery of invasive focus.

Note: This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.

title: IDC, biopsy (有一點點gland)
content:
Invasive carcinoma of no special type (ductal).

Microscopic description:
Section shows breast tissue strips infiltrated by neoplastic cells arranged in nests, cords, isolated cells and focal glandular patterns. The tumor cells show slightly enlarged nuclei with occasional prominent nucleoli.

- Nuclear pleomorphism: score 1/ 2/ 3
- Mitotic count: score 1/ 2/ 3

4-7 mitoses per mm2 (score = 2)
>= 8 mitoses per mm2 (score = 3)

title: IDC, biopsy NEW
content:
Invasive carcinoma of no special type (ductal).

Microscopic description:
Histologic type: Invasive carcinoma of no special type (ductal).
Histologic grade (Nottingham histologic score):
  Grade I (score 3-5) / Grade II (score 6,7) / Grade III (score 8,9)

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

HER2 Result Criteria Negative (Score 0) No staining observed or Membrane stating that is incomplete and is faint/barely perceptible and within ≤10% of tumor cells 

Negative (Score 1+) Incomplete membrane staining that is faint/barely perceptible and within >10% of tumor cells* 

Equivocal (Score 2+)† Weak to moderate complete membrane staining in >10% of tumor cells or Circumferential membrane staining that is intense but within ≤10% of tumor cells* 

Positive (Score 3+) Circumferential membrane staining that is complete and >10% of tumor cells*

Indeterminate:
Technical issues prevent the test from being reported as positive, negative, or equivocal.
Conditions may include:
Inadequate specimen handling
Artifacts (crush or edge artifacts) that make interpretation difficult
Analytic testing failure
Another specimen should be requested for testing to determine HER2 status.
Reason for indeterminate testing should be noted in a comment in the report.

title: ILC
content:
Invasive lobular carcinoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows breast tissue infiltrated by neoplastic cells arranged in cords with Indian filing pattern. The tumor cells show mildly enlarged nuclei.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells show loss of E-cadherin, consistent with the diagnosis.
Histologic type: Invasive lobular carcinoma
Histologic grade (Nottingham histologic score):
Grade II (score 6,7)
- Tubule formation: score 3
- Nuclear pleomorphism: score 2
- Mitotic count: score 1

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDY:
1. ER (Ab): Positive (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
2. PR (Ab): Positive (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
3. Her-2/neu (Ab): Positive (3+) /Equivocal (2+) /Negative (1+/0)

title: Intraductal papillary with pseudoinvasion
content:
Section shows papillary proliferation of ductal epithelial cells within dilated duct with preserved myoepithelial cells. There is entrapment of epithelial structures by fibrohyaline stroma, resulting in a pseudo invasive appearance. 

Immunohistochemically, the ductal epithelial cells are heterogeneously positive for CK5/6. P63 highlights the myoepithelial cells.

No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: Intraductal papilloma
content:
Intraductal papilloma
Microscopic description:
Section shows papillary proliferation of ductal epithelial cells within dilated duct with preserved myoepithelial cells. 

Immunohistochemically, the ductal epithelial cells are heterogeneously positive for CK5/6. P63 highlights the myoepithelial cells.

No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: Intraductal papilloma with atypical ductal hyperplasia (ADH)
content:
Intraductal papilloma with atypical ductal hyperplasia.

Microscopic description:
Section shows papillary proliferation of ductal epithelial cells within dilated duct with preserved surrounding myoepithelial cells. There is focal population of monotonous cells arranged in solid and cribriform patterns (_ mm in maximum dimension), suggestive of atypical ductal hyperplasia. 

Microcalcification is present inside the ADH and in benign mammary acini.

Immunohistochemically, the proliferative ductal epithelial cells shows focal loss of CK5/6. P63 highlights the surrounding myoepithelial cells.

The surgical margin is involved.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDY (performed on section B): SMMHC, p63, CK5/6.

title: Invasive carcinoma with apocrine differentiation
content:
Invasive carcinoma with apocrine differentiation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows an infiltrating tumor comprised of large
epithelial cells with abundant eosinophilic and granular
cytoplasm, well defined cell borders, enlarged nuclei and
prominent nucleoli. The tumor cells are strongly positive
for AR, and negative for ER and PR, suggestive of apocrine
differentiation. A definite subtype can only be made on
the subsequent larger specimen.

Microscopic description:
Histologic type: invasive carcinoma with apocrine
differentiation
Histologic grade (Nottingham histologic score):
Grade II (score 6,7)
Tubule formation: score 3
Nuclear pleomorphism: score 2
Mitotic count: score 2
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDY:
ER (Ab): Negative (0%)
PR (Ab): Negative (0%)
Her-2/neu (Ab): Equivocal (2+)
Ki-67: 40%
P63 immunostaining demonstrates the absence of myoepithelial cells at the periphery of invasive focus.
AR: Positive.
(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

Invasive carcinoma with apocrine differentiation

title: Invasive ductal carcinoma with mucin production.
content:
Invasive ductal carcinoma with mucin production.

Microscopic description:
Section shows nests of tumor cells invading the stroma and focally floating within lakes of extracellular mucin. The tumor cells exhibit marked cytologic atypia with eosinophilic cytoplasm.

Histologic grade (Nottingham histologic score):
Grade 
- Tubule formation: score

title: LCIS [F] Atypia
content:
lobular carcinoma in situ.

Microscopic description:
Sections show expanded acini featuring proliferation of discohesive cells with intracytoplasmic vacuoles (enlarged and mildly pleomorphic nuclei). Pagetoid spread of the terminal ducts is also seen. 

NOTE: LCIS is a benign condition and is not treated as a carcinoma. It is properly considered a proliferative disease with associated risk for developing a breast cancer in the future and, therefore, is no longer included in the AJCC 8th ed. cancer staging system.

title: mammary hamartoma
content:
mammary hamartoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows a circumscribed nodule composed of breast
ducts, lobules, fibrous stroma, and adipose tissue.
Microcalcifications are seen within the mammary acini.

title: Mastitis with abscess formation
content:
Mastitis with abscess formation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows mammary tissue cores exhibiting a heavy infiltrate in fibroadipose tissue and in and around mammary lobules. The infiltrate is composed of acute and chronic inflammatory cells, and scattered histiocytes and some multinucleated giant cells.

The GMS and PAS stains are negative for fungal organisms. Immunohistochemically, no invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by CK(AE1/AE3).

title: MCL with ADH
content:
Mucocele-like lesion with atypical ductal hyperplasia.

Microscopic description:
Sections show round to ovoid cystically dilated spaces lined
by epithelial and myoepithelial cells in a sclerotic stroma.
The dilated ducts are focally filled by mucin with
extravasated mucin pools into surrounding stroma.
There is focal population of monotonous cells with Romen
bridge-like architecture (<2 mm in maximum dimension),
suggestive of atypical ductal hyperplasia.
Microcalcifications are present inside the ADH and in benign
mammary acini.

Immunohistochemically, the proliferative ductal epithelial
cells show loss of CK5/6.

The margin is

title: metastatic carcinoma, consistent with mammary origin
content:
Metastatic carcinoma, consistent with mammary origin

Microscopic description:
Section shows pulmonary tissue infiltrated by nests of neoplastic cells featuring vesicular nuclei with indistinct nucleoli. 
Immunohistochemically, these tumor cells are positive for GATA3 but not TTF1.

The overall findings, taken together with clinical information, are compatible with metastatic invasive ductal carcinoma of mammary origin. 
 
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDY:
1. ER (Ab): Positive Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
2. PR (Ab): Positive Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
3. Her-2/neu (Ab): Positive (3+) /Equivocal (2+) /Negative (1+/0)

title: metastatic IDC
content:
Metastatic carcinoma, consistent with mammary origin.

Microscopic description:
Sections show blood clots admixed with few cluster of tumor cells being positive for AE1/AE3 and GATA3.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDY:
ER (Ab): Positive /Low Positive (1-10% of cells with nuclear positivity)/ Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
PR (Ab): Positive /Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
Her-2/neu (Ab): Positive (3+) /Equivocal (2+) /Negative (1+/0)

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: mucin-producing carcinoma.
content:
Mucin-producing carcinoma. 

Microscopic description:
Section shows islands of tumor cells floating within lakes of extracellular mucin. The tumor cells exhibit mild cytologic atypia with relatively uniform nuclei and small amounts of eosinophilic cytoplasm.

The overall features suggest a mucin-producing carcinoma, which can be either pure mucinous or mixed ductal-mucinous carcinoma. A definite diagnosis can only be made on the subsequent larger specimen.

Histologic grade (Nottingham histologic score):
Grade I (score 3-5)
- Tubule formation: score 2   
- Nuclear pleomorphism: score 1
- Mitotic count: score 1

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDY:
1. ER (Ab): Positive (_%, strong)
2. PR (Ab): Positive (_%, strong)
3. Her-2/neu (Ab): _
4. Ki-67: _%.

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

title: Mucocele-like lesion [MCL]
content:
Mucocele-like lesion.

Sections show round to ovoid cystically dilated spaces lined by epithelial and myoepithelial cells in a sclerotic stroma. The dilated ducts are focally filled by mucin with extravasated mucin pools into surrounding stroma.

title: Mucocele-like lesion with focal atypical ductal hyperplasia
content:
Mucocele-like lesion with focal atypical ductal hyperplasia

Microscopic description:
Section shows round to ovoid cystically dilated spaces lined by epithelial and myoepithelial cells in a sclerotic stroma. The dilated ducts are focally filled by mucin with extravasated mucin pools into surrounding stroma. Microcalcifications are seen. There is focal proliferation of monotonous cells with bridging and second lumen formation, and with loss of CK5/6 in these proliferative cells, suggestive of atypical ductal hyperplasia.

P63 and SMMHC highlight the preserved myoepithelial cells.

Taken together, the specimen shows a mucocele-like lesion with focal atypical ductal hyperplasia. Total excision of the mass is suggested because a more significant lesion cannot be comfortably excluded based on core biopsy sampling alone.

title: Normal mammary tissue BRE33
content:
Normal mammary tissue. See Note.

Note: This sample may not be representative of the lesion.  Re-biopsy is recommended.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
The core tissues include normal breast ducts and lobules, as well as fibrous and adipose stroma. No pathological changes, including microcalcifications or malignancy, are observed in these samples. Based on imaging findings, this sample may not be representative of the lesion.  Re-biopsy is recommended.

Immunohistochemically, the myoepithelial cells are highlighted by p63. No invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by AE1/AE3.

title: Paget disease
content:
Paget disease.

Microscopic description:
Section shows single cells or clusters of cells spread throughout the epidermis with epidermal ulceration.
The tumor cells have abundant pale cytoplasm, irregular large nucleus with prominent nucleoli.
Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for CK7 and EMA, and negative for p40, confirming the diagnosis.

1. ER (Ab): negative (0%).
2. PR (Ab): negative (0%).
3. Her-2/neu (Ab): positive (3+).

title: Papillary lesion
content:
papillary lesion.

Microscopic description:
Section shows florid proliferation of ductal epithelial cells with preserved myoepithelial cells in focal papillary pattern.

Immunohistochemically, the ductal epithelial cells are heterogeneously positive for CK5/6. P63 highlights the myoepithelial cells. Total excision is recommended.

title: Papillary lesion (DD IPC, EPC)
content:
Section shows breast tissue with proliferative ductal cells with focal papillary pattern. The ductal cells show columnar shaped with enlarged and rounded nuclei and increased mitotic activity. Extracellular mucin is also seen.

Immunohistochemically, the proliferative ductal cells show loss of CK5/6 expression. P63 and SMMHC demonstrate the absence of myoepithelial cells in the fibrovascular cores.

Taken together, this is a papillary lesion with differential diagnoses of intraductal papillary carcinoma or encapsulated papillary carcinoma. Total excision of the lesion is recommended for a definite diagnosis.

title: Papillary lesion with atypia
content:
Section shows florid proliferation of ductal epithelial cells with preserved surrounding myoepithelial cells in focal papillary pattern. Focal atypical ductal proliferation with monomorphic appearance is seen. 

Immunohistochemically, the ductal epithelial cells show focal loss of CK5/6. P63 highlights the myoepithelial cells at the periphery as well as within the fibrovascular cores, but lost in the atypical foci. Total excision is recommended.

title: Papillary lesion, needle biopsy (Attenuation of p63)
content:
The breast tissue strips are fragmented and reveal fibrotic areas and a florid 
proliferation of ductal epithelial cells that are immunoreactive to CK5/6 and form 
compact-arranged ductules or solid sheets, surrounded by myoepithelial cells as 

highlighted by p63 immunostain. Attenuation of intermingling myoepithelial cells is 
demonstrated by p63 stain. Total excision of the lesion may be needed for an accurate 
diagnosis.

content:

title: Perilobular hemangioma
content: A perilobular hemangioma is seen, which is characterized by aggregate of capillary-sized blood vessels in association with a lobule. The endothelial cells are highlighted by CD31 but not S100 confirming the diagnosis.

title: Phyllodes tumor, borderline.
content:
Phyllodes tumor, borderline.

Microscopic description:
Histologic Type: Phyllodes tumor, borderline
Tumor Size: 
Stromal Cellularity: Moderate
Stromal Atypia: Mild to moderate
Stromal Overgrowth: Absent
Mitotic Rate: 9 mitoses per 10 HPFs
Histologic Tumor Border: Focally permeative
Malignant Heterologous Elements: Not identified
Margin: Margin involved by phyllodes tumor
Regional Lymph Node Status: Not applicable.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: Pseudoangiomatous stomal hyperplasia [PASH]
content:
Pseudoangiomatous stomal hyperplasia.

Microscopic description:
Section shows proliferative stromal cells forming slit-like clefts, surrounding benign mammary ducts are seen.
Immunohistochemically, these spindle cells are positive for CD34 but not CD31, AE1/AE3 or p63.

NOTE: PASH can be associated with other benign or malignant lesions, or as the stromal component of fibroepithelial lesions.

title: Sclerosing adenosis (with IHC)
content:
Sclerosing adenosis.

Microscopic description:
Sections show (a demarcated tumor composed of) angular or dilated tubules in a sclerotic stroma, (accompanied by focal proliferation of epithelial cells with second lumen formation). Microcalcifications are also seen.

Immunohistochemically, the proliferated epithelial cells focally express CK5/6, suggesting usual ductal hyperplasia. SMMHC demonstrates the surrounding myoepithelial cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: sclerosing lymphocytic mastitis (please see NOTE).
content:
sclerosing lymphocytic mastitis (please see NOTE).

Microscopic description:
The breast biopsy shows stromal fibrosis, periductal and
perivascular lymphocytic infiltrates, and epithelioid
myofibroblasts in the stroma.

P63 highlights the preserved myoepithelial cells. No
invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by AE1/AE3. CD3 and CD20
demonstrate the distribution of T and B lymphocytes.

NOTE: sclerosing lymphocytic mastitis is often associated
with diabetes (diabetic mastopathy). Clinical correlation is
recommanded.

title: Siliconoma
content:
Siliconoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows benign mammary tissue and a lesion showing variable-sized spaces
surrounded by histiocytes and multinucleated giant cells.

title: SIZE/Prior biopsy or excision
content: NOTE: If there has been a prior core needle biopsy or incisional biopsy showing a larger area of invasion than in the excisional specimen, the largest dimension of the invasive carcinoma in the prior specimen should be used for T classification. Please correlate with previous pathological report.

title: solid papillary carcinoma associated with microinvasive carcinoma (pT1mi) [microinvasion]
content:
solid papillary carcinoma associated with microinvasive carcinoma (pT1mi).

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

  microinvasive carcinoma

Extent of tumor: 
  Skin involvement: absent
Chest wall invasion deeper than pectoralis muscle: not applicable
Nipple: not assessed
Ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS): solid papillary carcinoma

  Deep:  cm;

title: Spindle cell lesion
content:
Microscopic description:
Section shows mammary tissue cores composed of mammary ducts featuring usual ductal hyperplasia in a background of bland-looking spindle cells.

Immunohistochemically, myoepithelial cells surrounding the ducts are highlighted by p63. The spindle cells are negative for 34betaE12 and P63. The spindle cells are stained blue by Masson trichrome stain.

The differential diagnoses include fibroepithelial lesion or other mesenchymal tumors,  of either benign or low malignant potential. Total excision of the lesion is recommended for a definite diagnosis.

title: Spindle cell neoplasm Bx (Borderline or Malignant phyllodes)
content:
Spindle cell neoplasm. See description.

Microscopic description:
Section shows breast tissue with a lesion composed of
spindle cells bearing moderate cellular atypia with
increased cellularity. Occasional mitosis is seen. Focally
dilated ductal epithelium is seen.

Immunohistochemically, the spindle cells are diffusely
positive for bcl-2 and SMA but not AE1/AE3 and p63. CD34 is
focally positive.

The categorization of this spindle cell lesion is difficult
in this small biopsied smample. Based on histology and
immunoprofile, the differential diagnosis includes phyllodes
tumor of either borderline or malignant. Metaplastic
carcinoma is less likely due to complete absence of AE1/AE3
and p63 stainings. A soft tissue neoplasm is less likely but
cannot be completely excluded.

title: Suggestive of fibroepithelial lesion
content:
Benign mammary tissue (please see NOTE).

Microscopic description:
The tissue cores are composed predominantly of normal breast tissue with fibrocystic change. There are tissue fragments showing mild proliferation of mammary glands set in a fibrous stroma. 

By immunohistochemistry, p63 immunostain demonstrates the preserved myoepithelial cells. 

No malignancy is seen in this specimen.

NOTE: Based on the imaging finding, this specimen could either be suggestive of a fibroepithelial lesion or be not representative for the lesion. 
Re-biopsy is suggested if malignancy is suspected clinically.

title: Tubular adenoma
content:
Tubular adenoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows small round tubular structures lined by regular epithelial and myoepithelial cells surrounded by scant loosely cellular stroma.

title: Tubular carcinoma.
content:
More than 90% of the tumors are composed of tubules and
glands with open lumina lined by a single layer or
neoplastic epithelium, which is consistent with the
diagnosis of tubular carcinoma.

title: UDH
content:
usual ductal hyperplasia.

Sections show breast tissue revealing proliferation of epithelial cells lined by myoepithelial cells with microcalcifications in the lumen. Fibrosis is seen within the stroma.
Immunohistochemically, the myoepithelial cells are highlighted by SMMHC and p63. Staining for CK5/6 is heterogeneously positive in the proliferative epithelial cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: 因應評鑑需求 需修改之部位與原申請單內容不同需填寫紙本申請書
content:

title: 莊茂德margin
content:
However, because the specimen is not oriented, this involved margin may represent margin transected from partial mastectomy specimen. The exact margin status
cannot be evaluated. Clinical correlation is recommended.
      (This case was discussed with Dr Chuang on 2022/04/25 10 am)

title: 請確定microcalcifications有沒有切乾淨
content:
Only few microcalcifications in the mammary acini are present. Clinico-radiological correlation to determine whether all calcifications have been removed is suggested.
No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: Appendix
content:
Acellular mucin in LN or deposit → pN0

Presence of acellular mucin is not considered a positive margin in the context of LAMN or HAMN but should be recorded in a comment or note.

Acellular mucin on the serosal surface without a stromal reaction is likely to be the result of contamination of the specimen by handling during dissection, while acellular mucin on the serosal surface with mesothelial reaction, stromal fibrosis and/or neovascularization represents involvement of the serosa by LAMN/HAMN and is relevant for staging

v. 2022 Dec

title: Colorectum
content:
Acellular pools of mucin in specimens following neoadjuvant therapy are considered to represent completely eradicated tumor and are not used to assign pT stage or counted as positive lymph nodes.

v. 2022 June

title: AO
content:
Sections show sheets of round to oval cells with sharply delineated cell border and  
clear perinuclear halo, being positive for GFAP. Increased Ki-67 labeling index and  
mitotic activity are note (>6/10 HPFs).

title: CAA
content:
cerebral amyloid angiopathy with intracerebral hemorrhage.

Microscopic description:
Section shows blood clots and brain tissue in which
thickened vascular wall of leptomeningeal and superficial
cortical blood vessels is seen.
Congo red staining demonstrates apple-green birefringence
within vascular wall under polarized light.

title: Cavernoma
content:
Section shows tightly juxtaposed abnormal vascular channels with hyalinized wall and containing thrombi. Dystrophic calcifications and hemosiderin deposition are also
seen. The surrounding brain parenchyma shows gliosis. No arteries are demonstrated by elastic stain.

Immunohistochemically, little intervening brain parenchyma is demonstrated by GFAP.

The overall features are consistent with cavernous malformation (cavernoma). No malignancy is observed.

title: chordoma
content:
chordoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows lobular architecture composed of tumor cells arranged in cords or ribbons in a myxoid matrix. Tumour cells are large with clear to eosinophilic cytoplasm characterized by vacuolated or bubbly cytoplasm.
Immunoohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for AE1/AE3 ____

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: GBM, Glioblastoma, WHO grade IV,
content:
Section shows tumor cells with focal marked cellular anaplasia and increased mitotic activity, along with occasional gemistocytes and multinucleated giant tumor cells. Prominent necrosis and microvascular proliferation are noted. The background is myxoid.
Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are diffusely positive for GFAP and p53. The proliferation index demonstrated by Ki-67 is focally increased up to _.
Section shows tumor cells with increased cellularity and marked cellular atypia in a fibbrillary background along with prominent pseudopalisading necrosis and microvascular proliferation

Sections show tumor cells with increased cellularity, cellular atypia and increased
mitotic activity in a fibrillary background. Necrosis with focal pseudopalisading and
microvascular proliferation are seen. There's focal aggregate of neutrophils.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are diffusely positive for GFAP. The
proliferation index demonstrated by Ki-67 is focally increased up to 25%.

Taken together, this is a glioblastoma.

Results of biomarker testing:

MGMT (clone MT3.1) immunohistochemistry: negative

IDH1 R132H immunohistochemistry: positive (IDH1 R132H mutation)

ATRX immunohistochemistry: loss of expression (ATRX mutation)

Ki-67 (MIB-1) labeling index: 50

title: Hemangioblastoma
content:
Sections show fragmented tissue containing cerebellum and tumorous tissue revealing rich capillary networks with intervening stromal cells. The stromal cells show clear, foamy cytoplasm containing intracytoplasmic vacuoles. Hyalin globules are focally seen.

Immunohistochemically, the stromal cells are positive for S-100 and but not GFAP or Pax8.

ICD code 1

title: Intracerebral hemorrhage [ICH]
content:
intracerebral hemorrhage.

Microscopic description:
Sections show predominantly blood clots admixed with a few limited fragments of brain tissue and focal aggregates of foamy histiocytes, some lymphocytes, neutrophils and eosinophils. No vascular malformations are noted. There are some neutrophils
infiltrating the vascular wall without fibrinoid necrosis. No evidence of amyloid deposition is demonstrated by Congo red stain.

title: Meningiom (atypical)
content:
Sections show oval to spindle-shaped meningothelial cells arranged in whorled 
patterns. Focal area shows hypercellularity 

with increased mitotic activity (5/10HPF). Necrosis and brain invasion are not discerned.

Immunohistochemically, the proliferation index demonstrated by Ki-67 is around 10%
 in the hypercellular area. 

Taken together, this is an atypical meningioma (WHO grage II).

title: Meningioma (chordoid features)
content: Some areas show cords or trabeculae of eosinophilic cells in an abundant mucoid matrix background, resembling chordoma and accounting for about _% of whole tumor.

title: Meningioma (meningothelial)
content:
Meningioma (meningothelial)

Microscopic description:
Section shows oval to spindle-shaped meningothelial cells arranged in fascicles and focal whorls. Neither necrosis or increased mitotic activity are noted.

title: Meningioma (secretory)
content: Eosinophilic secretion in the intracellular lumina is seen focally (secretory type).

title: Meningioma (transitional type with focal chordoid feature and metaplasia)
content:
Sections show a meningioma with a mixed pattern combining those of meningothelial as well as fibrous meningioma. Whorl formation is noted in places. Chordoid feature and metaplastic adipose tissue formation are also observed in focal areas. There is no atypical change, e.g. loss of architecture, increased in mitotic activity, or necrosis, identified in the specimen examination. The MIB-1 labeling index (LI) is 4.

Comment:
Since the tumor shows no atypical features and the MIB-1 LI is low, it should be diagnosed as a grade I meninigioma.

title: Meningioma (transitional)
content:
Sections show bland-looking tumor cells with oval to spindle nuclei arranged in fascicles and forming whorls.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for EMA but not GFAP. No brain invasion is demonstrated by GFAP. The proliferation index as determined by Ki-67 is low.

title: Meningioma (WHO grade 1)
content:
Meningioma (WHO grade 1)
Microscopic description:
The sections show meningothelial and microcystic meningioma.
There is no evidence of necrosis, brain invasion, sheet-like
growth or increased cellularity. There are no prominent
nucleoli or small cells with high nuclear to cytoplasmic
ratio. Mitotic figures are not identified. A few brain
tissue is present and highlighted by GFAP. No brain invasion
is noted. The dura is focally invaded by tumor.
By immunohistochemistry, the tumor cells are positive for
SSTR2a, consistent with the diagnosis. The Ki67 is low.

title: NOTE: low-grade glioma/Non-representative for the lesion.
content:
Note: The glioma is low-grade in this specimen, which may be
non-representative. As the image shown, a high-grade glioma
should be considered.

title: Pituitary adenoma
content:
LABORATORY DATA: IGF-1 _ ng/mL. ACTH _pg/mL. HGH _uIU/mL. Cortisal _ ug/dL. Prolactin: _ng/mL.

Sections show sheets of uniform cells interrupted by a delicate capillary network with focal papillary pattern and perivascular pseudoresette. Hemorrhage and neutrophilic infiltrate is also seen.

Reticulin stain reveals loss of acinar pattern

title: Pituitary adenoma with apoplexy
content:
Section shows sheets of uniform cells interrupted by a delicate capillary network. 
Hemorrhage and neutrophilic infiltrate is also seen. Focal hemorrhage and necrosis is 
seen consistent with apoplexy. 

//Fragments of normal pituitary gland tissue are also 
seen. 

Immunohistochemically, the cells are positive for synaptophysin. The proliferation 
index as determined by Ki-67 is low. Reticulin stain reveals loss of acinar pattern.

title: air-drying artifact
content:
Nondiagnostic. Extensive air-drying artifact in this alcohol-fixed
smear makes the cytologic interpretation difficult. A repeat FNA with rapid wet-fixation is suggested if clinically indicated.

No evidence of tumor cell infiltration is confirmed by AE1/AE3 immunostain.
CD3 and CD20 demonstrate the distribution of T and B lymphocytes which shows no evidence of lymphoma.

title: Aspergillus is present, which is mostly due to contamination. Please correlate w
content: Aspergillus is present, which is mostly due to contamination. Please correlate with clinical symptom. And obtain another sample later if clinically indicated.

title: ATYPIA sparsely cellular aspirate/microfollicles
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
ATYPIA OF UNDETERMINED SIGNIFICANCE. See Note.

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid gland, left lobe, aspiration

ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sparsely cellular aspirate of follicular cells shows a predominantly microfollicular architecture, some containing small amounts of colloid.

Note: A follicular neoplasm may be considered; however, it is insufficient to make a follicular neoplasm interpretation due to discrepancy between the cellularity and the cell pattern. Other follicular lesions can have overlapping cytomorphologic features. Please correlate clinically. Repeat aspirate may be helpful if clinically indicated.

title: ATYPICAL [Kerainized cells/SCC vs cyst]
content:
Atypia of undetermined significance. See note.

CYTOLOGY: aspiration,____

ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

Microscopic description:
Abundant keratinized cells are seen admixed with inflammatory cells, granular debris and anucleated and degenerated nucleated squamous cells. Mild atypia is seen.

Note: The differential diagnosis include metastatic SCC and reactive squamous atypia in a benign squamous cyst.
Excisional biopsy is recommended.

title: BF-metastatic IDC
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
MALIGNANT, consistent with metastatic carcinoma of mammary origin.

CYTOLOGY: Pleural effusion, ___

ADEQUACY: satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
It shows some three-dimentional clusters of cells with coarse granular nuclear chromatin and prominent nucleoli. Intracytoplasmic vacuoles are also found.

Cell block:
It shows neoplastic cells being positive for GATA3 but not TTF1, consistent with metastatic carcinoma of mammary origin.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDY:
1. ER (Ab): Positive /Low Positive (1-10% of cells with nuclear positivity)/ Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
2. PR (Ab): Positive /Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
3. Her-2/neu (Ab): Positive (3+) /Equivocal (2+) /Negative (1+/0)

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

title: Fat necrosis
content: Different levels of fat necrosis (Derived in accordance with Jorge et al. [6]). a Primary level of fat necrosis indicates fragments of adipose tissue. b Primary level of fat necrosis indicates individual adipocytes. c Medium level of fat necrosis indicates infiltration by histiocytes. d Medium level of fat necrosis indicates conglomeration of RBCs referred to as “myospherulosis”. e Late stage of fat necrosis indicates single multinucleated giant cell. f Late stage of fat necrosis indicates calcifications (are common in late stage of fat necrosis). g Late stage of fat necrosis indicates macrophages containing hemosiderin. h Late stage of fat necrosis indicates calcifications

title: PAP SMEAR AGC, favor neoplastic
content: NOTE: A significant percentage of patients with this cytologic interpretation have underlying high-grade squamous or glandular intraepithelial abnomalities. Further diagnostic follow-up procedures, such as colposcopy with endocervical sampling, are suggested as clinically indicated.

title: PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
content:
Microscopic description:
Sections show nests and irregular cords of squamoid cells embedded in a collagenous stroma. Cytologic atypia is mild to absent, and mitotic figures are not identified. Immunohistochemical studies demonstrate increased p53 expression. The lesional cells are positive for p40, AE1/AE3, and CK19.

Comment:
The differential diagnosis includes squamous odontogenic tumor, mucoepidermoid carcinoma (no mucin-producing cells are identified by mucicarmine stain), and squamous cell carcinoma. Complete excision is recommended for definitive diagnosis..

title: R-A
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
ATYPICAL
Atypical cells present

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears contain some atypical cells with high N/C ratio and atypical nuclear features. These findings cannot differentiate between reactive change and malignant change.

title: R-Adeno
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
MALIGNANT, most likely adenocarcinoma

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Some ball-like or glandular clusters of atypical epithelial cells with high nuclear to cytoplasmic ratio, coarse granular nuclear chromatin and prominent nucleoli are identified.

title: R-HSV
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY
Consistent with herpes simplex virus infection

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Benign squamous and respiratory epithelial cells with carbon-laden macrophages are
present. No malignant cells are found.
Multinucleated cells with ground glass appearance, nuclear molding and intranuclear
viral inclusions are present, suggesting of herpes simplex virus infection.

title: R-N
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
NEGATIVE FOR MALIGNANCY

CYTOLOGY: SPUTUM

ADEQUACY:
Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Benign squamous and respiratory epithelial cells with carbon-laden macrophages are present. No malignant cells are
found.

title: R-non-small cell
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
MALIGNANT, most likely non-small cell carcinoma

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Some clusters of large neoplastic epithelial cells with high nuclear to cytoplasmic ratio, coarse granular nuclear chromatin and prominent nucleoli are identified.

title: R-SCC
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
MALIGNANT, most likely squamous cell carcinoma

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Some clusters or single cells with nuclear pleomorphism, hyperchromatism, and coarse chromatin are present. Tadpole cells and keratinization are also noted.

title: R-small cell
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
MALIGNANT, most likely small cell carcinoma

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Some clusters of cells with nuclear molding and crush artifact are seen. These cells have scant cytoplasm and hyperchromatic nuclei with finely granular chromatin and inconspicuous nucleoli.

title: R-Sus
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
SUSPICIOUS FOR MALIGNANCY

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Some atypical cells exhibit high N/C ratio and atypical nuclear features but not conclusive for malignancy. Tissue biopsy is recommended for confirmation before undertaking definitive therapy.

title: R-UN
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
UNSATISFACTORY specimen

CYTOLOGY: Sputum

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Only superficial squamous cells are identified. No alveolar macrophages and respiratory epithelial cells are observed. This is an unsatisfactory specimen.

title: SG -basaloid neoplasm
content:
Basaloid neoplasm

1. If the tumor has characteristic features of basal cell adenoma/adenocarcinoma (e.g., a peripheral band of hyaline material): 

NEOPLASTIC CELLS PRESENT. Basal cell neoplasm of salivary gland origin (see Note). note: The cytologic features are compatible with either basal cell adenoma or basal cell adenocarci-noma. Distinction between the two cannot be made on cytologic material. Excision is recommended for precise classification. 

2. If the tumor lacks distinctive cytologic features: 

NEOPLASTIC CELLS PRESENT. Basaloid neoplasm (see Note). note: The differential diagnosis is broad in scope and includes basal cell adenoma, basal cell adenocarcinoma, the solid variant of adenoid cystic carcinoma, and nonsalivary gland neoplasms. Excision is recommended for precise classification, including intraoperative frozen section examination if clinically indicated

title: SG-AUS
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Atypia of undetermined significance

Microscopic description:
It shows a few groups of atypical epithelial cells revealing
increased N:C ratio. However, air-drying artifact in this
alcohol-fixed smear makes the cytologic interpretation
difficult. The overall findings are suggestive but not
diagnostic of a neoplasm.

title: SG-AUS (reactive / inflammation)
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Atypia of undetermined significance

Microscopic description:
It shows rare atypical cells with reactive and reparative atypia in an inflammatory background which is indefiite for a neoplasm.

title: SG-AUS-basaloid cells
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
ATYPIA OF UNDETERMINED SIGNIFICANCE
Few clusters of basaloid cells with mild atypia. See note.

Evaluation limited by scant cellularity.

Note: While the aspirate may represent chronic sialadenitis with metaplasia and reactive changes, a salivary gland neoplasm with basaloid features cannot be completely excluded. Recommend clinical and radiologic correlations and additional sampling if clnically indicated.

title: SG-AUS-oncocytic
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
ATYPIA OF UNDETERMINED SIGNIFICANCE
Scant oncocytic cells with cytological and/or architectural atypia. See note.

Evaluation limited by scant cellularity.

Note: While the aspirate may represent oncocytic metaplasia or oncocytic hyperplasia, a neoplastic process cannot be entirely excluded. Recommend clinical and radiologic correlations and additional sampling if clincally indicated.

title: SG-Benign neoplasm-Mixed tumor [SGPA]
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN NEOPLASM
Consistent with mixed tumor (pleomorphic adenoma)

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Presence of epithelial cells, mesencymal cells and fibrillar myxoid stroma. These findings are consistent with mixed tumor.

title: SG-Benign neoplasm-Warthin
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN NEOPLASM
Consistent with Warthin's tumor

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Flat sheets of oncocytic cells are present in a lymphocytic background. Warthin's tumor should be considered.

title: SG-benign/normal
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN/NORMAL

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Benign salivary lobules are seen. No malignant cells are found.

NOTE:
Clinical and radiologic correlations are needed to exclude a non-representative FNA sample.

title: SG-chronic sialadenitis
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Evaluation limited by scant cellularity
Consistent with chronic chronic sialadenitis. See note.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
It shows small groups of ductal cells with absent or scant acinar cells. 
Chronic inflammation is seen. No malignant cells are found.

Note: Clinical and radiologic correlations are recommended to ensure that the aspirate is representative of the lesion.

title: SG-Oncocytic
content:
Neoplasm with oncocytoid feature. See note.

Note: The specimen shows a low-grade neoplasm with oncocytoid features. Some lymphocytes are seen in the background. 
The differential diagnosis includes Warthin tumor and other tumors with oncocytic change.

title: SG-SUMP-Basaloid cell
content:
DIAGNOSIS:

NEOPLASM: SALIVARY GLAND NEOPLASM OF UNCERTAIN MALIGNANT POTENTIAL. (SUMP)

Cellular basaloid neoplasm. See note.
Note: The specimen show a monomorphic population of basaloid cells with minimal nuclear atypia associated with fibrillary matrix. No mitoses or tumor necrosis is seen. The findings are suggestive of a cellular pleomorphic adenoma; however, other matrix-producing basaloid tumors such as basal cell adenoma, basal cell adenocarcinoma, and epithelial-myoepithelial carcinoma cannot be completely excluded.

title: SG-SUMP-Clear cell
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
 NEOPLASM: SALIVARY GLAND NEOPLASM OF UNCERTAIN MALIGNANT POTENTIAL. (SUMP)

Cellular neoplasm with clear cell features

Note:

title: SG-SUMP-Oncocytic/oncocytoid neoplasm
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
 NEOPLASM: SALIVARY GLAND NEOPLASM OF UNCERTAIN MALIGNANT POTENTIAL. (SUMP)

Cellular oncocytic/oncocytoid neoplasm

Note:

title: SG-UN Non Diagnostic
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Non-diagnostic 

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears reveal acellularity or too scant cells for evaluation. Tissue biopsy is recommended if clinical suspicion of malignancy is high.

title: Soft tissue
content:
Schwannoma
Old fishnet

title: Spermatocele
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Spermatocele  

CYTOLOGY: scrotum

ADEQUACY:
Satisfactory for interpretation

Microscopic description:
Smear shows spermatozoa admixed with histiocytes and epididymal lining cells are found in persistent spermatoceles.

title: T-A
content:
AUS - cytologic atypia

Hypocellular specimen, however, findings are suggestive of a follicular lesion with some features suggestive of but not diagnostic for papillary carcinoma.

title: T-A cytologic atypia.
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
ATYPIA OF UNDETERMINED SIGNIFICANCE.
Focal cytologic atypia. 

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid gland, _, aspiration

ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show follicular cells which are predominantly benign appearing, but with occasional nuclear grooves or intranuclear inclusions. Papillary carcinoma cannot be confidently excluded. 

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Risk of malignancy: 6-18%.
3. Recommended management: Repeat FNA or lobectomy

title: T-A-architectural atypia
content:
ATYPIA OF UNDETERMINED SIGNIFICANCE. 

Sparsely cellular aspirate comprised of follicular cells with architectural atypia. Colloid is absent.

Note: Molecular testing or a repeat aspirate may be helpful if clinically indicated.

title: T-A-Hurthle cell aspirates
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Atypia of Undetermined Significance
Hurthle cell aspirates

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid gland, right, aspiration

ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
It shows a sparsely cellular aspirate comprised almost exclusively of Hurthle cells with minimal colloid and some lymphocytes in the background.

NOTE: Although this pattern is very low risk, a folicular neoplasm, Hurthle cell type, cannot be entirely excluded.
Please also correlate if patient has a history of Hashimoto thyroiditis. Clinical-radiologic correlation is advised.

title: T-B
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN
Consistent with a benign follicular nodule

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid, __, aspiration
ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show some well-preserved groups of benign and uniform-appearing follicular cells and some colloid material. No malignant cells are identified.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Risk of malignancy: 2-7%. 
3. Recommended management: Clinical and sonographic follow-up.

title: T-B-Colloid-Cyst
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN
Consistent with colloid nodule with cystic change

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid gland, ______, aspiration

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show abundant colloid,
consistent with colloid nodule. There are macrophages but no
epithelial cells.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda
   System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Cystic change can occur in nodular goiter and papillary
   carcinoma. Please correlate the sonographic findings and
   aspirate if residual solid part is present.

title: T-B-Cyst
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
NON-DIAGNOSTIC OR UNSATISFACTORY
Cystic fluid only

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid gland, _, fine needle aspiration

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
The specimen is nondiagnostic because it consists almost exclusively of histiocytes. Recommend correlation with cyst size and complexity on ultrasound to assist with further management of the lesion. 

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda
System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Cystic change can occur in nodular goiter and papillary carcinoma. Please correlate the sonographic findings and aspirate if residual solid part is present.

title: T-B-Hashimoto
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN
Consistent with chronic lymphocytic (Hashimoto''''s) thyroiditis

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid gland, ______, aspiration

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show some clusters of Hurthle cells and many lymphocytes and plasma cells in the background. Findings are suggestive of chronic lymphocytic (Hashimoto's) thyroiditis.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Risk of malignancy: 2-7%. 
3. Recommended management: Clinical and sonographic follow-up.

title: T-B-Hashimoto (不知道病人有沒有)
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN
Numerous polymorphic lymphoid cells and scattered oncocytic cells.

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid gland, ______, aspiration

Note: The findings are suggestive of chronic lymphocytic (Hashimoto) thyroiditis in the proper clinical setting.

title: T-B-NG
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN
Consistent with nodular goiter (follicular nodular disease)

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid, ___ lobe, aspiration

ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show a macrofollicular lesion composed of moderate to abundant follicular cells arranged in honeycomb flat sheets and some colloid material. Findings are suggestive of nodular goiter.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Risk of malignancy: 2-7%. 
3. Recommended management: Clinical and sonographic follow-up.

title: T-B-NG-C
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN
Consistent with nodular goiter (follicular nodular disease) with cystic change

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show a macrofollicular lesion composed of moderate follicular cells arranged in honeycomb flat sheets, abundant macrophages and some colloid material. Findings are suggestive of nodular goiter with cystic change.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Risk of malignancy: 2-7%. 
3. Recommended management: Clinical and sonographic follow-up.

title: T-B-NG-H
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN
Consistent with nodular goiter (follicular nodular disease) with Hurthle cell change

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show follicular cells and Hurthle cells in sheets or dispersed single cells. Colloid and foamy histiocytes are also present. Nodular goiter with Hurthle cell change is considered.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Risk of malignancy: 2-7%. 
3. Recommended management: Clinical and sonographic follow-up.

title: T-B-subacute granulomatous thyroiditis
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN
see NOTE. 

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid, left, aspiration
ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Scattered multinucleated giant cells are seen in a background of lymphocytes and degenerate cells.
    
NOTE: Subacute granulomatous thyroiditis should be considered. Please correlate clinically.

title: T-Colloid
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN
Consistent with colloid nodule

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show abundant colloid and scant follicular cells, consistent with colloid nodule.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Risk of malignancy: 2-7%. 
3. Recommended management: Clinical and sonographic follow-up.

title: T-cyst
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
NON-DIAGNOSTIC OR UNSATISFACTORY
Cystic fluid only

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid gland, ____, aspiration

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears contain mostly macrophages but no epithelial cells or colloid, consistent with cystic change.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda
   System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Cystic change can occur in nodular goiter and papillary
   carcinoma. Please correlate the sonographic findings and
   aspirate if residual solid part is present.

title: T-FN
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Suspicious for a Follicular Neoplasm

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Cellular aspirate of follicular cells with a predominantly microfollicular architecture, scattered isolated cells, and scant colloid.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. The differential diagnoses include nodular goiter, follicular adenoma, follicular variant of papillary carcinoma, follicular carcinoma, and the recently described NIFTP, which have overlapping cytomorphologic features and cannot be accurately distinguished by FNA alone. 
3. Risk of malignancy: 23-34%.
4. Recommended management: Molecular testing, diagnostic lobectomy.

title: T-FN DDx PTC, NIFTP
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Follicular Neoplasm

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Cellular aspirate of follicular cells with a predominantly microfollicular architecture, scattered isolated cells, and scant colloid.

Note:
Although the architectural features suggest a follicular neoplasm, some nuclear features raise the possibility of an invasive follicular variant of papillary carcinoma or its indolent counterpart, NIFTP; difinitive distinction among these entities is not possible on cytologic mateiral.

title: T-FN SEE NOTE
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Suspicious for a Follicular Neoplasm. See Note.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Cellular aspirate of follicular cells with a predominantly microfollicular architecture, scattered isolated cells, and scant colloid.

Note: Although the architectural features suggest a follicular neoplasm, some nuclear features raise the possibility of an invasive follicular variant of papillary carcinoma or its recently described indolent counterpart, NIFTP; definitive distinction among these entities is not possible on cytologic material.

title: T-FN-Sus
content:
Example 4 SUSPICIOUS FOR FOLLICULAR NEOPLASM (SEE NOTE).

Note: Although the architectural features suggest a follicular neoplasm, some nuclear features raise the possibility of an invasive follicular variant of papillary carcinoma or its recently described indolent counterpart, NIFTP; definitive distinction among these entities is not possible on cytologic material.

title: T-HN
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Suspicious for a follicular neoplasm, Hurthle cell (oncocytic) type

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show exclusively moderate to abundant Hurthle cells in dispersed single cells or clusters, absence of colloid and no lymphocytes. 

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. The risk of malignancy ranges from 10 to 40%.
3. Recommended management: Diagnostic surgical excision.

title: T-Hurthle (DDx)
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Hurthle cell change. (please see description)

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid gland, ____, aspiration

ADEQUACY:
Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show Hurthle cells in groups or dispersed single cells with round and eccentric nuclei. 
A few colloid and lymphocytes are present in the background.  
The differential diagnosis includes chronic lymphocytic thyroiditis, nodular goiter with Hurthle cell change and Hurthle cell neoplasm.

title: T-M-PAP
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
MALIGNANT
Papillary carcinoma

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid gland, _lobe, aspiration
ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show moderate to abundant atypical follicular cells in sheets, follicles and papillary groups. The atypical follicular cells have characteristic nuclear features of papillary carcinoma, including enlarged nuclei with ground glass feature, nuclear grooves and intranuclear inclusions. Scant colloid is found.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. The risk of malignancy ranges from 97-100%.
3. Recommended management: Near-total thyroidectomy or lobectomy
4. A small propotion of cases (~3-4%) diagnosed as malignant, compatible with PTC, may prove to be NIFTP on histology. 

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: T-M-PAP DDx NIFTP
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
MALIGNANT
Papillary carcinoma

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show moderate to abundant atypical follicular cells in sheets, follicles and papillary groups. The atypical follicular cells have characteristic nuclear features of papillary carcinoma, including enlarged nuclei with ground glass feature, nuclear grooves and intranuclear inclusions. Scant colloid is found.
    
Note:
With the recent reclassification of a subset of indolent thyroid malignancies as NIFTP, the positive predictive value of the malignant category for thyroid FNA is expected to drop from 99 to about 94-96%. Thus, a small proportion of cases is interpreted as malignant by FNA my prove to be NIFTP upon histolgic examination.

title: T-Sus-PAP
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
SUSPICIOUS FOR MALAIGNANCY
Suspicious for papillary carcinoma

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show atypical follicular cells in sheets and papillary groups. The atypical follicular cells have enlarged and ground glass nuclei. However, nuclear grooves and intranuclear inclusions are occasionally found. Findings are suspicious for papillary carcinoma.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. The risk of malignancy ranges from 67-83%.
3. Recommended management: Molecular testing, lobectomy or near-total thyroidectomy.

title: T-Sus-PAP DDx NIFTP
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
SUSPICIOUS FOR MALAIGNANCY
Suspicious for papillary carcinoma; see Note. 

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show microfollicular groups display nuclear enlargement, variable chromatin pallor, and rare nuclear grooves. 

Note: The overall cytomophologic features are suggestive of a follicular variant of papillary carcinoma or its recently described indolent counterpart, noninvasive follicular thyroid neoplasm with papillary-like nuclear features (NIFTP). Definite distinction between these entities is not possible on cytologic material.

title: T-UN
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
NON-DIAGNOSTIC OR UNSATISFACTORY
Virtually acellular specimen

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid, _, aspiration

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears contain no colloid and too scant follicular cells for
diagnosis.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda
   System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Recommended management: Repeat fine needle aspiration
   with ultrasound guidance.

title: T-UN <6 groups
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
NON-DIAGNOSTIC OR UNSATISFACTORY

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid, _, aspiration

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears contain fewer than six groups of well-preserved, well-stained follicular cell groups with ten cells each. 

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Bethesda
   System for Reporting Thyroid Cytopathology.
2. Recommended management: Repeat fine needle aspiration
   with ultrasound guidance.

title: T-UN 保存差
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
NONDIAGNOSTIC

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid, _, aspiration

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Specimen processed and examined, but nondiagnostic due to poor fixation and cellular preservation. 

NOTE: peat aspirate should be considered if clinically indicated.

title: T-UN 血片
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
NONDIAGNOSTIC

CYTOLOGY: Thyroid, _, aspiration

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Extensive obscuring blood hinders the evaluation of follicular cells.
A repeat aspiration should be considered if clinically indicated.

title: U-AUC
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Atypical urothelial cells present

CYTOLOGY: Urine

ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for evaluation

Note: The urine specimen contains rare urothelial cells with increased nuclear cytoplasmic ratios, nuclear enlargement, and irregular nuclear membranes. The paucity of these atypical urothelial cells limits further classification. According to The Paris System for reporting urinary cytology, the "atypical urothelial cells" category is associated with a 24-53% risk of high-grade malignancy. Follow-up is recommended as clinically warranted.

title: U-AUC+Candida
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Atypical urothelial cells present and Candida infection

Note: The urine specimen contains rare urothelial cells with increased nuclear cytoplasmic ratios, nuclear enlargement, and irregular nuclear membranes. The paucity of these atypical urothelial cells limits further classification. According to The Paris System for reporting urinary cytology, the  "atypical urothelial cells" category is associated with a 24-53% risk of high-grade malignancy. Follow-up is recommended as clinically warranted.

Candida hyphae and spores are also present.

title: U-candida infection
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Negative for high-grade urothelial carcinoma
Candida infection

CYTOLOGY: Urine

ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
The urothelial cells are benign and with reactive changes.
There is significant numbers of inflammatory cells present
in association with candida hyphae and spores. No malignant
cells are noted.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Paris System for Reporting Urinary Cytology.
2. Estimated risk of high-grade malignancy: 8-24%.

title: U-HGUC
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade urothelial carcinoma

CYTOLOGY: Urine

ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears contain atypical urothelial cells with high N/C ratio (>0.7), moderate to severe hyperchromasia, coarse chromatin and markedly irregular nuclear membranes. High-grade urothelial carcinoma is considered.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Paris System for Reporting Urinary Cytology.
2. Estimated risk of high-grade malignancy: 76-100%.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: U-HGUC+Squamous
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade urothelial carcinoma with squamous differentiation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears contain atypical urothelial cells with high N/C ratio (>0.7), moderate to severe hyperchromasia, coarse chromatin and markedly irregular nuclear membranes. Keratinization is also noted. High-grade urothelial carcinoma with squamous differentiation is considered.

Note:
The diagnostic category is according to The Paris System for Reporting Urinary Cytology. 
Estimated risk of high-grade malignancy: 76-100%.

title: U-LGUN
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Low-grade urothelial neoplasm

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears contain three-dimensional cellular papillary clusters with fibrovascular cores. Low-grade urothelial neoplasm is considered. This category includes urothelial papilloma, papillary urothelial neoplasm of low malignant potential (PUNLMP), Low-grade papillary urothelial carcinoma, and flat , low-grade intraurothelial neoplasia.

Note: 
The diagnostic category is according to The Paris System for Reporting Urinary Cytology.
Estimated risk of high-grade malignancy: 0-44%.

title: U-negative for HGUC
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Negative for high-grade urothelial carcinoma

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears contain benign urothelial cells or benign urothelial tissue fragments. No malignant cells are found.

Note: The diagnostic category is according to The Paris System for Reporting Urinary Cytology.

title: U-negative for HGUC_聖馬
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Negative for high-grade urothelial carcinoma

CYTOLOGY: Urine

ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
It shows benign urothelial cells or benign urothelial tissue fragments. No malignant cells are found.
    
Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Paris System
   for Reporting Urinary Cytology.        
2. Estimated risk of high-grade malignancy: 8-24%.

title: U-Polyoma virus
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Negative for high-grade urothelial carcinoma
Suggestive of polyoma virus infection

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears contain benign urothelial cells of predominantly superficial and intermediate cells. Decoy cells are present, suggestive of polyoma virus infection.

Note: 
The diagnostic category is according to The Paris System for Reporting Urinary Cytology.
Estimated risk of high-grade malignancy: 8-24%.

title: U-Suspicious for HGUC
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Suspicious for high-grade urothelial carcinoma

CYTOLOGY: Urine
ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears contain a few atypical urothelial cells with high N/C ratio (>0.7), and at least 2 of the 3 following features : moderate to severe hyperchromasia, irregular clumpy chromatin and irregular nuclear membranes. These findings are suspicious for high-grade urothelial carcinoma.

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Paris System
   for Reporting Urinary Cytology.
2. Estimated risk of high-grade malignancy: 59-94%.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: U-UN
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Unsatisfactory/Non-diagnostic

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show a virtually acellular specimen

Note:
1. The diagnostic category is according to The Paris System for Reporting Urinary Cytology.
2. Recommended management: Repeat cytology, cystoscopy in 3 months if increased clinicical suspicion.
TR

title: T-A-architectural (scant cells)
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
ATYPIA OF UNDETERMINED SIGNIFICANCE. 
Sparsely cellular aspirate comprised of follicular cells with architectural atypia. Colloid is absent.

TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Thyroid, ___ lobe, fine needle aspiration
ADEQUACY: Specimen adequacy is limited by scant epithelial cellularity

Note: A repeat aspirate may be helpful in order to further characterize the lesion.

title: T-A-nuclear
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
ATYPIA OF UNDETERMINED SIGNIFICANCE. 
Follicular cells, predominantly benign-appearing, with focal nuclear atypia.

TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Thyroid, ___ lobe, FNA
ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show follicular cells which are predominantly benign appearing, but with occasional nuclear grooves or intranuclear inclusions. Papillary carcinoma cannot be confidently excluded.

title: T-A-oncocytic (patient with multiple, bilateral nodules; multinocular goiter)
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
ATYPIA OF UNDETERMINED SIGNIFICANCE. 
The specimen is moderately cellular and consists almost exclusively of oncocytic cells. Colloid is scant, and there is no apparent increase in lymphoid cells. 
Note: In a patient with multiple nodules, the findings likely represent oncocytic cell hyperplasia in the setting of multinodular goiter; however an oncocytic fol-licular neoplasm cannot be entirely excluded.

TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Thyroid, ___ lobe, fine needle aspiration
ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

title: T-B
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN
Consistent with nodular goiter (follicular nodular disease)

TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Thyroid, ___ lobe, fine needle aspiration

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Smears show a macrofollicular lesion composed of moderate to abundant follicular cells arranged in honeycomb flat sheets and some colloid material. Findings are suggestive of nodular goiter.

title: T-B-NG-oncocytic
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
BENIGN
Benign-appearing follicular cells, colloid, and occasional oncocytic cells, consistent with nodular goiter (follicular nodular disease).
