title: T-FN
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
FOLLICULAR NEOPLASM
Cellular aspirate of follicular cells with a predominantly microfollicular architecture, scattered isolated cells, and scant colloid.
Note: Although the cytologic features are in keeping with a follicular neoplasm (FN), approximately 30% of cases diagnosed as Follicular Neoplasm (Bethesda IV) on FNA turn out to be benign follicular nodular disease on surgical resection. Potential histologic follow-up on resection include most commonly follicular adenoma, and less commonly (in decreasing order of frequency) hyperplastic nodules, follicular thyroid carcinoma, and much less likely NIFTP or FVPTC.

TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Thyroid, ___ lobe, fine needle aspiration
ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

title: T-FN-oncocytic
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
FOLLICULAR NEOPLASM (oncocytic follicular neoplasm)
Cellular aspirate consisting of abundant isolated oncocytes in the absence of colloid.

TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Thyroid, ___ lobe, fine needle aspiration
ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

title: T-FN-r/o PTC
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
FOLLICULAR NEOPLASM
Note: Although the architectural features suggest a follicular neoplasm, some nuclear features raise the possibility of an invasive follicular variant of papillary carcinoma or its indolent counterpart, NIFTP; definitive distinction among these entities is not possible on cytologic material.

TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Thyroid, ___ lobe, fine needle aspiration
ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

title: T-M-PTC
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
MALIGNANT.
Papillary thyroid carcinoma.

Note: With the reclassification of a subset of indolent thyroid malignancies as noninvasive follicular thyroid neoplasm with papillary-like nuclear features (NIFTP), the positive predictive value of the malignant category for thyroid FNA is expected to drop from 97-99% to about 94-96%. Thus, a small proportion of cases interpreted as malignant by FNA may prove to be NIFTP upon histologic examination.

TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Thyroid, ___ lobe, fine needle aspiration
ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

title: T-SUS-PTC
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
SUSPICIOUS FOR MALIGNANCY.
Suspicious for papillary thyroid carcinoma.

TYPE OF SPECIMEN: Thyroid, ___ lobe, fine needle aspiration
ADEQUACY: Satisfactory for interpretation

title: abnormal MMR
content: Adenocarcinoma, with abnormal result of IHC for mismatch repair testing (see description).

title: Acute appendicitis with serrated adenoma
content:
Acute appendicitis with serrated adenoma

Sections show appendiceal tissue featuring serrated adenoma. The adenoma shows
architectural complexity with serrations extending into the crypt bases, which are
dilated. The lining epithelial cells show focally mild dysplasia. The appendiceal wall
is diffusely infiltrated by PMNs.

title: Acute appendicitis, gangrenous and perforated.
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Appendix, appendectomy --- Acute appendicitis, gangrenous and perforated.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of an appendix measuring _ cm in length and ___ cm in diameter.

Representative sections are taken and submitted in one cassette.

Microscopic description:
The appendicial wall is diffusely infiltrated by dense PMNs with gangrenous change and loss of its architecture. Focal abscess formation is noted. The serosa is coated by fibrinopurulent exudate. No malignancy is observed.

title: Acute colitis
content:
acute colitis.

Section shows colonic mucosal fragments revealing intact glands and neutrophilic infiltrate in the lamina propria. No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: Acute colitis (clinical r/o IBD)
content:
Acute colitis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows colonic mucosal tissue with ulceration and mixed inflammatory
infiltrate in the lamina propria. Crypt abscess and focal crypt distortion are seen.
No viral inclusion is highlighted by anti-CMV immunostain. Inflammatory bowel disease
should be excluded clinically. No malignancy is observed.

content:

Section shows gastric tissue with neoplastic cells featuring enlarged nuclei and prominent nucleoli arranged in villoglandular and cribriform patterns. 
No Helicobacter Pylori is seen. 
Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are highlighted by cytokeratin (AE1/AE3).

Adenocarcinoma.

IHC Testing for Mismatch Repair (MMR) Proteins

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

Intramucosal adenocarcinoma Criteria

title: Adenomatous lesion
content:
Adenomatous lesion.

The lamina propria is edematous and inflamed. No invasion is identified.  Although no definite evidence of malignancy is noted, re-biopsy or close follow-up is suggested.

title: Angiodysplasia with hemorrhage and infarct.
content:
PATHOLOGICAL DIAGNOSIS:
Angiodysplasia with hemorrhage and infarct.

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
Sections reveal increased number of tortuous small to medium-sized vessels in the submucosa and subserosa. The
vessels are dilated and lined by mildly pump endothelial cells and have a thin layer of smooth muscle. Hemorrhagic
necrosis from mucosal to inner muscular propria with neutrophilic infiltration is present. Bilateral resection ends are viable. There is no evidence
for malignancy.

title: APP_ruptured_right hemi
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Appendix, needlescopic right hemicolectomy --- 
Acute appendicitis, ruptured.

Received is a right hemicolectomy specimen including
terminal ileum (_ cm in length and _ cm in circumference),
ascending colon (_ cm in length and _ cm in circumference),
and appendix (_ cm in length and _ cm in diameter).
The external surface of the appendix is coated with purulent exudates.

Representative sections are taken and labeled as follows:
A1: Bilateral margins
A2¡VA3: Appendix
A4:
A5: Other area
A6: Pericolonic tissue

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
The appendiceal wall is partially necrotic with loss of its architecture. The PMN infiltration is diffuse and extensive, involving the entire thickness of the wall. The serosa is coated with fibrinopurulent exudate.
A diverticulus revealing chronic inflammatory infiltrate near the base is seen.
Five lymph nodes seen show preserved nodal architecture. No malignancy is observed.

title: At least intramucosal adenocarcinoma (see description)
content:
At least intramucosal adenocarcinoma (see description)

Microscopic description:
Section shows superficial sampling of colonic mucosal tissue composed of tubuloglandular structures that are lined by tall columnar epithelium exhibiting high-grade dysplasia with architectural complexity. Lamina propria invasion is noted.

The overall features suggest an adenocarcinoma with invasion into lamina propria at least. The sample doesnt contain submucosal tissue for evaluation. Underlying invasion cannot be excluded due to superficial sampling of this tissue. Clinical correlation is suggested.

NOTE: No submucosal tissue is present for proper evaluation of invasion depth. Underlying submucosal invasion cannot be excluded.

title: Autoimmune metaplastic atrophic gastritis/pernicious anemia pattern

title: Colon EMR
content:
Adenocarcinoma.

Microscopic description:
1. Tumor histological type: adenocarcinoma
2. Tumor size: cm
3. Histological grade: well / moderately /poorly differentiated; undifferentiated
4. Invasion depth: submucosa (T1)
5 Tumor Budding: Number of tumor buds in 1 “hotspot” field (specify total number in  area=0.785 mm2)
___ Low score (0-4) ___Intermediate score (5-9) ___High score (10 or more) ___  Cannot be determined
6. Type of polyp in which invasive carcinoma arose: __Tubular adenoma; __Tubulovillous  adenoma; __Villous
adenoma; __Hyperplastic polyp; __ Sessile serrated adenoma/polyp; __ Traditional  serrated adenoma;
7. Depth of submucosa invasion: <1 mm/ >1mm
8. Angiolymphatic invasion: present / absent
9. Perineural invasion: present / absent
10. Margin: uninvolved/difficult to define in this specimen) (if close, ---- mm distance)
pTNM :   pT___N___( AJCC 8th Edition)
Immunohistochemical stain:  (Results reported in previous specimen _)
    EGFR: _+ intensity of membranous staining in _% of
    tumor cells.
    IHC Testing for Mismatch Repair (MMR) Proteins
    1. MLH1:intact nuclear expression
    2. MSH2:intact nuclear expression
    3. MSH6:intact nuclear expression
    4. PMS2:intact nuclear expression
IHC Interpretation: No loss of nuclear expression of MMR proteins: low probability of microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H)

title: Colon_MMR
content:
COMMENT: There are exceptions to the above IHC interpretations. These results should not be considered in isolation, and clinical correlation with genetic counseling is recommended to assess the need for germline testing.
NOTE I: (Interpretation of the test)
No loss of nuclear expression of MMR proteins: low probability of microsatellite instability-high (MSI-H)Loss of nuclear expression of MLH1 and PMS2: testing for methylation of the MLH1 promoter and/or mutation of BRAF is indicated (the presence of a BRAF V600E mutation and/or MLH1 methylation suggests that the tumor is sporadic and germline evaluation is probably not indicated; absence of both MLH1 methylation and of BRAF V600E mutation suggests the possibility of Lynch syndrome, and sequencing and/or large deletion/duplication testing of germline MLH1 may be indicated)Loss of nuclear expression of MSH2 and MSH6: high probability of Lynch syndrome (sequencing and/or large deletion/duplication testing of germline MSH2 may be indicated, and, if negative, sequencing and/or large deletion/duplication testing of germline MSH6 may be indicated)Loss of nuclear expression of MSH6 only: high probability of Lynch syndrome (sequencing and/or large deletion/duplication testing of germline MSH6 may be indicated)Loss of nuclear expression of PMS2 only: high probability of Lynch syndrome (sequencing and/or large deletion/duplication testing of germline PMS2 may be indicated)Reference: CAP Template for Reporting Results of Biomarker Testing of Specimens From Patients With Carcinoma of the Colon and Rectum Version: 1.2.0.0

NOTE II:
Most pathologists regard convincingly strong expression of MMR proteins within >10% of the tumor as indicative of preserved (intact) expression.
Reference: Am J Surg Pathol 2016;40:e17¡Ve34.

title: Colonic inertia
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Colon, total abdominal colectomy --- Vascular congestion and fibrosis, clinically colonic inertia.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received is a total colectomy specimen including ileum (_ cm in length, _ cm in diameter), appendix (_ cm in length, _ cm in diameter), and colon (_ cm in length, _ cm in circumference). The mucosa of colon is focally flattening with variable thickness of bowel wall. No tumor is seen.

Representative sections are taken and labeled as follows:
A1: bilateral margins
A2: appendix
A3: ileocecal valve
A4-A8 colon
A9: pericolonic fat    

Microscopic description:
The colonic tissue shows variable thickness of muscularis propria with fibrosis, accompanied by vascular congestion of bowel wall. Ganglion cells are present throughout the colonic segments that were sampled. The appendix demonstrates fibrous obliteration.
No evidence of inflammation or infection is seen.

Immunohistochemically, the  dysplastic cells show block-like p16 positivity. Ki-67 staining shows increased  proliferation index in these dysplastic cells.

title: Herpes esophagitis
content:
The esophageal mucosal tissues show ulceration with intraepithelial neutrophilic infiltration and reactive epithelial atypia. Separate necrotic exudates are also seen
admixed with the squamous cells revealing nuclear changes of nuclear moulding, chromatin margination and multinucleation, consistent with Herpes esophagitis.

The GMS and PAS stains are negative for fungal hyphae. No malignancy is observed.

ADDENDUM REPORT (ISSUED on 2015/03/13)
Immunostaining for anti-CMV performed per request by the clinician is negative. The
histologic findings are characteristics for Herpes simplex esophagitis.

title: IBD checklist
content:
2024.9更新 twiap

Architectural distortion: not identified/mild/moderate/severe
Nancy index:
  absence of significant histological disease (grade 0)
  chronic inflammatory infiltrate with no acute inflammatory infiltrate (grade 1)
  mildly active disease (grade 2)
  moderately active disease (grade 3)
  severely active disease (grade 4)
Granuloma: not identified/present in section____.
Paneth metaplasia in distal colon (splenic flexure and beyond): not identified/present in section____.
Pyloric metaplasia: not identified/present in section____.
Basal plasmacytosis: not identified/present in section____.
 Neutrophilic infiltrate:  lamina propria   cryptitis   microabscess
Dysplasia: not identified/indefinite for dysplasia/present in section____
(1) Conventional dysplasia: low-grade/high-grade 
(2) Nonconventional dysplasia: type: hypermucinous/goblet cell deficient/crypt cell dysplasia/traditional serrated adenoma (TSA)-like/sessile serrated lesion (SSL)-like/serrated dysplasia, no otherwise specified. 
Fissuring ulcer: not identified/present in section____.
Ancillary studies:
  No invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by AE1/AE3.     
  No viral inclusion is demonstrated by anti-CMV immunostain.    

Overall assessment: (optional)
Normal or nearly normal intestinal tissue 
Mild non-specific chronic inflammation
Quiescent colitis
More supportive of acute colitis
More supportive of ulcerative colitis
More supportive of Crohn’s disease
Supportive of inflammatory bowel disease but further subclassification can't be done

Comment:
The diagnosis of inflammatory bowel disease cannot be made by histology alone. Please correlate with clinical presentation.
The absence of inflammation or only mild inflammation can be due to sampling of uninflammatory sites or treatment and cannot be used against the diagnosis of inflammatory bowel disease.

Explanatory Note:
Please make the diagnosis and rule out other disease (such as amebiasis, lymphoma, etc.) before grading the lesions.  
Nancy Index: 
Grade 0: No or only mild increase in chronic inflammatory cells
Grade 1: Moderate or severe increase in chronic inﬂammatory cells (lymphocytes, plasma cells, and eosinophils) deﬁned as presence of an increase in chronic inﬂammatory cells that is easily apparent.
Grade 2: Mild increase in neutrophils deﬁned as few or rare neutrophils in lamina propria or in the epithelium that are difﬁcult to see
Grade 3: Moderate or severe increase neutrophils deﬁned as presence of multiple clusters of neutrophils in lamina propria and/or in epithelium that are easily apparent
Grade 4: Ulcers or erosions deﬁned as loss of colonic crypts replaced with “immature” granulation tissue (disorganized blood vessels with extravasated neutrophils) or the presence of ﬁbrinopurulent exudate
CMV immunostaining is suggested for all cases with ulceration and treatment refractory colitis
Some antibody clones (such as the clone used in Ventana machine) showed non-specific cytoplasmic granular staining in neutrophils. Please only interpretate the cells with strong positive nuclear staining as positive. 
CMV immunostaining should be graded as low grade (<=4 positive cells per piece of tissue) or high grade (>5 positive cells in at least one piece of tissue)
SATB2 is lost in 40% of IBD-associated dysplasia but not loss in regenerative colonic mucosa. Hence, SATB2 immunostaining is suggested for cases with indefinite for dysplasia.
Lymphoid aggregate and lymphoid follicle formation are normal finding for terminal ileum specimen. Should not diagnose as inflammatory tissue only based on this finding. 
Histologic remission is defined as Nancy grade 0 or 1 and is used as the criteria for treatment end point. 
The American College of Gastroenterology and American Gastroenterology Association suggest that the presence or absence of dysplasia is confirmed by at least one pathologist who is skilled in GI pathology or who has broad experience with dysplasia in UC. 

 

(A) Ulceration of colonic mucosa with inflamed granulation tissue corresponding to grade 4 of the Nancy index (HES ×200). (B) Ulceration of colonic mucosa with neutrophils in fibrin corresponding to grade 4 of the Nancy index (HES ×200). (C) Presence of multiple clusters of neutrophils in lamina propria and/or in epithelium that are easily apparent. Acute inflammatory cells infiltrate is moderate to severe, corresponding to grade 3 of the Nancy index. (HES ×200). (D) Presence of few or rare neutrophils in lamina propria or in the epithelium that are difficult to see. Acute inflammatory cells infiltrate is mild, corresponding to grade 2 of the Nancy index (HES ×200). (E) Biopsy specimen showing no acute inflammatory cells infiltrate and presence of a moderate-to-severe increase in chronic inflammatory cells number corresponding to grade 1 of the Nancy index (HES ×200). (F) Biopsy specimen showing a mild increase in chronic inflammatory cell number. In this case, it defines a grade 0 of the Nancy index (HES ×200). HES, hematoxylin-eosin-saffron.
Marchal-Bressenot et al. Gut, 2016

title: IBD checklist (old version)
content:
Microscopic description:
Architecture:
  Distortion: focal / diffuse
  Mucin/goblet cell depletion: mild / moderate / severe
  Paneth cell metaplasia: present / absent
  Dysplasia: absent / low-grade / high grade
  Associated with adenocarcinoma: absent
Inflammatory infiltrates:
  Neutrophilic infiltrate:  lamina propria   cryptitis   microabscess
  Basal plasmacytosis: present / absent
  Eosinophilic infiltrate: mild / prominent
  Epithelioid granuloma: present / absent
Ancillary studies:
  No invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by AE1/AE3.     
  No viral inclusion is demonstrated by anti-CMV immunostain.    

Differential diagnosis:
  TB (acid-fast stain, PCR)
  Amebiasis, CMV, Pseudomembranous colitis
  Behcet’s disease
  Lymphoma

Comments:
  No evidence of inflammatory bowel disease
  Chronic active colitis, indeterminate
  In favor of inflammatory bowel disease over other disease
    In favor of ulcerative colitis
    In favor of Crohn disease
    Cannot be subclassified
    Quiescent stage

Activity score: Nancy histological index
  absence of significant histological disease (grade 0)
  chronic inflammatory infiltrate with no acute inflammatory infiltrate (grade 1)
  mildly active disease (grade 2)
  moderately active disease (grade 3)
  severely active disease (grade 4)

title: Intestinal pseudo-obstruction, constipation, inertia
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Colon, total abdominal colectomy ---
consistent with intestinal pseudo-obstruction.
Vascular congestion and fibrosis, clinically colonic inertia.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received is a total colectomy specimen including ileum (__ cm in length, __ cm in diameter), appendix (__ cm in length, __ cm in diameter), and colon (__ cm in length, __ cm in circumference). The mucosa of colon is focally flattening with variable thickness of bowel wall. No tumor is seen.

Representative sections are taken and labeled as follows:
A1: bilateral margins
A2: appendix
A3: ileocecal valve
A4-A8 colon
A9: pericolonic fat    

Microscopic description:
The colonic tissue shows variable thickness of muscularis propria with degenerative
change of myocytes, accompanied by delicate interstitial fibrosis in the muscularis
propria, especially the inner circular layer which is highlighted by Masson Trichrome
stain. Vascular congestion of bowel wall and focal submucosal fibrosis are also seen. Ganglion cells are present throughout
the colonic segments that were sampled. No evidence of inflammation or infection is
seen. The appendix is unremarkable.

Immunohistochemically, S100 stain demonstrates the neural plexuses without remarkable
changes. CD117 highlights the interstitial cells of Cajal. SMA and desmin show no
obvious decreased staining in the muscular layer. Staining for Congo red is negative,
excluding amyloidosis. No inclusion or microorganism is demonstrated by PAS stain.

The overall features suggest motility disorder which can be primary or secondary to
systemic conditions such as diabetes mellitus, scleroderma or drug injury etc.
Clinical correlation is recommended.
Microscopic description:
The colonic tissue shows variable thickness of muscularis propria with vascular congestion of bowel wall. Ganglion cells are present throughout the colonic segments that were sampled. No evidence of inflammation or infection is
seen. The appendix is unremarkable.

title: intestinal-type adenoma. [duodenum,small intestine, ampulla]
content:
Intestinal-type adenoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows crowded tubuloglandular structures that are lined by tall columnar epithelium in which individual cells exhibit mild dysplasia. Paneth and goblet cells are intermingled. No frank malignancy is noted.

title: Intramucosal adenocarcinoma
content:
Intramucosal adenocarcinoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows crowded tubuloglandular structures that are lined by tall columnar epithelium. The lining epithelium exhibit high-grade dysplasia which shows rounded, heaped-up cells with open chromatin and prominent nucleoli, as well as architectural complexity. Lamina propria invasion is focally noted. The surgical margin is free. 

NOTE: Intramucosal adenocarcinoma is biologically equivalent to high-grade dysplasia.

[We report intramucosal carcinoma in adenomas as such and always include a note stating that it is biologically equivalent to high-grade dysplasia (Tis) and that complete polypectomy should be curative. We report our findings this way in case additional sampling discloses deeper invasion.]

Consistent with ischemic colitis.

Section shows superficial epithelial erosion with preserved deep portion of the colonic crypts, accompanied by focal reactive atypia. Fibrosis and granulation tissue formation in the lamina propria are also discerned. No malignancy is observed.

content:
-- Ulceration.

with infiltrate of acute and chronic inflammatory cells.
Necrotic debris are seen over the destructed surface.
No viral inclusion is demonstrated by anti-CMV immunostain.
Some bright purple crystals with fish scale appearance are
identified. These structures are black on AFB and are

title: LAMN
content:
The appendix show dilated lumen containing mucus with lining mucosa revealing areas of  villous hyperplasia with mild cellular atypia and also areas of attenuation of the mucosa, loss of submucosal lymphoid tissue and obliteration of muscularis mucosa.
Acelllular mucin pools in the wall, subserosal tissue and focal serosal surface are noted. No evidence of invasive carcinoma is seen. Five pericolic lymph nodes are identified and they are free of metastasis.

Taken together, this is consistent with a low-grade appendiceal mucinous neoplasm with  focal acellular mucin spread beyond serosa. The surgical margins are free of tumor.

Margin: Cannot be evaluated

title: Leiomyoma
content:
Leiomyoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows colonic mucosal tissue with proliferative fascicles of spindle cells with brightly eosinophilic cytoplasm and blunt-ended nuclei, in association with muscularis mucosae. Neither increased mitotic activity nor cellular atypia is noted.

Immunohistochemically, the spindle cells are positive for desmin but not CD34 or
CD117, consistent with smooth muscle differentiation.

melanosis coli.

title: Meta Colorectal CA with CDX2 negative COMMENT
content:
COMMENT:
The overall features show metastatic adenocarcinoma with immunophenotypes (CK7+/CK20-/CDX2-) not typical for colorectal origin. However, a proportion of colorectal
adenocarcinoma can be CK20-negative or with loss of CDX2 expression. A diagnosis of metastatic adenocarcinoma of colorectal origin can be made after clinico-radiological
correlation for exclusion of other origin.

title: Metastatic adenocarcinoma, consistent with colorectal origin
content:
Metastatic adenocarcinoma, consistent with colorectal origin.

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
Section shows lung tissue infiltrated by complex tubuloglandular structures lined by neoplastic columnar cells with necrosis. 

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for CDX-2, consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma of colorectal origin.

content:
Mixed hemorrhoid.

Fungal hyphae identified (see comment).

Microscopic Description:
The section shows lung tissue with abundant fungal elements, including several broad hyphae which are thin-walled and pauciseptate.

Comment:
Fungal infection such as mucormycosis should be considered. The differential diagnosis includes aspergillosis. Correlation with microbial cultures (or molecular analysis) is essential if determination of fungal species is required for clinical management.

title: Mucosal schwann cell hamartoma
content:
Mucosal schwann cell hamartoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows polypoid colonic mucosal tissue with uniform, bland-looking spindle cells in the lamina propria. These spindle cells shows elongated, wavy nuclei, fine chromatin, and indistinct nucleoli, abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm and ill-defined cell borders. Entrapped crypts are seen.

By immunohistochemistry, these spindles cells are positive for S-100, but negative for desmin, SMA, CD117 or EMA.

mucosal tag.

title: perineurioma (benign fibroblastic polyp).
content:
perineurioma (benign fibroblastic polyp).

title: phlebosclerotic colitis_biopsy
content:
Ischemic change, consistent with phlebosclerotic colitis (see description).

Microscopic description:
Section shows deposition of collagen in the lamina propria and around blood vessels in the mucosa consistent
with ischemic change. Focal infiltrate of mixed inflammatory cells is algo seen. Congo red is negative for amyloid deposition.

The characteristic features of phlebosclerotic colitis, which include calcification and thrombosis of the mesenteric
veins, are difficult to be evaluated in the mucosal biopsy.

Together with the imaging findings, it can be consistent but not pathognomonic for phlebosclerotic colitis.

Correlate with clinical history of taking Chinese herbs is mandatory.

No Helicobacter Pylori is seen.

title: Resins
content:
Sevelamer/___ resins identified, See note

Note: In the small series available, Sevelamer was sometimes associated with mucosal injury.

Eosinophilic crystals identified, See note

Note: No clinical notes are available at time of slide review. Eosinophilic crystals are seen on H&E. These structures are yellow on AFB and are suggestive of Bile Acid Sequestrants (BAS: Colesevelam (Welchol), Colestipol (Colestid), Cholestyramine (LoCholest, Prevalite, Questran). In the small series available, BAS have no established role in causing mucosal damage.
    

Reference: Swanson BJ, Limketkai BN, Liu TC, Montgomery E, Nazari K, Park JY, Santangelo WC, Torbenson MS, Voltaggio L, Yearsley MM, Arnold CA. Sevelamer crystals in the gastrointestinal tract (GIT): a new entity associated with mucosal injury. Am J Surg Pathol. 2013 Nov;37(11):1686-93. ￼

title: UC vs infectious colitis
content:
chronic active colitis with cryptitis and crypt abscesses (please see description).

Microscopic description:
Section shows mild and focal crypt architectural distortion, lymphoplasmacytic expansion of the lamina propria, cryptitis and crypt abscesses. Many neutrophils are also seen within the lamina propria.
NOTE: The overall findings are not pathognomonic but consistent with ulcerative colitis. However, presence of many neutrophils within the lamina propria is more favor of infectious colitis. The initial episode of ulcerative colitis may histologically appear similar to infectious colitis; therefore, infectious etiologies should be excluded prior to confirming a diagnosis of ulcerative colitis.

Immunohistochemically, no viral inclusions are demonstrated by anti-CMV immunostain.

Necrotic debris are seen over the destructed surface as well as separate fragments. No Helicobacter pylori microorganisms are highlighted by Giemsa stain. Fungal spores and hyphae are seen.

Microscopic description:
The gastric tissue fragments reveal partial destruction of the mucosa with marked infiltration by mixed inflammatory cells in the lamina propria. Necrotic debris are seen over the destructed surface as well as separate fragments. 

Helicobacter pylori microorganisms are identified in the surface of the adjacent mucosa and in the gastric pits. 

No invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by AE1/AE3.

Ancillary studies:
1. Special stain: Giemsa stain.
2. Immunohistochemical stain: AE1/AE3.

mild chronic inflammation (see description).

No Helicobater Pylori is seen.

content:
-- Ulcer with Helicobacter pylori infection.
-- Intestinal metaplasia.

Some Helicobacter Pylori microorganisms are demonstrated by Giemsa stain. No invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by AE1/AE3. No malignancy is observed.

title: Ulcer, no CMV (colon) [COLulcer]

Ulceration.

No malignancy is observed.

There is no granuloma or cryptitis, crypt abscess identified.

title: ULCER00 no HP no fungus [stoulcer
content:
ulceration.

The gastric tissue reveals partial destruction of the mucosa with infiltrate of acute and chronic inflammatory cells in the lamina propria. Necrotic debris are seen over the destructed surface. 
No Helicobacter pylori microorganisms are identified by Giemsa stain. No invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by AE1/AE3. No fungus is highlighted by PAS. No malignancy is observed.

Ancillary studies:
1. Special stain: Giemsa stain, PAS.
2. Immunohistochemical stain: AE1/AE3.

title: Ulceration with cellular atypia
content:
Ulceration with cellular atypia (see COMMENT)

Microscopic description:
Section shows gastric tissue fragments revealing partial
destruction of the mucosa with marked infiltration by mixed
inflammatory cells in the lamina propria. Necrotic
debris is seen over the destructed surface as well as
separate fragments. No Helicobacter Pylori is seen. No
invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by AE1/AE3.

COMMENT:
Focal cellular atypia along the ulcer base is noted. This is
most likely due to the ulcer/inflammation. However, the
possibility of dysplasia cannot be excluded. Please
re-biopsy after the treatment of the ulcer.

title: Ulceration with cellular atypia (Esophagus)
content:
Ulceration with cellular atypia (see COMMENT).

Microscopic description:
Section shows juxtaposed squamous and gastric mucosal tissue
with ulceration and mixed inflammatory cells infiltrate. 
No intestinal metaplasia is seen.

COMMENT:
Focal cellular atypia of the squamous epithelium is noted along the ulcer base. p53 demonstrates a wild-type staining pattern, while Ki-67 is increased. These changes are most likely reactive, secondary to ulceration and inflammation; however, the possibility of dysplasia cannot be entirely excluded. Clinical follow-up and re-biopsy, if indicated, are recommended.

content:
Vascular ectasia

Some dilated mucosal capillaries are seen in the lamina
propria. The colonic glands are unremarkable.

Villous adenoma

It shows a polypoid tissue composed of fingerlike projections in which individual villus is lined by tall columnar epithelium featuring hyperchromatism and pseudostratification of the nuclei. No stromal invasion as to suggest malignant transformation is noted.

title: 申覆 GI
content:
This case is a clinically gastric ulcer, r/o malignancy.

There is dense infiltrate of inflammatory cells within the lamina propria, in which a poorly-differentiated adenocarcinoma could be overlooked solely on HE-stained section. AE1/AE3 is perfromed to exclude malignancy.  Thank you.

There is a dense lymphoid infiltrate with nodular pattern that the differential diagnosis include lymphoid hyperplasia and follicular lymphoma.
CD3, CD20, and bcl2 are performed to exclude follicular lymphoma.

Crohn disease is suspected by clinician.
CMV can complicate with IBD, asides with equivical stroma. cells, anti-CMV immunostain is performed to exclude CMV infection.
CD68 is performed to see if there is granuloma which is usually poorly formed  in Crohn disease and could be difficultly seen on HE stain0

title: Acute tubular necrosis (kidney)
content:
Sections show renal tissue cores revealing tubular dilatation with necrosis 
of epithelial cells. Some peritubular chronic inflammatory cells are seen.

title: AML, angiomyolipoma
content: The tissue fragments are composed predominantly of mature adipose tissue along with spindled and epithelioid myoid cells, and thick-walled blood vessels. Mitotic activity is rare (<1/10 HPF). Fat necrosis with aggregates of foamy histiocytes, and hemorrhage are seen in focal areas.

title: ASAP [F]
content:
atypical small acinar proliferation

Microscopic description:
Section shows prostatic tissue with tiny aggregates of atypical acini.

Immunohistochemically, there is focal loss of p63 and 34BE12-positive basal cells in the periphery of these atypical glands. No immunoreactivity for AMACR is noted in these atypical acini. These foci represent atypical small acinar proliferation (ASAP). Re-biopsy is recommended.

TESTIS: Sections of the testis reveal congestion with focal atrophy of seminiferous tubules. Necrotizing inflammation is seen within tunica albuginea and tunica vaginalis. No malignancy is observed.

title: HGPIN
content:
high-grade prostatic intraepithelial neoplasia.

Microscopic description:
It shows prostatic tissue strips with focal high-grade prostatic intraepithelial neoplasia, which shows bridging or tufting of epithelial cells bearing conspicuous nucleoli and being immunoreactive to p504s with surrounding basal cells highlighted by p63 and HMWCK (34βE12) immunostains. Close follow-up with repeated biopsy is highly recommended.

title: Intraductal carcinoma of the prostate; see comment.
content:
Comment: Intraductal carcinoma is usually associated with high-grade invasive cancer. 
Re-biopsy is suggested. 
Thought whether to proceed directly to definitve therapy versus do a repeat biopsy as our recommendation to discover invasive (usually high-grade cancer) has been debated.

title: inverted PUNLMP
content:
Inverted papillary urothelial neoplasm of low malignant potential (inverted PUNLMP).

MICROSCOPIC FINDINGS:
Sections show mostly inverted papilloma, characterized by anastomosing urothelial cords of normal thickness and preserved polarity, growing in an endophytic pattern from the normal overlying urothelium, invaginating into the lamina propria. However, focal areas show thickening of the urothelium, with mild cellular and nuclear enlargement and without variation in nuclear size.
Immunohistochemically, the urothelial cells are negative for CK20. No overexpression of p53 is seen. The Ki67 proliferation index shows no siginificant increase. The pathologic findings are consistent with inverted PUNLMP.
Chips of benign prostatic tissue is also seen.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: Inverted urothelial papilloma.
content:
Inverted urothelial papilloma.

MICROSCOPIC FINDINGS:
Section shosws downward growing nests and trabeculae of urothelial cells, with pseudoglandular spaces and peripheral palisading. The urothelial cells show spindled appearance with little intervening stroma. Overlying surface urothelium is unremarkable.

title: Malakoplakia
content:
Malakoplakia.
Microscopic description: 
Sections show soft tissue lined by urothelium accompanied by accumulation of foamy histiocytes (von Hansemann's histiocytes) containing Michaelis-Gutmann bodies in the subepeithelial tissue.

Immunohistochemically, the histiocytes are highlighted by CD68. The urothelium is positive for CK7 but not CK20.

Stainings for PAS and GMS highlight the Michaelis-Gutmann bodies, consistent with the diagnosis.

title: Oncocytoma
content: Sections show a well-demarcated tumor composed of solid nests of tumor cells in a hyalinized stroma. The tumor cells are uniform with polygonal shape, featuring eosinophilic and granular cytoplasm, and uniform round nuclei. Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are diffusely positive for CD117, focally positive for CD10 but not S-100. Hale colloidal iron stain is negative.

title: Oncocytoma (with degenerative atypia)
content: The tumor is composed of cells with abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. Focally, cells are arranged in nests in a fibromyxoid stroma. In this area the well-preserved nuclei that with fine chromatin are uniformly round with variably sized nucleoli. However, the majority of cells have prominent atypia, yet the atypia appears degenerative nuclear atypia with multinucleation, pseudoinclusions, and indistinct smudgy chromatin. Mitotic figures and necrosis are absent.

title: Papillary RCC
content: The tumor is composed of large, round to polygonal cells with abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm. Architecturally, there are numerous papillary fronds lined by multilayered columnar granular eosinophilic/oncocytic cells. The nuclei are relatively round with prominent nucleoli. Numerous foamy histiocytes occupy the papillary fibrovascular cores. Hemosiderin deposition is present in some of the histiocytes as well in some of the epithelium.

Pheochromocytoma

and being positive for synaptophysin ///. No necrosis is noted. Mitosis is
hardly identified. The sustentacular cells are highlighted by S-100.  

 Life-long follow-up is recommended.

KIDNEY

Simple cyst
Section shows a cystic lesion with thin fibrous wall, focally lined by flattened epithelial cells. No malignancy is observed.
Atrophy

Sections show renal parenchyma exhibiting extensive glomerulosclerosis and tubular thyroidization, accompanied by multiple cysts lined by bland-looking, flattened or cuboidal epithelium. The ureter is unremarkable.
RCC
Sections show a tumor composed of clear cells with distinct cell membrane, separated by delicate vascular network arranged in nesting and (focal papillary patterns.)

title: Urothelial dysplasia
content:
Urothelial dysplasia.

Microscopic description:
Section shows urothelial dysplasia in which appreciable cytologic atypia and architectural changes are present but not enough to classify it as carcinoma in situ.
Staining for CK20 is negative. P53 shows no overexpression.

title: Urothelial dysplasia with early papillary formation.
content:
Urothelial dysplasia with early papillary formation.

MICROSCOPIC FINDINGS:
Section shows urothelial dysplasia in which appreciable
cytologic atypia and architectural changes are present but
not enough to classify it as carcinoma in situ. Early
papillary formation is seen.
Staining for CK20 is negative. P53 is focally positive.

title: Urothelial papilloma
content:
Urothelial papilloma.
MICROSCOPIC FINDINGS:
The section shows slender fibrovascular cores lined by normal urothelium with prominent umbrella cell layer highlighted by CK20. P53 shows wild type staining.

title: varicocele
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Left internal spermatic vein, high ligation ---
varicocele.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of a piece of tissue measuring _____ cm.
All for section.

Microscopic description:
Section shows tortuous and dilated veins with narrowing of the lumens, and invagination of intima.

title: a. Scanty endometrial tissue with suppurative exudates
content:
a. Scanty endometrial tissue with suppurative exudates
b. Suggestive of actinomycosis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows scanty endometrial glandular and stromal
tissue with heavy collections of neutrophilic exudate and
fibrinoid deposition. Presence of a few bacterial colonies
(sulphur granules) found at the center of the inflammatory
reaction, composed of basophilic radiating filaments are
seen, suggestive of actinomycosis.

title: acute and chronic cervicitis. [CAI]
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, cervix, biopsy --- Acute and chronic cervicitis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows ecto- and endocervical tissue with subepithelial infiltrate of mixed inflammatory cells. Intraepithelial neutrophilic infiltrate is also seen. No malignancy is observed.

title: acute chorioamnionitis.
content:
acute chorioamnionitis.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections of the placenta shows neutrophils aggregating in
the subchorionoic fibrin, and some within connective tissue
layer of the chorion and amnion. (Maternal response, stage
2, intermediate). Scattered neutrophils within umbilical
vessel wall and abundant neutrophils located at the
periphery of the umbilical cord are seen. (Fetal response,
stage 1, early) No fungal element is demonstrated by PAS
stain.

Blaustein p1472

title: adenocarcinoma. favor CCC
content:
adenocarcinoma. see NOTE.

Microscopic description:
Section shows variably-sized tubules with intraluminal dense
eosinophilic secretions, composed of tumor cells including
some hobnail cells with moderate to marked nuclear atypia
and abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm.

NOTE: Based on the morphology and the immunophenotypes
(performed on S1101519C), it is an adenocarcinoma, most
likely clear cell carcinoma. However, a definite diagnosis
can only be made on the subsequent larger specimen, as the
tumor is not fully sampled.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: Adenomatoid tumor
content:
adenomatoid tumor.

Microscopic description: 
There are multiple irregular spaces lined by flattened to cuboidal cells scattered between the myometrial bundles. These cells are positive for calretinin, suggesting an adenomatoid tumor.

title: adenomyoma.
content:
adenomyoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows polypoid tissue composed of an admixture of morphologically benign endometrioid-type glands, endometrioid-type stroma, and smooth muscle.
Immunohistochemically, the endometrioid-type stromal component is positive with CD10, and the smooth muscle component positive with desmin.

title: adult granulosa cell tumor.
content:
adult granulosa cell tumor.

Microscopic description:
The tumor invades the serosal and subserosal layer of
small intestine, and is composed neoplastic cells bearing
scant cytoplasm with round to oval nuclei, arranged in
solid, trabecular and insular patterns. Nulear grooves are
seen. Call-Exner bodies are also noted.

title: AIS HPV-associated.
content:
adenocarcinoma in situ (AIS), HPV-associated.

Microscopic description:
Section shows cervical tissue revealing adenocarcinoma in situ, with depleted mucin, nuclear hyperchromasia, apoptosis, and mitoses.
By immunohistochemistry, the dysplastic glandular cells are diffusely positive for p16 with high Ki-67 proliferation index.

title: At least complex atypical hyperplasia, grade 1 adenocarcinoma cannot be excluded
content:
Section shows endometrial tissue exhibiting complex hyperplasia featuring marked glandular complexity and crowding, with additional finding of cellular atypia.  

Small foci of confluent, haphazard glandular pattern with merging of glnads and interconnecting gland lumens are seen. Grade 1 adenocarcinoma cannot be excluded.

title: Atrophy
content:
Atrophy.

Microscopic description:
Section shows cervical tissue in which the squamous epithelium shows atrophic change. The squamous cells are hyperchromatic but uniform and evenly spaced without mitotic activity. Immunohistochemically, p16 is negative. The Ki-67 index shows no significant increase.

title: Atypical adenofibromatous tummor
content:
Section shows a polypoid lesion featuring cleft-like glands and polypoid stromal 
projections lined by benign endocervical epithelium. The underlying stroma is composed
 of uniform fibrous cells with presence of one mitotic figure, and focal periglandular 
cuffing. 

Immunohistochemically, the stromal cells are positive for CD10 and SMA, with 
periglandular condenstion highlighted by immunostains. 

Taken together, this is an atypical adenofibromatous tumor which shows a predominant 
features of adenofibroma with some worrisome features including periglandular cuffing 
and detectable mitotic activity. A hysterectomy is recommended to rule out the 
presence of abnormal areas indicative of adenosarcoma.

title: Section shows endometrial tissue revealing atypical hyperplasia, which is charac
content:
Section shows endometrial tissue revealing atypical hyperplasia, which is characterized by glandular crowding with little intervening stroma and cellular atypia. Squamous morules are also seen.  

A well-differentiated adenocarcinoma cannot be excluded.  Clinical correlation is highly recommended.

title: Bartholin cyst.
content:
Bartholin cyst.              

Microscopic description:
Section shows cystic spaces lined by transitional, low cuboidal, or mucinous epithelium, with normal mucus glands adjacent to the cyst.

title: Breakdown (&太碎無法評估)
content:
Blood clots with scanty endometrial tissue

Microscopic description:
It shows blood clot and fragmented endometrial tissue with stromal breakdown. The glandular density cannot be evaluated precisely due to fragmentation of specimen.

Blood clots with scanty endometrial tissue
Microscopic description:
Section shows blood clot and fragmented endometrial tissue with stromal cells aggregating into packed balls, separating from the glands. The glandular density cannot be evaluated due to fragmentation of specimen.

title: carcinosarcoma MMMT
content:
Carcinosarcoma (malignant mixed Mullerian tumor).

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a tumor composed of a high-grade carcinoma,
which is difficult to subclassify, and homologous stromal
component. Squamous differentiation is also seen. The
endometrium shows atrophic change and is not involved by
tumor. Adenomyosis is identified adjacent to the tumor, and
is partially involved by tumor.

(Carcinosarcoma is generally considered to be high grade
and it is not recommended to assign a histologic grade)

title: Careful monitoring / CIN2?
content:
COMMENT: The changes warrant careful monitoring due to the
focal features that raise concern for CIN2 but do not meet
the criteria.

title: cellular angiofibroma.
content:
cellular angiofibroma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a well-demarcated, polypoid tumor lined by
squamous mucosa, which is uniformly cellular composed of
bland, spindle-shaged cells admixed with mast cells, and
small to medium-sized blood vessels.

By immunohistochemistry, the tumor cells are positive for
ER, CD34 and desmin but not S100, Myogenin or myoD1.

title: chronic cervicitis with squamous metaplasia.
content:
chronic cervicitis with squamous metaplasia.

Microscopic description:
Section shows endocervical tissue with subepithelial infiltrate of chronic inflammatory cells and squamous metaplasia. No malignancy is observed.

title: Chronic endometritis
content:
Chronic endometritis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows endometrial glandular and stromal tissue with collections of neutrophils, histiocytes and scattered plasma cells.

NOTE: An endometrial polyp or sampling of endometrium overlying leiomyoma may also contain plasma cells. Please correlate with clinical symptom.

title: CIN1
content:
Section shows focal mild dysplasia of squamous epithelium confirmed by p16 and Ki-67.

The cervical tissue shows the squamous epithelium focally replaced by dysplastic cells involving the innermost one-fourth of the epithelial thickness accompanied by koilocytosis. Chronic inflammatory infiltrate in the subepithelial tissue along with squamous metaplasia is also seen.

With anti-p16 immunostain, only few superfical cells present with positive reactivity

Staining for p16 is negative. The proliferation index as determined by Ki-67 is increased confined within basal and parabasal layers.

title: CIN1_Low-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (LSIL)
content:
Low-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (LSIL) (CIN1).

Microscopic description:
Section shows parabasal hyperplasia confined to the lower
third of the epithelium, accompanied by koilocytotic atypia
of the upper layers.

Staining for p16 is negative.
The Ki67 shows increased nuclear staining confined within basal and parabasal layer.

Staining for p16 shows diffuse and strong positive over 1/3 layer of squamous epithelium.

title: CIN2
content:
High-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (HSIL) (CIN2).

Microscopic description:
The cervical tissue shows evidence of CIN2 in which the inner half of the epithelium is replaced by dysplastic cells, (which is also demonstrated by positive immunostaining for p16 and increased Ki-67 labeling index in the dysplastic cells.)
        
(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

title: CIN3
content:
High-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (HSIL) (CIN3).

Microscopic description:

Section shows full thickness immaturity of squamous epithelium, featuring irregular polarity, nuclear pleomorphism and presence of mitotic figures in the superficial layer. Atrophic change is also seen. Stainings for p16 and ki-67 support the diagnosis.
 
Microscopic description:

Section shows areas of full-layered dysplasia of squamous epithelium, featuring p16 immunoreactivity and high Ki-67 labeling index.

title: complex atypical hyperplasia
content:
complex atypical hyperplasia.

Microscopic description:
Section shows endometrial tissue exhibiting crowded aggregates of tubular or branching glands lined by columnar cells featuring focal cellular atypia with little intervening stroma.

Small foci of confluent glandular pattern with merging of glnads and interconnecting gland lumens are seen. Grade 1 adenocarcinoma cannot be excluded.

Immunohistochemically, the atypical cells are positive for ER and PR.

title: Complex hyperplasia without atypia, endometrium
content:
Complex hyperplasia without atypia

Microscopic description:
Section shows endometrial tissue revealing closely packed endometrial glands with occasional back-to-back crowding with little intervening stroma. The nuclei are uniform in size and shape without overt atypia.

title: CON - HSIL [conization]
content:
High-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (HSIL)/Carcinoma in situ (CIS). _Margin____

All tissue is submitted in 4 cassettes.
A1: 12-3 o'clock of cervix
A2:  3-6 o'clock of cervix
A3:  6-9 o'clock of cervix
A4:  9-12 o'clock of cervix

Microscopic description:
Sections of the cervix shows the entire thickness of epithelium replaced by dysplastic squamous cells with extension into the underlying endocervical glands with partial or complete replacement by the dysplastic cells. No stromal invasion is seen. The surgical margin is free of tumor.

Section taken from the endocervix is unremarkable.

Endocervical Margin: 
Ectocervical Margin: 
Microscopic description:
Histologic type: high-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion 
  (HSIL)/carcinoma in situ (CIS)
Tumor site: 
  Left superior (anterior) quadrant (12 to 3 o'clock) 
  Left inferior (posterior) quadrant (3 to 6 o'clock) 
  Right inferior (posterior) quadrant (6 to 9 o'clock)
  Right superior (anterior) quadrant (9 to 12 o'clock)
Margin status: all margins negative for HSIL.

title: condyloma acuminatum???.
content:
benign squamous epithelial lesion (see description).

Microscopic description:
Section shows a tiny tissue fragment with vague papillary structure lined by squamous epithelium focally revealing koilocytic-like change. The differential diagnosis include condyloma acuminatum, and fibroepithelial polyp, which cannot be accurately distinguished in this limited specimen.
Please correlate clinically.

title: condyloma/cervix
content:
exophytic low-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (condyloma).

Microscopic description:
Section shows parabasal hyperplasia confined to the lower third of the epithelium, accompanied by koilocytotic atypia of the upper layers. There is prominent papillary architecture in which the individual papillae are supported by a fibrovascular core.
Staining for p16 is focally positive.       
The Ki67 shows increased nuclear staining confined within basal and parabasal la    yer.

title: Confirmed, vaginal wall, colporrhaphy and repair of enterocele
content: Section shows benign squamous mucosa and submucosal tissue. No evidence of malignancy.

title: consistent with complete hydatidiform mole.
content:
consistent with complete hydatidiform mole.

Microscopic description:
Section shows hydropic villi with frequent central cisterns. Trophoblastic hyperplasia is seen. The villous stroma is myxoid with focally increased cellularity and karyorrhexis.

Immunohistochemically, the majority (>90%) of cytotrophoblasts and villous stromal cells are negative for p57, consistent with the diagnosis.

Clinical correlation including serum test for hCG is recommended.

title: consistent with IUD-associated endometritis.
content:
consistent with IUD-associated endometritis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows endometrial tissue with infiltrate of mixed
inflammatory cells including plasma cells, admixed with
Actinomytotic granules highlighted by PAS.

title: consistent with retained placenta.
content:
consistent with retained placenta.

Microscopic description:
Sections show the presence of degenerated placental tissue with mixed inflammatory cell infiltration
and focal calcification. Hemorrhage and fibrin precipitation
are present. It is consistent with retained placenta.

title: consistent with very early complete
content:
consistent with very early complete hydatidiform mole.

Microscopic description:

Sections show villi of normal size with polypoid appearance
accompanied by mild trophoblastic hyperplasia and moderate
cytological atypia. The villous stroma is myxoid with
focally increased cellularity and karyorrhexis.

Immunohistochemically, the majority (>90%) of
cytotrophoblasts and villous stromal cells are negative for
p57. Ki67 index is high in cytotrophoblast. The overall
features are consistent with very early complete
hydatidiform mole.

title: CXPO (CAI no SM) cxpocai
content:
Microscopic description:
Section shows polypoid or frond-like architecture containing columnar endocervical-type mucinous epithelium. The stroma is edematous and containing acute and chronic inflammatory cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: CXPO (CAI SM) cxpocaism
content:
Microscopic description:
Section shows polypoid or frond-like architecture containing columnar endocervical-type mucinous epithelium, which is admixed with variable amounts of squamous metaplasia. The stroma is edematous and containing acute and chronic inflammatory cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: CXPO (CI no SM) cxpoci
content:
Microscopic description:
Section shows polypoid or frond-like architecture containing columnar endocervical-type mucinous epithelium. The stroma is edematous and containing chronic inflammatory cells. 
No malignancy is observed.

title: CXPO (SM CI) cxpocism
content:
Microscopic description:
Section shows polypoid or frond-like architecture containing columnar endocervical-type mucinous epithelium, which is admixed with variable amounts of squamous metaplasia. The stroma is edematous and containing chronic inflammatory cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: CXPO tubal metaplasia
content:
Endocervical polyp.

Microscopic description:
Sections show polypoid or frond-like architecture containing
columnar endocervical-type mucinous epithelium, which is
admixed with variable amounts of tubal metaplasia. The
stroma is edematous and containing chronic inflammatory
cells.

Immunohistochemically, the linning epithelium of tubal
metaplasia is positive for vimenting and bcl-2, consistent
with the diagnosis and excluding adenocarcinoma in situ.
No malignancy is observed.

title: Cystic follicles (R/O PCOS)
content:
Cystic follicles (see NOTE)

Microscopic description:
Section shows ovarian tissue with multiple cystic follicles
lined by nonluteinized granulosa cells and an outer thicker
layer of luteinized theca interna cells. Corpora albicantia
is seen. No overt fibrosis of superficial cortex is seen.

NOTE: The findings are non-specific and not pathognomonic
for polycystic ovarian syndrome. Please correlate with
clinical features and ultrasonography.

title: decidual tissue with chronic
content:
decidual tissue with chronic
inflammation. (please see description)

content:
decidual tissue.

content:
decidualization.

Section shows endometrial tissue with a prominent decidualized stroma.

content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, endometrium, diagnostic D & C ---
decidualization. (hormone effect?)

The specimen consists of multiple pieces of tissue measuring _ gm in total.

content:
-- dedifferentiated carcinoma.
-- associated with an endometrioid carcinoma FIGO grade 2.

Section shows differentiated component which shows 
low grade endometrioid adenocarcinoma, with diffuse, sheet-like undifferentiated component.

Grade: not applicable
Dedifferentiated carcinomas are generally considered to be high grade and it is not recommended to assign a histologic grade to this tumor type

Undifferentiated uterine carcinoma

content:

title: ECC
content:

title: EM Benign endometrial tissue
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Endometrium, diagnostic D&C --- benign endometrial tissue.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of multiple pieces of tissue measuring _ gm in total.

All tissue is submitted in one cassette. 

Microscopic description:
Section shows benign endometrial tissue in ___ phase. Neither hyperplasia nor malignancy is observed.

title: EMPO
content:
Endometrial polyp.

Microscopic description:
Section shows polypoid tissue lined by endometrial
epithelium with glands set in a stroma with thick-walled
blood vessels. No malignancy is observed.

title: endometrial polyp with atypical stromal cells. see note.
content:
endometrial polyp with atypical stromal cells. see note.

Microscopic description:    
Section shows endometrial polyp focally containing stromal cells with markedly atypical symplastic-like nuclei.
Immunohistochemically, these stromal cells are positive for CD10 and focally positive for desmin but not CD68 or AE1/AE3.

Note: Although studies with follow-up are limited, behavior appears to be indolent with no recurrences or progression to malignancy reported. Follow-up is suggested.

title: Endometrial polyp with focal complex hyperplasia,
content: Section shows polypoid tissue lined by endometrial epithelium with glands set in a stroma with thick-walled blood vessels. There is focal complex hyperplasia revealing cribriform architecture without cellular atypia.

title: Endometrial polyp, infarcted.
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, cervix, polypectomy --- Endometrial polyp, infarcted.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of _ pieces of polypoid tissue measuring up to  __cm.
All for section.

Microscopic description:
Section shows polypoid tissue with prominent infarction. It is focally lined by endometrial epithelium with hypersecretory glands set in a decidualized stroma and numerous blood vessels with acute inflammatory exudate. No malignancy is observed.

title: endometrioid carcinoma (cervix)
content:
endometrioid carcinoma. (see description)

Microscopic description:
Section reveals confluent glandular structures lined by columnar epithelium with loss of intervening stroma and forming villoglandular architecture. Intraluminal necrosis is also seen.
The overall features are indicative of endometrioid carcinoma, and extension from an endometrial tumor should be taken into consideration. Please correlate with clinical presentation.

Immunohistochemical stains:
ER (Ab): Positive (80%, moderate)
PR (Ab): Positive (90%, strong)
P16: No overexpression.
Vimentin: Focally positive.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: Endometrioid carcinoma (EM)
content:
Endometrioid carcinoma.

Microscopic description:
The endometrial tissue reveals confluent glandular structures lined by columnar epithelium with loss of intervening stroma and focal cribriform architecture. Intraluminal necrosis is also seen.
There is non-squamous solid growth pattern, accounting for
around ___% of total tumorous tissue in this specimen.

1. ER (Ab): Positive / Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
2. PR (Ab): Positive /Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

title: Endometrioma
content:
Endometrioma

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
Sections shows cystic lesions with variable destruction of its lining epithelium and with heavy accumulation of hemosiderin-laden macrophages in the stroma. There are occasional endometrial glandular linings and associated stroma identified.

title: Endometriosis
content: Sections show ovarian tissue with cystic lesion lined by hemosiderin-laden macrophages and endometrial tissue.

title: Epithelioid leiomyoma
content: Section shows well-circumscribed nodule withhin myometrium  composed of epithelioid cells with appreciable eosinophilic or clear cytoplasm and being positive for desmin, SMA and CD10 (focal). Neither cellular atypism nor mitosis is observed.

title: Fibroma, ovary
content:
Fibroma.

Microscopic description:
It shows ovarian tissue with tumor composed of uniform spindle cells bearing oval to spindle shaped nuclei in a collagenous stroma. Mitosis is hardly identified (_/10HFP). No cellular atypia is discerned.

Fibroma

title: focal complex atypical hyperplasia with extensive squamous differentiation. see NOTE.
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, endometrium, diagnostic D & C ---
focal complex atypical hyperplasia with extensive squamous differentiation. see NOTE.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of multiple pieces of tissue measuring ___ gm in total.
All for section.

Microscopic description:
Section shows endometrial tissue predominantly lined by
squamoid cells with focal areas exhibiting
crowded aggregates of tubular or branching glands lined by columnar cells featuring focal cellular atypia with little intervening stroma.
Immunohistochemically, the squamoid cells are positive for ER, and p63 but not PR.

NOTE:
Because the diagnosis depends on an evaluation of the glandular component, I cannot do any better than call it focal complex atypical hyperplasia with extensive squamous differentiation.
Repeat sampling for more specimen to exclude coexistent neoplasia is recommended.

title: Focal high-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (HSIL) (CIN2).
content:
Focal high-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (HSIL) (CIN2).

Microscopic description:
The cervical tissue shows predominant LSIL with
focal evidence of CIN2 in which the inner half of the
epithelium is replaced by dysplastic cells, which is also
demonstrated by positive immunostaining for p16 and
increased Ki-67 labeling index in the dysplastic cells.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: Focal simple hyperplasia without atypia
content:
focal simple hyperplasia without atypia

Microscopic description:
Section shows endometrial glands in _early secretory phase with focal areas exhibiting simple hyperplasia characterized by glandular branching and crowding. No malignancy is observed.

title: Follicular cyst / Cystic follicle
content:
Follicular cyst / Cystic follicle

Microscopic description:
The ovarian cyst is lined by luteinized granulosa cells
overlying luteinized theca cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: FZ_ovary_fibrothecoma
content:
Pathological Diagnosis :
Ovary, __, frozen biopsy: 
Stromal tumor, with differential diagnoses of fibroma or
thecoma.

NOTE: Extensive sampling is needed to exclude malignant component. Please follow the pathology report of the subsequent permanent specimen for a definitive diagnosis.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of a piece of ovarian tumor measuring _ cm and _ gm. The fallopian tube measures __ cm (length x diameter).

title: Gestational endometrium with Arias-Stella reaction
content:
gestational endometrium with Arias-Stella reaction.

Microscopic description:
Only decidual tissues with hypersecretory glands exhibiting Arias-Stella phenomenon are seen. No villous tissue, trophoblasts or fetal products are identifiable.     

These findings are also noted in missed abortion and in ectopic pregnancies. Please correlate.

title: Gestational tissue
content: Sections show gestational tissue with discernible trophoblasts and chorionic villi as well as blood clot tissue and inflammatory exudates.

title: Gestational tissue, c/w incomplete abortion
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, content, therapeutic D & C ---
Gestational tissue, consistent with incomplete abortion.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of multiple pieces of tissue measuring __ gm in total.

Sections show blood clots tissue admixed with decidual tissue and presence of chorionic villi and trophoblasts.

title: Glandular and stromal breakdown
content:
Glandular and stromal breakdown

Microscopic description:
Section reveals mainly blood clots with some fragmented clusters of endometrial glands and condensed cluster of stromal cells. No frank malignancy is noted.

title: hadatid cyst (paratubal cyst).
content:
hadatid cyst (paratubal cyst).
