Section shows cystic lesion with thin wall lined by a
   single layer of flattened or cubodal cells.

title: Hemorrhagic corpus luteum cyst
content:
Hemorrhagic corpus luteum cyst.

Microscopic description:
Sections show blood-containing cystic lesion lined by connective tissue and theca or granulosa cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: High-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (HSIL) (CIN2)
content:
High-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (HSIL) (CIN2)

Microscopic description:
The cervical tissue shows conspicuous maturation but prominent parabasal atypia including variation in nuclear size, hyperchromasia and presence of mitotic figures. 

It is also demonstrated by positive immunostaining for p16 and
increased Ki-67 labeling index in the dysplastic cells.

title: Hydrosalpinx
content:
Hydrosalpinx.

Microscopic description:
Bilateral fallopian tubes show dilated lumen with attenuated epithelium.

title: inadequate specimen. (predominant endocervical tissue)
content:
inadequate specimen. (predominant endocervical tissue)

Microscopic description:
Section shows predominant endocervical tissue with
lymphoplasmacytic infiltration. There is few and inadequate
squamous epithelium present for evaluation. P16 is focally
positive. No significant increase of Ki-67 index is seen.

title: Leiomyoma
content:
Leiomyoma.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show bland-looking spindle-shaped cells arranged in interlacing fascicles with focal degenerative change.

title: leiomyoma with bizarre nuclei (symplastic leiomyoma)
content:
leiomyoma with bizarre nuclei (symplastic leiomyoma)

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show spindle-shaped cells arranged in interlacing
fascicles with marked degenerative change. Scattered bizarre
cells show with pseudoinclusions are seen. Neither necrosis
nor increased mitotic activity is seen.

title: Leiomyoma with infarct-type necrosis
content:
Leiomyoma with infarct-type necrosis.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show bland-looking spindle-shaped cells arranged in
interlacing fascicles with focal infarct-type necrosis.
Neither overt cellular atypia nor significantly increased mitotic activity is noted.

title: Low-grade endometrial stromal sarcoma
content:
Microscopic description:
Sections taken from the cystic tumor show tumor cells
featuring uniform, oval to fusiform nuclei with scant
cytoplasm, with a delicate arteriolar network. Foamy
histiocytes aggregate, cystic change, and focal smooth
muscle differentiation are seen. There are focal irregular
islands of tumour cells permeating the myometrium in a
tongue-like growth pattern. By immunohistochemistry, the
tumor cells are diffusely positive for CD10 and negative for
Melan-A, inhibin and AE1/AE3. Areas of smooth muscle
differentiation are positive with desmin. Calretinin shows
scattered and weak staining within individual tumor cells.
The overall features are consistent with a low-grade
endometrial stromal sarcoma.

Histologic Type: Low-grade endometrial stromal sarcoma
Tumor Size: _ cm in greatest dimension
Tumor Site: Uterine corpus, within myometrium
Lymphovascular Invasion: Present Absent
Margins Status: Negative for sarcoma / Involved
Other findings:
  Chronic cervicitis.
  Leiomyoma.
  Proliferative Secretory Inactive endometrium.
Lymph nodes: Not assessed
pTNM: pT_Nx.
TNM Descriptors: Not applicable.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: low-grade serous carcinoma.
content:
low-grade serous carcinoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows infiltrative tumor cells in papillary
arrangement in cleft-like spaces, with numerous psammoma
bodies. The tumor cells show low-grade nuclear atypia.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for
PAX8, CK7, and WT-1 but not GATA3, AMACR, TTF-1 or CK20. P53
shows wild-type staining pattern.

Taken together, this is a metastatic carcinoma, consistent
with gynecologic origin. Low-grade serous carcinoma is
mostly likely according to the histology and
immunophenotype.

title: LSIL noendo
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, cervix, biopsy 
-- low-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (LSIL) (CIN1).
-- no endocervical tissue present for evaluation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows parabasal hyperplasia confined to the lower
third of the epithelium, accompanied by koilocytotic atypia
of the upper layers. There is no endocervical or transitional zone tissue present for evaluation.
Staining for p16 is negative.
The Ki67 shows increased nuclear staining confined within basal and parabasal layer.
Staining for p16 shows diffuse and strong positive over 1/3 layer of squamous epithelium.

title: metastatic endometrioid carcinoma
content:
metastatic carcinoma, consistent with gynecological origin.

Microscopic description:
Sections show hepatic parenchyma infiltrated by complex
tubuloglandular structures lined by neoplastic columnar
cells in a desmoplastic background.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for PAX8
and ER. The morphology and immunophenotype are
consistent with metastatic carcinoma of gynecological
origin.

1. ER (Ab): Positive / Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)
2. PR (Ab): Positive /Negative (  %, weak / moderate / strong)

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

title: Mucinous cystadenoma
content:
It shows multiple cysts lined by single-layered, mucin-producing, bland looking, columnar epithelium. The fallopian tube is unremarkable.

Mucinous cystadenoma.

Gross description:
Received is a cystic mass measuring _ x _ x _ cm. It had been sectioned partially, revealing a cystic lesion with smooth outer surface and containing multiple cysts filled with viscous gelatinous fluid. 

Microscopic description:
Sections show cystic spaces lined by simple, mucin-producing, tall columnar epithelium with focal papillary fronding or crypt formation. Neither stromal invasion nor overt cellular atypia is discerned.

title: mural mesothelial hyperplasia
content:
Mural mesothelial hyperplasia.

Microscopic description:
There is proliferation of epithelial cells arranged in
cords and tubular structures within the wall adjacent to the
ovarian surface. The proliferative cells show absent or
minimal atypia with occasional intracytoplasmic vacuoles.

title: Nabothian cyst.
content:
Nabothian cyst.       

Microscopic description:    
Dilated mucin filled cyst lined by flattened mucinous epithelium without atypia.

title: no endocervix [noendo]
content:
chronic inflammation, with no endocervical tissue present
for evaluation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows ectocervical tissue only with scattered
lymphocytic infiltration. There is no endocervical or
transitional zone tissue present for evaluation.

title: NOECTO
content:
chronic inflammation, with no ectocervical tissue present for evaluation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows endocervical tissue only with scattered
lymphocytic infiltration. There is no ectocervical or
transitional zone tissue present for evaluation.

title: NOTE Cx 上皮不完整
content:
NOTE: The dysplastic epithelium is focally attenuated without surface epithelium for evaluation of degree of dysplasia. Follow-up or re-biopsy is suggested.

The fragmentation of dysplastic cells leads to difficulty of evaluation of degree of dysplasia. And the dysplastic cells are exhausted out in further sections for p16 and Ki-67 immunohistochemical analysis. HSIL cannot be excluded.

Observation with a repeat biopsy is suggested.

title: NOTE: This doesn't represent for precise stage for this
content:
NOTE: This doesn't represent for precise stage for this
  patient due to incomplete surgical staging. (eg, omentum
  not removed, and incompete lymph node dissection)

title: Ovary_ teratoma
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
_ ovary, laparoscopic oophorectomy, partial cystectomy, oophorocystectomy
--- Mature  cystic teratoma.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received is an unilocular cystic mass measuring _ x _ x _ cm and weighing _ g, and filled with sebaceous material, keratin, and hair.
Unilocular cystic mass filled with sebaceous material, keratin, and hair.

Representative sections are taken and labeled as follows:
Representative sections are taken and submitted in _ cassettes. 

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
The ovarian tumor is lined by bland-looking squamous epithelium with keratinous materials production, accompanied by skin appendages, fat, smooth muscles and cartilage in the underlying stroma. Associated foreign body reactions are  present. No immature component is observed.

The ovarian tumor is composed of mature tissues derived from three germ layers, including squamous epithelium and its adnexal structures, cartilage, smooth muscle, adipose tissue as well as thyroid glands. No evidence of immaturity or malignancy is observed.

title: Ovary_mucinous cystadenoma (1)
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Mucinous cystadenoma, _ ovary_

REFERENCE:
PREVIOUS PATHOLOGIC EXAMS:
GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received is a cystic mass measuring _ x _ x _ cm. It had been sectioned partially, revealing a cystic lesion with smooth outer surface and containing multiple cysts filled with viscous gelatinous fluid. 

Representative sections are taken and labeled as follows:

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
Sections show cystic spaces lined by simple, mucin-producing, tall columnar epithelium with focal papillary fronding or crypt formation. Neither stromal invasion nor overt cellular atypia is discerned.

title: Ovary_mucinous cystadenoma (2)
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
_ ovary, laparoscopic partial oophorocystectomy --- Mucinous cystadenoma.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
The specimen consists of one adnexal tissue composed of one segment of attached fallopian tube (_ cm in length and _ cm in diameter) and the ovary (_ x _ x _ and _ gm) which is totally replaced by one multicystic tumor. On cut, it contains mucoid fluid.

 Representative sections are taken and labeled as follows: 

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
It shows multiple cysts lined by single-layered, mucin-producing, bland looking, columnar epithelium. The fallopian tube is unremarkable.

Sections show cystic spaces lined by simple, mucin-producing, tall columnar epithelium with focal papillary fronding or crypt formation. Neither stromal invasion nor overt cellular atypia is discerned.

title: p16 negative
content: Staining for p16 is negative. The proliferation index as determined by Ki-67 shows no significant increase.

title: papillary immature squamous metaplasia (=immature condyloma, papillary low-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion).
content:
papillary immature squamous metaplasia (=immature condyloma, papillary low-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion).

Microscopic description:
Section shows papillary frond lined by squamous cells with mild atypia with low mitoses at transitional zone.
Staining for p16 is focally positive.
The Ki67 shows increased nuclear staining confined within
basal and parabasal layer.

title: Papillary squamous cell carcinoma
content:
Papillary squamous cell carcinoma

Microscopic descriptions:
Section shows papillary projections that are covered by
several layers of atypical squamous cells with marked
cellular atypia and brisk mitotic activity. Focal blurring
of the epithelial-stromal interface and loss of intervening
stroma suggestive of invasion are noted. The endocervical
glands show partial replacement by the tumor cells.

Immunohistochemically, the atypical cells are strongly and
diffusely positive for p16, consistent with the diagnosis.

title: Peritoneal inclusion cyst.
content:
Peritoneal inclusion cyst.

Microscopic description:
The cystic lesion is lined by cuboidal or flattened mesothelial cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: plexiform tumorlet
content:
Micorscopic focus of epithelioid leiomyoma is noted, which is characterized by
epithelioid tumor cells arranged in cords and nests separated by a hyalinized stroma.
This focus measures 1 mm in maximum dimension, which represents a plexiform tumorlet.

title: Products of conception (不像molar pregnancy)
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, content, D & C --- Products of conception, morphologically no evidence of hydatidiform mole.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of multiple pieces of tissue measuring  __ gm in total.
All for section.

Microscopic description:
Section shows products of conception including chorionic villi and trophoblasts. Staining for p57 is positive in villous stroma and cytotrophoblast. No hydropic villi or distinct trophoblastic hyperplasia is noted. The overall morphology and immunohistochemical study show no evidence of hydatidiform mole. Please correlate with clinical finding.

title: Products of conception (只有IT cells) [Pr03]
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, therapeutic D & C --- Products of conception.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of multiple pieces of tissue measuring ___ gm in aggregate.

All for section.

Microscopic description:
Section shows implantation site intermediate trophoblasts that infiltrate the decidua and cluster around the spiral arterioles. 
Immunohistochemically, the intermediate trophoblasts are highlighted by AE1/AE3. 
No evidence of malignancy is noted.

title: Products of conception 2 [Pr01]
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, therapeutic D & C --- Products of conception.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of multiple pieces of tissue measuring ___ gm in aggregate.

All for section.

Microscopic description:
The specimen shows decidual tissues with hypersecretory glands admixed with chorionic villi and trophoblasts. No evidence of malignancy is noted.

title: Products of conception [Pr00]
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, therapeutic D & C --- Products of conception.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of multiple pieces of tissue measuring ___ gm in aggregate.

All for section.

Microscopic description:
The specimen shows products of conception including chorionic villi and trophoblasts. No evidence of malignancy is noted.

title: Pyometra: Scanty endometrial tissue with suppurative exudates, consistent with pyometra
content:
Scanty endometrial tissue with suppurative exudates

Microscopic description:
Section shows scanty endometrial glandular and stromal tissue with heavy collections of neutrophilic exudate and fibrinoid deposition. No plasma cell is demonstrated by
CD138 immunostain.

title: Scanty atrophic endometrial tissue [atrophy]
content:
Scanty atrophic endometrial tissue.

Microscopic description:
Section shows  superficial strips or wisps of atrophic glands, with little or no stroma, admixed with cervical mucus, ectocervical or endocervical tissue. Atrophic endometrium is suggested.

title: Scanty endometrial tissue [SCANT]
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, endometrium, diagnostic D & C --- Blood clots with scanty endometrial tissue.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of multiple pieces of tissue measuring _ gm in total.
All for section.

Microscopic description:
Section shows predominant blood clots with too scanty endometrial tissue for proper histologic evaluation. Repeated study is suggested if clinically indicated.

title: SCC, MD
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, cervix, biopsy ---
squamous cell carcinoma, moderately differentiated.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of multiple pieces of tissue up to ____ cm.
All for section.

Microscopic description:
Section shows infiltrating nests of tumor cells revealing
nuclear pleomorphism and focal dyskeratosis in a
desmoplastic stroma. Associated HSIL (CIN3) is also present.
Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for p40
and p16, consistent with the diagnosis.

title: Secretory phase with fragments suggestive of endometrial polyp
content:
Secretory phase with fragments suggestive of endometrial polyp

Microscopic description:
Section shows endometrial glands and stroma in ___ phase. 
There is polypoid tissue lined by endometrial epithelium with glands set in a fibrous stroma
with thick-walled blood vessels, which is suggestive of endometrial polyp. Neither hyperplasia nor malignancy is observed.

title: Sections taken from the _ o'clock of cervix show an endocervical gland totally r
content: Sections taken from the _ o'clock of cervix show an endocervical gland totally replaced by neoplastic squamous cells without stromal invasion. The surgical margin is free of tumor.

title: Segments of fallopian tubes, right and left salpinges, tubal ligation
content:
Both segments reveal presence of normal mucosal folds and its muscular walls. The

tubal lumen are patent.

title: Seromucinous borderline tumor
content:
Sections show large papillary structures lined by endocervical type epithelial cells 
exhibiting nuclear stratification. The stroma of papillae is edematous and containing 
neutrophilic infiltrate.

title: Serous carcinoma
content:
Section shows solid sheets of tumor cells with slit-like spaces and focal complex papillary architecture bearing high-grade nuclear features.
Sections show tumor cells in soid sheets with slit-like spaces and focal papillary and cribriform arrangement. The tumor cells show enlarged and pleomorphic nuclei along with large bizarre or multinucleated forms. Numerous psammoma bodies are noted. Necrosis is prominent. Residual ovarian stroma tissue is identified in the tumor.

title: Serous cystadenofibroma.
content:
Serous cystadenofibroma.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of a piece of ovarian tissue measuring ___ cm. On cut section, it shows a unilocular cyst that contains variable acmounts of firm, nodular excrescences measuring up to ____ cm.

All for section.

Microscopic description:
Sections show cystic spaces lined by a single layer of columnar, ciliated cells with focal papillary projections composed of fibromatous stroma and covered by a single layer of epithelium. Neither stromal invasion nor overt cellular atypia is discerned.

title: Serous cystadenoma
content:
Serous cystadenoma.

Microscopic description:
Sections show cystic spaces lined by a single layer of tall, columnar, ciliated cells with focal papillary structure. Neither stromal invasion nor overt cellular atypia is discerned.

title: SIL 深度無法評估
content: The involved thickness of epithelium cannot be welhsill evaluated due to fragmentation of these atypical cells.

title: STUMP
content:
Smooth muscle tumor of uncertain malignant potential (STUMP)

Microscopic description:
Sections show spindle-shaped cells arranged in interlacing fascicles with focal presence of mild to moderate atypia and increased mitotic activity (9/10 HPF). No necrosis is identified. Close follow up is recommended.

STUMP

title: STUMP/myxoid/epithelioid/necrosis
content:
Smooth muscle tumor of uncertain malignant potential (STUMP).

Microscopic description:
Sections show spindle-shaped cells arranged in interlacing
fascicles with focal presence of mild to moderate atypia and
myxoid or epithelioid morphology. Mitotic activity is around
2 per 10 HPF. There are areas of necrosis of infarct-type.
Areas of necrosis that the type of necrosis cannot be
determined with certainty are also seen, in which the viable
lesional cells have a perivascular distribution, and with
difficulty in distinction between infarct-type and tumor
cell necrosis.
Taken together, this is a smooth muscle tumor of uncertain
malignant potential. Close follow up is recommended.

title: Tubal pregnancy
content:
Ectopic tubal pregnancy.

Microscopic description:
Villous tissue within the blood clot are seen in the tubal lume    n.

title: Tubo-ovarian abscess, TOA
content:
Tubo-ovarian abscess.

Microscopic description:
Sections show distorted structures of fallopian tube and ovarian parenchyma with a mixture of inflammatory infiltrates including neutrophils, lymphocytes, histiocytes and plasma cells, accompanied by hemorrhage and necrosis. No malignancy is observed.

title: The subtype cannot be established in curettage specimens, as the tumor is not fu
content: The subtype cannot be established in curettage specimens, as the tumor is not fully sampled.

content:

Fallopian tube, right left, salpingectomy ---

title: Uterus_myomectomy
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Uterus, myomectomy --- Leiomyoma.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
The specimen is composed of ___ pieces of grayish-tan myomatous nodules masses/ tissue measuring _ x _ x _ cm and _ gm in total. On cut sections, whorls of whitish fibrous tissue is noted.

Representative sections are submitted in __ cassettes.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show bland-looking spindle-shaped cells arranged in interlacing fascicles with focal degenerative change.

title: uVIN2, warty type
content:
High-grade squamous intraepithelial lesion (HSIL) (usual VIN2, warty type).

Microscopic description:
In the lower two thirds of the epithelium cells are crowded and lack maturation. Within the upper third of the epithelium there is cellular maturation with koilocytosis.
Ki67 is significantly increased in dysplastic cells. P16 is expressed, with a block-type pattern of staining.

title: Vestibular papilloma
content:
vestibular papilloma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows an exophytic lesion comprising an arborescence
of vascular connective tissue clothed by a multilayered
squamous epithelium. The latter is well differentiated and
shows an orderly sequence of maturation. There is no
koilocytic change nor evidence of malignancy.

title: [CPS+fungus]
content:
chronic sinusitis with chronic noninvasive fungal ball.

Microscopic description:
Section shows prominent fungal balls composed of fungal
element with sepated hyphae and yeast forms. No invasion
into mucosa or soft tissue is seen in this section.
The sinus mucosal tissue shows lymphocytic and 
eosinophilic infiltration.

Eosinophil count per high power field:

title: ___ vocal cord, endolaryngeal microsurgery ---  epithelial cyst.
content:
___ vocal cord, endolaryngeal microsurgery ---  epithelial cyst.

Microscopic description:
Section shows epithelial-type cyst and an intact surface squamous epithelium. The cyst is lined by flattened epithelium. No malignancy is observed.

title: Actinomycosis
content:
Actinomycosis characterized by club-shaped filamentous organisms in a radiating pattern is seen

Actinomyces are a part of the normal flora of the cervicofacial region, gastrointestinal tract, and urogenital tract

title: ADCC
content:
Section shows salivary gland tissue with an infiltrative tumor composed of neoplastic cells arranged in tubular and cribriform patterns, with perineural invasion. The tumor cells show angular cells mixed with oval cells. The stroma is hyalinized and contains
base membrane material. The surgical margin is involved. Immunohistochemical stains show that p63, p40 and S-100 reveals variable staining in tumor cells.

-- Tumor type: adenoid cystic carcinoma
Solid component: 
-- Tumor size: 
-- Tumor location: 
-- Tumor extension:
Tumor extension: The tumor is mainly confined within
   salivary gland parenchyma. Focal infiltrate into
   surrounding soft tissue is seen microscopically.
   Extraparenchymal extension: absent
   NOTE: Extraparenchymal extension is clinical or
   macroscopic evidence of invasion of soft tissues.
   Microscopic evidence alone does not constitute
   extraparenchymal extension for classification purposes.

-- Tumor grade: I (tubular and cribriform areas without solid area)
High-grade transformation: present/absent

-- Surgical margin: 
-- Immunohistochemical stains:

title: Allergic fungal sinusitis
content:
Allergic fungal sinusitis.
Microscopic description:
The section shows eosinophilic mucin with necrotic
eosinophils, inflammatory cells, and crystals
Some fungal elements are identified by PAS.c

title: Allergic fungal sinusitis.
content:
Microscopic description:
The sinus mucosal tissues show glandular proliferation,
marked stromal edema, lymphocytic and abundant eosinophilic
infiltration. Some mucoid and eosinophilic
exudate is noted, admixed with fungal elements, which are
also highlighted by PAS. No evidence of malignancy is noted.

title: Ameloblastoma, unicystic, mural involvement
content:
Sections show cystic spaces lined by odontogenic
epithelium with barely evidence of ameloblastic change,
however, typical ameloblastic epithelial nests or sheets can
be identified in the cystic fibrous walls that with typical
features of palisading basal cells with reverse polarity and
subnuclear vacuoles. Foci of palisading with thicken
basement membrane still can be identified. The X-ray images
further strengthen the diagnosis of unicystic ameloblastoma.

title: Basal cell adenoma (salivary gland)
content:
Sections show salivary gland tissue and one encapsulated tumor featuring proliferative
 basaloid cell in trabecular and tubular pattern with focal palisading. There is no 
evidence of invasion.

title: Branchial cleft cyst
content:
Branchial cleft cyst

Microscopic description:
Section shows a cystic lesion lined by respiratory squamous epithelium. Lymphoid stroma with  hyperplasia is noted in the cystic wall. Lobules of salivary gland acini are focally  seen. No malignancy is observed.

title: C/W inflamed cyst.
content:
Consistent with inflammatory odontogenic cyst.

Microscopic description:
Section shows diffuse infiltrate of neutrophils, and lymphoplasmacytic cells. Focal lining of stratified squamous epithelium is seen. No malignancy is observed.    
    
Fragmented bony spicules are also seen. No malignancy is observed.

consistent with inflammatory odontogenic cyst.
    
Microscopic description:
Section shows a cystic lesion focally lined by stratified squamous epithelium. There is infiltrate of neutrophils, and lymphoplasmacytic cells. No malignancy is oberved.

title: C/W sialolithiasis
content:
Consistent with sialolithiasis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows lamellated calculi consistent with sialolithiasis.

title: CAI (squamous mucosa)
content:
chronic active inflammation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows squamous mucosal tissue with acute and chronic inflammatory infiltrate in the underlying stroma.

title: cemento-osseous dysplasia / cementoma
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Benign fibroosseous lesion (see NOTE).

Microscopic description:

Section shows spicules of bone in a fibrous background.

NOTE:
Taken together, this is a benign fibroosseous lesion, which can be consistent with cemento-osseous dysplasia (cementoma). Clinical and radiographic correlation is required for the diagnosis.

title: Cholesteatoma
content:
Cholesteatoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows laminated keratin consistent with cholesteatoma.

title: CHR
content:
Chronic hypertrophic rhinitis, bilateral inferior turbinates, submucosal turbinectomy

Chronic hypertrophic rhinitis.

Microscopic description:
The turbinate tissue shows stromal edema, vascular proliferation and glandular hyperplasia as well as lymphocytic and eosinophilic cells infiltration.

Eosinophil count per high power field:

title: CHR NSD
content:
Chronic rhinitis with polypoid change and clinically nasal septal deviation.

Microscopic description:
Section of the turbinates reveal marked stromal edema, vascular proliferation and glandular hyperplasia as well as lymphocytic and eosinophilic cells infiltration.
Strip of hyaline cartilage is consistent with septal cartilage.

Eosinophil count per high power field:

title: CHR NSD & CPS
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
1. Chronic rhinitis with polypoid change and clinically nasal septal deviation, bilateral inferior turbinates and nasal septum, septomeatoplasty
2. Chronic sinusitis with polypoid change, bilateral paranasal sinuses, multiple sinusectomy

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
1. The specimen is composed of ___ fragments of turbinate tissue measuring up to ___ x
   ___ x ___ cm, ___ fragments of bony tissue measuring up to ___ x ___ x ___ cm, and
   ___ fragment of septal cartilage measuring ___ x ___ x ___ cm. (RA)

2. The specimen is composed of multiple pieces of soft tissue measuring up to ___ x ___
   x ___ cm and multiple pieces of bony tissue measuring up to ___ x ___ x ___ cm. (RB)

Representative sections are submitted in two cassettes.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
1. Section of the turbinates reveal marked stromal edema, vascular proliferation and
   glandular hyperplasia as well as lymphocytic and eosinophilic cells infiltration.
   No evidence of malignancy.
   Strip of hyaline cartilage is consistent with septal cartilage.

2. The sinus mucosal tissue shows glandular proliferation, marked stromal edema, lymphocytic
   and eosinophilic inflammation. No evidence of malignancy.

Eosinophil count per high power field:

title: chronic active
content:
chronic active tonsillitis

Microscopic description:
The lymphoid tissues exhibit increased numbers of lymphoid
follicles and germinal center formation, associated with
stromal congestion and plasma cell infiltration,
suppurative inflammation of the epithelial crypts, and
focal epithelial ulceration. No evidence of malignancy is
observed.

title: Chronic rhinitis with polypoid change and clinically nasal septal deviation, bilateral inferior turbinates and nasal septum, septomeatoplasty
content:
Section of the turbinates reveal marked stromal edema, vascular proliferation and 
glandular hyperplasia as well as lymphocytic and eosinophilic cells infiltration. No 
evidence of malignancy. 
Eosinophil count per high power field: 

Strip of hyaline cartilage is consistent with septal cartilage.

title: chronic sialadenitis
content:
chronic sialadenitis.

Microscopic description:
It shows lobules of salivary acini with interstitial fibrosis and marked lymphoplasmacytic infiltration featuring lymphoid follicles formation and leading to atrophy and loss of acini. Microliths are also noted.

title: Chronic sialadenitis with Sialolithiasis (r/o IgG4-related disease)
content:
Sections show lobules of salivary acini with interstitial fibrosis and marked 
lymphoplasmacytic infiltration as well as occasional neutrophils and eosinophils, 

leading to atrophy and loss of acini. Microliths are also noted. No malignancy is 
observed. There is no elevated IgG4:IgG ratio of plasma cells in this tissue as 
demonstrated by immunohistochemical stains, excluding IgG4-related disease.

Four lymph nodes are seen with preserved nodal architecture.

title: Chronic tonsillitis
content:
Chronic tonsillitis

Microscopic description:
The lymphoid tissues exhibit increased numbers of lymphoid follicles and germinal center formation, associated with stromal congestion and plasma cell infiltration, fibrinopurulent collection in the epithelial crypts, and focal epithelial ulceration. No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: COM
content:
Consistent with chronic otitis media.

Microscopic description:
The covering squamous epithelium is eroded focally and the
underlying fibrous tissue shows chronic inflammation,
fibrosis and vascular dilation.

title: COM+cholesteatoma
content:
right/left middle ear, tympanoplasty with mastoidectomy --- 
chronic otitis media with cholesteatoma

Microscopic description:
The section shows soft tissue with chronic inflammation and laminated keratin consistent with chronic otitis media with cholesteatoma.

title: Confirmed, adenoids, adenoidectomy
content:
Confirmed.

Microscopic description:
It shows benign epithelium and lymphoid stroma.

title: Consistent with auricular deformity
content: Section shows fibrosis with granulation tissue proliferation and hyaline cartilage. No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: consistent with cholesteatoma.
content:
consistent with cholesteatoma.

Microscopic description:
The section shows laminated keratin consistent with cholesteatoma.

title: consistent with hordeolum. [針眼]
content:
consistent with hordeolum.

Microscopic description:
Section shows granulation tissue with mixed inflammatory cells infiltrate as well as aggregates of multinucleated giant cells.

title: consistent with impacted tooth.
content:
consistent with impacted tooth.

Microscopic description:
Section shows mature tubular dentin and enamel matrix consistent with impacted tooth.

title: consistent with nasopalatine duct cyst, inflamed.
content:
consistent with nasopalatine duct cyst, inflamed.

Microscopic description:
Section shows cystic lesion with diffuse infiltrate of neutrophils, and lymphoplasmacytic cells. Focal lining of
stratified squamous epithelium is seen. Fragmented bony spicules are also seen. The findings are are consistent but not pathognomonic for nasopalatine duct cyst. Clinico-radiological correlation is mandatory for the diagnosis. No malignancy is observed.

title: consistent with odontogenic cyst.
content:
consistent with odontogenic cyst.

Microscopic description:
Section shows cystic space lined by stratified squamous epithelium. 
Odontogenic epithelial rests are seen within the cystic wall. No malignancy is observed.

title: Consistent with preauricular fistula.
content:
Consistent with preauricular fistula.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a fistulous tract lined by squamous epithelium containing keratin material. Granulation tissue with acute and chronic inflammatory infiltrates is also observed.

title: Consistent with pterygium
content: It shows benign epithelium with subepithlial solar elastosis and vascular congestion.

title: Correlation with serologic studies

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for synaptophysin and TTF-1 but not thyroglobulin.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: salivary gland neoplasm, not further specified (with myoepithelial differentiation)

Microscopic description:
Section shows squamous mucosal tissue with a separate tumor
fragment composed of both ductal and myoepithelial cells
which are highlighted by CK7, S100, SMMHC, p63, and
The Ki-67 index is low. No mucus cell is demonstrated by mucicarmine stain.

The immunohistochemical profile suggests salivary gland neoplasm with myoepithelial differentiation, including pleomorphic adenoma, myoepithelial neoplasm, adenoid cystic carcinoma, epi-myoepithelial carcinoma, and polymorphous low-grade adenocarcinoma. There is substantial overlap in the immunohistochemical profile among these tumors.
Fragment contains entirely of lesional cells without surrounding tissue to evaluate for invasion.
Conservative but complete excision is recommended for a definite diagnosis.

pleomorphic adenoma should be considered.

title: Subacute granulomatous thyroiditis (de Quervain thyroiditis) (thyroid)
content:
Sections show focally destroyed thyroid follicles by marked inflammatory infiltrate 
which is composed of lymphocytes, plasma cells, neutrophils, and epithelioid 
histiocytes. Granulomas containing multinucleated giant cells are seen both within the

thyroid follicles and interfollicular regions. There is also interfollicular fibrosis.

Immunohistochemically, IgG highlights scattered plasma cells without increased 
IgG4/IgG ratio as determined by immunostains, excluding IgG4 related disease. 
There are no mycobacterial microorganisms as demonstrated by acid fast, and 

Auramine-Rhodamine stains. No fungus is demonstrated by GMS and PAS. 

Taken together, the overall features are compatible with subacute granulomatous 
thyroiditis (de Quervain thyroiditis). Four lymph nodes are identified with preserved 

nodal architecture.

title: The covering respiratory epithelium shows squamous metaplasia and appears to be
content: The covering respiratory epithelium shows squamous metaplasia and appears to be intact and lacks any cellular atypia. The underlying lymphoid stroma shows proliferation of lymphoid tissue with germinal center formation. No evidence of tumor cell infiltration is demonstrated by AE1/AE3.

title: Thyroid
content:
Sections of the cystic mass of the right lobe of thyroid reveal a cystic wall with fibrosis and inflammatory exudates. No papillary tumor is left in the sections. The frozen section (2015-04-2161) is reviewed, and it shows a small focus of papillary carcinoma. The following histologic details are defined with incorporation of the findings of the frozen section.

Consistent with Graves disease
Sections show hyperplastic follicles with papillary infolding, lined by cuboidal epithelium with uniform nuclei and prominent scalloping of the colloid. The stroma contains aggregates of lymphoid tissue with focal germinal center formation. The clinical and pathologic features are consistent with Graves disease. No malignancy is observed.
Anti-Tg _ IU/mL (↑), Anti-TPO _.1 IU/mL (↑), TSH _ uIU/mL (↓), T3 _ ng/dl (↑), T4 _ ug/dl (nl), Thyroglobulin _ ng/mL (nl)
Diffuse hyperplasia, parathyroid gland
Sections reveal parathyroid tissues with hyperplastic change. There are secondary changes including calcifications, cholesterol clefts formation and presence of hemosiderin-laden macrophages. No evidence of malignancy is discerned.

title: Thyroid ITCs
content:
NOTE:
Isolated tumor cells (ITCs) were identified in one lymph
node. However, there is no definitive guideline on whether
ITCs should be classified as pN0(i+), so in this case,
classification as pN1a is applied. Based on a few studies,
like micrometastasis, ITCs do not appear to carry a higher
risk of locoregional recurrence compared to node-negative
patients.

title: Thyroid, follicular adenoma
content:
follicular adenoma.

Microscopic description:
The histologic findings reveal a thinly encapsulated tumor, which architecturally and cytologically is different from the surrounding compressed gland. It is composed of crowded follicles with diminished colloid. No vascular or capsular invasion as to
suggest malignancy is observed.

Sections show a thinly encapsulated tumor composed predominantly of crowded follicles lined by uniform cuboid cells with diminished colloid, arranged in microfollicular, trabecular and organoid patterns. No vascular or capsular invasion as to suggest malignancy is observed.

cellular debris. Focal mucinous metaplasia is seen. No malignancy is observed.

title: Acute myeloid leukemia without differentiation (AML, M1, see Note).
content:
Acute myeloid leukemia without differentiation (AML, M1, see Note).

Microscopic Examination:
The bone marrow shows hypercellularity with hemopoietic
components accounting for 90-95% of the marrow space, with
decrease of normal hemopoietic element. There is diffuse
infiltration of blasts accounting for more than 90% of all
nucleated cells. Megakaryocytes are depleted. No
myelofibrosis is found.
Immunohistochemically, the blastic cells are positive for
CD34 immunostain, focally positive for MPO, and negative for
CD163 and CD71.
The BM aspiration also reveals hypercellular marrow with
dense blastic infiltration accounting for around 90% of all
nucleated cells. Cytoplasmic azurophilic granules and Auer
rods are occasionally found.
Promyelocytes and more mature cells are less than 10%.

Note: The classification of this AML is M1 based on the FAB
scheme but therapy-related AML (t-AML) associated with pre-
existing chemotherapy for breast carcinoma should be
considered.

title: Aplastic anemia.
content:
Aplastic anemia.

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
The bone marrow shows profound hypocellularity with
predominant adipose cells. There is marked decrease of
hemopoietic element (empty marrow).
Megakaryocytes are depleted.
Few lymphoplasma cells and erythroid colonies are present.
Neither dysplasia, nor evidence of excess of blast or
myelofibrosis is noted.

The BM aspiration is dry tapping.

Taken together the clinical & hematological features,
aplastic anemia is considered.

title: BM_Hypocellular marrow without lymphoma involvement.
content:
Hypocellular marrow without lymphoma involvement.

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
The bone marrow shows hypocellularity with hemopoietic
components accounting for about 30% of the marrow space,
and an M/E ratio of 3 : 1.
The morphology, maturation process and distribution of the
three lineages are within normal limits.
Megakaryocytes are decreased in quantity but normal in
morphology. Neither dysplasia, nor evidence of excess of
blast or myelofibrosis is noted. No atypical lymphoid cell
is identified, demonstrated by CD20 immunostain.
The BM smear shows normocellularity (30-40%) with a normal
M/E ratio. No atypical lymphocyte is noted.

title: BM_MM
content:
Plasma cell myeloma, histologic stage III.

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
The bone marrow shows hypocellularity with hemopoietic components accounting for about 40% of the marrow space,
with depletion of normal hemopoietic elements. Megakaryocytes are depleted in this specimen.
There is dense infiltration and aggregation of plasma cells, which are positive for CD138. CD3 and CD20 immunostains
reveal no other T-cell or B-cell component. Lambda light chain restriction is further identified.
Neither dysplasia, nor evidence of excess of blast or myelofibrosis is noted.
The BM smear shows normocellularity (~40%) with a dense plasmacytic infiltrate accounting for 70-80% of all
nucleated cells. Together with the clinical features, a plasma cell myeloma with lambda M protein is considered.

title: BM_MM (孔)
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Bone marrow, ___ iliac crest, biopsy --- Plasma cell myeloma.

Microscopic Examination:
The bone marrow tissue shows nearly normocellularity with hemopoietic components accounting for around 30-40% of the
marrow space, with decreased normal hemopoietic elements. Megakaryocytes are normal in quantity but atypical in
morphology. There is interstitial infiltration of plasma cells (40-50 % ) with variable maturation highlighted by
CD138 stain, which show lambda light chain restriction on kappa and lambda stains. There is minimal infiltration of
CD3-positive and CD20-positive lymphocytes. The BM aspiration also reveals increased plasma cells (30-
40%) with variable maturation and flame cell features, which are characterized by irregular cytoplasmic projections
with purple-red staining, consistent with IgA monoclonal gammopathy.

Ancillary studies: Kappa, CD20, CD3, Lambda, CD138.
