title: Burkitt lymphoma
content:
Sections show Burkitt lymphoma mainly involving from mucosa
into the nonperitonealized perimuscular tissue (mesentery)
with focal ulceration. The tumor cells are monomorhpic
medium-sized cells with non-cleaved round nuclei, coarse
chromatin and occasionally prominent nucleoli. Mitosis is
frequently seen. Bilateral section margins are free from
tumor invasion. Dissected lymph nodes show positive for
tumor involvement.

Immunohistochemically, these cells are diffusely positive
for CD20, CD10, bcl-6 and c-MYC, focally and weakly positive
for bcl-2, but negative for CD3, CD5, cyclin D1 and MUM-1.
The Ki-67 proliferation index is around 100%. EBER in situ
hybridization is negative.

ref. S0803351

title: Castleman disease
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Castleman disease, hyalin-vascular type, left neck lymph nodes, excision

MICROSCOPIC FINDINGS:
Section shows nodal structure with increased lymphoid follicles with thickened mantle
zones, small germinal centers and focal hyaline deposit. Increased number of vessels
in interfollicular spaces is seen. Some plasma cells are seen within interfollicular
regions.

Immunohistochemically, CD3, CD5, CD20, CD79a and CD43 demonstrate normal distribution
of T and B lymphocytes. The lymphoid cells of germinal center are highlighted by CD10
and bcl-6, and are negative for bcl-2. Stainings for CD21 reveal the follicular
dendritic meshworks. Staining for kappa and lambda show a polytypic pattern of plasma
cells within interfollicular region. IgD highlights the thickened mantle zone, which
is negative for IgM. The proliferation index as determined by Ki-67 is high in the
germinal centers and low in the interfollicular areas. Staining for cyclin D1 is
negative.

In conclusion, this is Castleman disease, hyalin-vascular type,
without evidence of malignancy.

title: CHL_lymphocyte-rich
content:
Sections show a lymph node partially effaced by paracortical expansion by singly 
scattered large atypical lymphoid cells, some with bi-nucleation, in a background of 
small lymphocytes with some residual lymphoid follicles. These atypical large cells 

show huge nuclei with prominent eosinophilic nucleoli corresponding to Hodgkin and 
Reed-Sternberg cells. Some mummified cells are also present. 

Immunohistochemically, these large cells display membrane and perinuclear dot 
positivity for CD30 and CD15 (focal), and nuclear positivity for MUM1 and Pax5 (weak). 
Stainings for CD3, CD20 and CD45 are all negative. CD21 highlights the residual 

follicular dendritic meshworks. 

Taken together, this is a classical Hodgkin lymphoma, lymphocyte-rich subtype.

title: Chronic idiopathic myelofibrosis with extramedullary
content: Sections show that the spleen is totally altered by extramedullary hematopoiesis with all three lineages of hematopoietic cells within the splenic cords and sinuses. There is an area of prominent fibrosis corresponding to the grossly firm and dark brownish mass.

title: Chronic passive congestion, consistent with clinically hypersplenism [Spleen]
content:
Chronic passive congestion, consistent with clinically hypersplenism

Microscopic description:
Sections show expanded and hypocellular red pulp  with
dilated sinuses and areas of hemorrhage. The white pulp is
inconspicuous.

title: Classic Hodgkin lymphoma, mixed cellularity subtype.
content:
Classic Hodgkin lymphoma, mixed cellularity subtype.

Microscopic description:
Sections show four lymph nodes and other fragmented nodal tissue effaced by singly scattered large atypical lymphoid cells, some with bi-nucleation, in a background of small lymphocytes, plasma cells and histiocytes. These atypical large cells show huge nuclei with prominent eosinophilic nucleoli corresponding to Hodgkin and Reed-Sternberg cells. Some mummified cells are also present.

Immunohistochemically, these large cells display membrane
and perinuclear dot positivity for CD30 and CD15, and are positive for MUM1 and weakly positive for Pax5.
Stainings for ALK, CD3, CD20 and CD45 are negative.
In situ hybridization for EBV-encoded RNA (EBER) is negative. Only few fibrous band is demonstrate by Masson Trichrome stain.

Taken together, this is a classical Hodgkin lymphoma, mixed
cellularity subtype.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: consistent with MGUS
content:
Hypocellular marrow without evidence of myeloma.

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
The bone marrow shows hypocellularity with hemopoietic components accounting for about 10% of the marrow space, and an M/E ratio of 1:1. The morphology, maturation process and distribution of the three lineages are within normal limits. Megakaryocytes are decreased in quantity and atypia in morphology.

There is interstitial infiltration of plasma cells accounting for about 5-10% of mononuclear cells.
No light chain restriction is identified. Neither dysplasia, nor evidence of excess of blast or
myelofibrosis is noted.

The BM aspiration also reveals hypocellular marrow. The morphology is consistent with MGUS.

Monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance (MGUS).

Microscopic description:
The bone marrow shows hypocellularity with hemopoietic components accounting for 0-30% of the marrow space, and M/E ratio of 3:1. Megakaryocytes are decreased in quantity and atypical in morphology. There are increased mature plasma cells (5-10%), highlighted by immunostain with CD138. No light chain restriction is noted by kappa and lambda stains (kappa:lambda=2-3:1). CD3 stains a few cells and CD20-positive cells are negligible. Neither dysplasia, nor evidence of excess of blast or myelofibrosis is noted. The bone marrow aspiration (C1080850) shows hypocellular marrow with scattered plasma cells. Taken together with the clinical history, monoclonal
gammopathy of undetermined significance (MGUS) is considered.

title: Dermatopathic lymphadenitis
content:
dermatopathic lymphadenitis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows paracortical aggregates of histiocytes, dendritic cells and Langerhans cells, _some containing melanin. Immunohistochemically, CD1a highlights the Langerhan cells in this area.

title: DLBCL vs BL, FISH will be performed
content:
COMMENTS: The differential diagnoses of this high-grade B-cell lymphoma include diffuse large B-cell lymphoma (DLBCL), Burkitt lymphoma (BL), and unclassifiable B-cell lymphoma with features between DLBCL and BL. 

Fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) will be performed and the results with updated diagnosis will be issued as addendum.

title: DLBCL, activated B-cell type, EBV-negative
content:
Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, activated B-cell type, EBV-negative.
Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, non-germinal center B-cell phenotype, EBV-negative.

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
Sections show total effacement of tonsillar architecture by diffuse large B-cell lymphoma composed of large atypical
lymphocytes with round nuclei, single centrally located to several nucleoli and an appreciable amount of clear to basophilic cytoplasm. No residual follicular dendritic
network is identified by CD21 immunostain. Immunohistochemically, these cells are positive for CD20, bcl-6, MUM-1, bcl-2 and c-MYC (focal), but negative for CD3,
CD10, cyclin D1, p40, p16 and CK. The Ki67 proliferative index is around 50%. EBER in situ hybridization is negative.

title: DLBCL, LN
content:
Section shows a nodal structure diffusely effaced by atypical lymphoid cells infiltrating the adjacent adipose tissue. These atypical lymphoid cells are large-sized with vesicular nuclei, prominent nucleoli, and abundant pale to eosinophilic cytoplasm, accompanied by increased mitotic activity.

Immunohistochemically, the atypical lymphoid cells are positive for CD20, bcl-2, c-Myc but not CD3, CD5, CD10, bcl-6, MUM-1, or cyclin D1. CD21 stain highlights the residual follicles. The proliferation index as determined by Ki-67 is around 90%.

Taken together, this is a diffuse large B-cell lymphoma of non-germinal center B-cell phenotype.

This is double expressor (bcl-2 and myc) diffuse large B-cell lymphoma of non-germinal  center B-cell phenotype. Dr. Shih-Sung Chuang concurs with the diagnosis.

Dr Shih-Sung Chuang concurs with the diagnosis.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDIES

title: DLBCL, stomach, GC type
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Stomach, antrum, endoscopic biopsy---
Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, germinal center B-cell type, EBV-negative.

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
Sections shows gastric tissue with diffuse infiltrate of atypical lymphocytes with round nuclei, prominent nucleoli and an appreciable amount of clear to basophilic cytoplasm. Residual follicular dendritic network is demonstrated by CD21 immunostain. These atypical lymphoid cells are positive for CD20, CD10, bcl-6 but negative for CD3, and cyclin D1. Around 30% of the tumor cells labeles for c-myc immunostain. The result is considered negative using the most frequently adopted cut-off value of 40% in the literature.
The Ki67 proliferative index is high up to 95%. EBER in situ hybridization is negative.

This is diffuse large B-cell lymphoma of germinal center B-cell phenotype,

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: DLBCL, stomach, non-GC
content:
Diffuse large B-cell lymphoma, stomach, biopsy

Microscopic description:
Section shows necrotic debris indicating ulceration. The gastric mucosal tissue is effaced by atypical lymphocytes in a diffuse pattern. These atypical lymphocytes are medium to large-sized with frequent mitoses. There is no lymphoepithelial lesion or H.pylori.
Immunohistochemically, these atypical lymphocytes express CD20, bcl-2, bcl-6, and MUM1 but not CD3, CD10, or cyclin D1. MYC is positive in around 40% cells, and is considered to be positive by the current criterion of 40% as the cutoff. The staining for CD21 is negative for follicular dendritic meshworks. The proliferation fraction as determined by Ki-67 is at 90%.

In situ hybridization for EBV-encoded mRNA (EBER) is negative.

This is diffuse large B-cell lymphoma of non-germinal center B-cell phenotype, and also a double expressor.

title: EBV, EBER
content: In situ hybridization for EBV-encoded RNA (EBER) is positive only in _

title: Empty marrow (see Note). suspect AA
content:
Empty marrow (see Note).

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
The bone marrow shows empty marrow with hemopoietic components accounting for <5% of the marrow space with
decrease of hemopoietic elements. Megakaryocytes are depleted. Neither dysplasia, nor evidence of excess of blast or
myelofibrosis is noted. No evidence of lymphoma/metastasis is noted.
The BM aspiration also reveals hypocellular marrow with an M/E ratio of 2-3:1. No evidence of myelodysplasia is found.

Note: If the specimen is representative, aplastic anemia should be considered. Otherwise, follow-up is recommended.

title: Extramedullary hematopoiesis (EMH)
content:
Hemopoietic cell lineages, including granulocytic cells (MPO
+), erythropoietic cells (CD71+) and megakaryocytes (CD61+), are 
noted.

title: Extramedullary hematopoiesis, spleen
content:
Sections show that the spleen is totally altered by extramedullary hematopoiesis with
all three lineages of hematopoietic cells within the splenic cords and sinuses.

title: Follicular lymphoma (LN)
content:
Sections show nodal architecture totally effaced by closely-packed follicles with extension into extranodal fat. The follicles are composed predominantly of centrocytes with irregular nuclear contour admixed with some centroblasts featuring medium- to large-sized, vesicular nuclei and distinct small nucleoli.

Immunohistochemically, the atypical lymphoid cells express _. CD21 highlights the follicular dendritic meshworks in the neoplastic follicles. The proliferation index demonstrated by Ki-67 is is around _%.

Taken together, this is a _-grade or grade _ follicular lymphoma.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDIES:

title: Follicular lymphoma, grade 1 (LN)
content:
Sections show nodal architecture totally effaced by closely-packed follicles. High
magnification of the follicles show that most lymphocytes are small centrocytes
admixed with occasional centroblasts, consistent with follicular lymphoma grade 1.

Immunohistochemically, the atypical lymphoid cells express CD10, CD20, bcl-2 and bcl-6
but not CD3, MUM-1 or cyclin D1. Immunostains for CD21, CD23 and CD35 highlight
follicular dendritic cell meshworks within neoplastic follicles. The proliferation
index demonstrated by Ki-67 is is around 30%.

Taken together, this is a low-grade or grade 1 follicular lymphoma.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDIES: CD3, CD10, CD20, CD21, CD23, CD35, bcl-2, bcl-6, Ki-67,
MUM1, cyclin D1.

Follicular lymphoma

title: Follicular lymphoma, low grade, biopsy
content: (Biopsy, low-grade FL) Section shows tissue cores composed of atypical lymphocytes in vague nodular pattern with stromal sclerosis. These atypical lymphocytes are composed predominantly of centrocytes with irregular nuclear contour admixed with some centroblasts featuring medium- to large-sized, vesicular nuclei and distinct small nucleoli. Immunohistochemically, the atypical lymphocytes are positive for CD10, CD20, bcl-2 and  bcl-6, but not CD3 or cyclin D1. CD21 highlights the follicular dendritic meshworks. The proliferation index demonstrated by Ki-67 is around 20%.  Taken together, this is a low-grade or grade 2 follicular lymphoma.

title: Hypersplenism
content:
Sections show preserved splenic architecture in most areas with markedly expanded red 
pulps, widening of splenic cords, and congestion in cords and sinuses. These findings 
are compatible with 

hypersplenism

.

title: Inadequate specimen (see Note)
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Bone marrow, iliac crest, biopsy ---
Inadequate specimen (see Note)

This case had been sent to Dr. Kung-Chao Chang at National
Cheng Kung University Hospital for further consultation

Gross description:
The specimen consists of a piece of tissue measuring
0.3x0.2x0.2 cm.

All for section after decalcification.

Microscopic description:
Section shows fibrous and bony tissue only. No marrow
component is identified.
The bone marrow smears also show dry tapping but some
hypersegmented neutrophils and mast cells are noted.

Note:
This specimen is insufficient for diagnosis. Although megaloblastic anemia might be impressed, re-biopsy is highly recommended.

Ref: S1409231
CBC/DC (114/8/24): WBC: 3200/uL; Hb: 10.6 g/dL; MCV: 104.3
fL; Platelet: 117K/uL; blast: 0; Neu: 85.0%; Lym: 10.0%.

title: Kappa, lambda (Benign/inconclusive)
content:
These plasma cells are polytypic for kappa and lambda light chains.

Kappa and lambda stains are inconclusive for the determination of light chain
restriction.

title: Kikuchi's disease (Histiocytic necrotizing lymphadenitis)
content:
Section shows fragmented lymphoid tissue with patchy areas of necrosis within the paracortex with numerous karyorrhectic nuclear debris surrounded by a mixture of mononuclear cells and histiocytes.

Immunohistochemically, stainings for CD3 and CD20 reveal normal distribution of T and B lymphocytes. CD21 highlights the follicular dendritic meshworks in lymphoid follicles. The mononuclear cells adjacent to the necrotic areas are positive for MPO. The proliferation index as determined by Ki-67 shows no significant increase.

title: Lab Reference
content: -- WBC _/uL, Band _%, Seg _%, Lym_%, Mono_%, Eosin _%, Baso _%, atypical lymphocyte _%, Hb _g/dL, PLT _/uL,

title: Lymphoid hyperplasia with clusters of epithelioid histiocytes & monocytoid lymph
content:
Lymphoid hyperplasia with clusters of epithelioid histiocytes & monocytoid lymphocytes, suggesting infectious lymphadenitis (see Note).

Microscopic description:
Sections show follicular and interfollicular hyperplasia with capsular and intra-lymphoid fibrosis. Collection of epithelioid histiocytes, encroaching upon or within the germinal centers & hyperplasia of monocytoid lymphocytes are present. Central suppurative inflammation is found in some granulomas. Expansion of histiocytes with foamy cytoplasm is also identified.

Immunohistochemically, the mixed inflammatory background is mixed positive for CD3 and CD20. CD68 and S100 highlight small granulomas and hyperplastic histiocytes. Spirochete immunostain is negative. Acid-fast, TB immunostain & PAS stains fail to reveal any specific etiology. IgG4-positive cells are few. Toxoplasma immunostain and EBER in situ hybridization are both negative.

Note: The histologic features suggest infection and toxoplasmic lymphadenitis or other pathogens should be considered first. Correlation with serologic studies is necessary.

ref: SF0800536

title: MALT lymphoma, parotid gland
content:
Mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) lymphomas

Sections show a mass composed of dense infiltrate of atypical lymphocytes infiltrating to adjacent fat, with lymphoepithelial lesion and residual salivary gland acini and 
ducts. Expanded lymphoid follicles with follicular colonization are also seen. The atypical lymphocytes are small to medium-sized with mild nuclear irregularity and
clear cytoplasm. The surrounding lymph nodes are also partially effaced by atypical lymphocytes, with focal increase of plasma cells. The adjacent salivary gland
shows lymphoepithelial sialadenitis with presence of lymphoepithelial islands.

Immunohistochemically, staining for CK (AE1/AE3) highlightes the lymphoepithelial lesion. The atypical lymphocytes are positive for CD20, but not CD3, CD21 or cyclin
D1. The CD21 staining highlights the expanded and disrupted follicular dendritic meshowarks. The proliferation fraction as determined by Ki-67 is around 10%. The
plasma cells are polytypic for both lambda and kappa light chain expression, suggesting reactive hyperplasia.

Taken together, this is mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) lymphoma with nodal involvement.

title: MALT lymphoma, stomach
content:
Mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) lymphoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows gastric mucosa infiltrated by atypical lymphocytes in a vaguely nodular
pattern. Lymphoepithelial lesions are noted focally. The atypical lymphocytes are
small to medium-sized with slightly irregular nuclei, moderately dispersed chromatin,
inconspicuous nucleoli, and pale cytoplasm.

Warthin Starry stain is negative for Helicobacter.

Immunohistochemically, these atypical lymphocytes express CD20 and bcl-2, but not CD3,
CD10, bcl-6, cyclin D1, or IgD. Staining for cytokeratin highlights the
lymphoepithelial lesions. The proliferation fraction by Ki-67 immunostaining is low.
CD21 highlights expanded and distorted follicular dendritic cell meshworks.

Taken together, this is a mucosa-associated lymphoid tissue (MALT) lymphoma.

title: Metastasis, totally effaced
content: Sections show soft tissue infiltrated by neoplastic __ cells with invasion into skeletal muscle fibers. Although no  residual lymphoid tissue is discernible, this metastatic lesion might represent a nodal metastasis with totally destroyed nodal architecture.

title: Myc
content: Around 20% of the tumor cells labeles for c-myc immunostain. The result is considered negative using the most frequently adopted cut-off value of 40% in the literature.

title: Mycosis fungoides
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
, skin, right thigh, biopsy

REFERENCE:

20200400
1. Mycosis fungoides, skin, back, upper, rash area, biopsy
2. Mycosis fungoides, skin, back, lower, brownish area, biopsy
2014031704
Mycosis fungoides, skin, back, biopsy
2013121281
1. Mycosis fungoides, skin, back, biopsy
2. Mycosis fungoides, skin, back, biopsy

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
The specimen is composed of one piece of skin tissue, measuring 0.4 x 0.3 x 0.3 cm.

All are embedded in one cassette.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show skin tissue with atypical lymphocyes infiltrate in the upper dermis
and around the blood vessels in deep dermis and superficial subcutis. The epidermis is involved by atypical lymphocytes in single cell or in small clusters and haloed lymphocytes in the basal layer. The atypical lymphocytes are small sized with slighly irregular nuclear contours.

Immunohistochemically, the atypical lymphocytes are

Taken clinical information and pathologic features together, this is consistent
with mycosis fungoides.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STAINS:

title: Myelodysplastic syndrome without excess of blasts.
content:
Myelodysplastic syndrome without excess of blasts.

Microscopic description:
The bone marrow shows hypocellularity with hemopoietic components accounting for about 30% of the marrow space, and M/E ration of 1-2: 1 on MPO staining. The morphology,
maturation process and distribution of the three lineages are dysplastic. Megakaryocytes are decreased in quantity and dysplastic in morphology on CD61 stain. No evidence of
excess of blast or myelofibrosis is noted. CD34 stain reveals no excess of blasts. The bone marrow aspiration also reveals hypocellular marrow
with myelodysplastic changes. No excess of blasts is found. 
Taken together the clinical & hematological features, myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS) without excess of blasts is considered.

title: Myeloid sarcoma
content:
Sections show _ tissue infiltrated by medium-sized, monotonous cells featuring scant cytoplasm, round or folded nuclei, fine

chromatin and small nucleoli. Necrosis and numerous mitotic figures are seen. 

Immunohistochemically, the myeloblasts are positive for CD34, MPO, CD163 and CD117. The proliferation index is high in tumor cells.

title: Myeloid sarcoma.
content:
Myeloid sarcoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows mammary tissue cores infiltrated by diffuse
sheets of rounded to oval neoplastic cells with occasional
nodular aggregates. The neoplastic cells exhibit
finely granular chromatin and scant to moderate amount of
cytoplasm.

Immunohistochemical study shows the neoplastic cells are
strongly positive for CD34 and MPO. Stainings for CD45 and
CD117 are weakly positive. Stainings for CK, CD3, and
CD20, TdT are negative.

This is myeloid sarcoma. 

Please correlate with clinical
history to exclude therapy-related myeloid neoplasm.

title: Non-M3 acute myeloid leukemia
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Non-M3 acute myeloid leukemia, bone marrow, iliac crest, biopsy

CLINICAL INFORMATION: r/o leukemia

HEMOGRAM:
WBC _K, Hb _, MCV _, Plt _K, Band 0%, Seg _%, Lym _%, Mono _%, Eos _%, Baso _%, Blast _%, NRBC _/100WBC.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
The specimen is four marrow cores measuring up to _ x _ x _ cm.
The specimen is totally embedded in one cassette after decalcification in the
Osteosoft solution. There is no fibrosis, granuloma, nor bony change.

MICROSCOPIC FINGDINGS:
The biopsy core is hypercellular with 60% cellularity and decreased normal trilineage
hematopoiesis. Myeloid cells show left-shifted maturaation with around 30% of marrow
cells are immature cells. The number of erythroblasts is decreased, while the
distribution and morphology of megakaryocytes are essentially unremarkable.

Immunohistochemical study shows that the immature cells express CD34 and CD117. CD61,
CD71 and MPO stainings highlight megakaryocytes, erythroblasts and mature myeloid
cells, respectively. These findings indicate non-M3 acute myeloid leukemia, most
likely AML-M2.

SPECIAL STAIN GROUP II:
1. Prussian blue stain is positive indicating substantial iron storage.
2. Reticulin stain shows a normal reticulin pattern without myelofibrosis.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMICAL STUDY: CD34, CD61, CD71, CD117, MPO.

title: plasma cell myeloma.
content:
plasma cell myeloma.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
The decalcified section shows fractures of bony spicules
with infiltration and aggregation of plasma cells with
variable maturation, which are highlighted by CD138 and
MUM1. Kappa light chain restriction is identified on kappa
and lambda stains.

Together with the clinical features, a plasma cell myeloma
is considered.

title: Plasmacytoma
content:
Section shows fracture of bony trabeculae infiltrated by sheets of relatively 
monotonous tumor cells featuring eccentrically located nuclei with stippled chromatin patterns and basophilic cytoplasm.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for CD20, CD79a, CD138, MUM-1, and cyclin D1 but not CD3, CD19 or AE1/AE3, and are monotypic for kappa light chain. They are negative for lambda light chain. 

Taken together, this is a plasmacytoma. Systemic survey is mandatory to rule out 

multiple myeloma.

title: Plasmacytoma, bone
content:
DIAGNOSIS: 
Plasmacytoma

Section shows bony spicules infiltrated by sheets of relatively monotonous tumor cells featuring eccentrically located nuclei with stippled chromatin patterns and basophilic cytoplasm.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells express CD138 diffusely and are monotypic for kappa /  lambda light chain. They are negative for _ light chain.

Taken together, this is a plasmacytoma. Systemic survey is mandatory to rule out multiple myeloma.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY: CD138, kappa and lambda light chains.

title: Primary cutaneous CD4-positive small/medium T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder.
content:
Primary cutaneous CD4-positive small/medium T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a relatively circumscribed lymphoproliferation in the dermis and superficial subcutaneous fat without epidermotropism. The infiltrate is in a diffuse pattern and is composed predominantly of small to medium lymphocytes, with scattered large cells.

Immunohistochemical study shows that most of the lymphocytes are T-cells expressing CD2, CD3, CD4, and BF1 but not CD8, CD10, CD20, TIA-1, PD-1, ICOS, or TCR-delta.
Scattered cells express bcl-6, CD25 and CD30. The labeling index by Ki67 is at around 20-30%. In situ hybridization for EBV is negative.

This is primary cutaneous CD4-positive small/medium T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder.

title: Primary mediastinal large B-cell lymphoma
content:
Primary mediastinal large B-cell lymphoma
Section shows atypical lymphoid cells in diffuse pattern partially surrounded by fibrosis. The atypical lymphoid cells are large-sized with pale cytoplasm and fine sclerosis compartmentalizing the tumor cells, in a background of small lymphocytes. Some Hodgkin-like cells are present. 

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for CD20, CD23, CD30, CD79a, bcl2, bcl6 (focal) and myc, MUM1, but not CD3, CD10 or cyclin D1 . AE1/AE3 highlight the thymic remnants which are infiltrated by tumor cells. , The proliferation index as determined by Ki-67 is around 50%.

title: Progressive transformation of germinal centers (PTGC)
content:
Some of the follicles show progressive transforation with extension of the mantle 
cells into the germinal centers.

title: PTCL (DDx ATLL and PTCL,NOS)
content:
The specimens show infiltration of diffuse sheets of atypical lymphoid cells within the lamina propria. These atypical lymphoid cells are medium-to large-sized with
irregular nuclei and brisk mitotic activity. Crushing artifacts are present.

Immunohistochemically, the atypical lymphocytes express __. The proliferation index as determined by Ki-67 immunostaining is around _%.

In situ hybridization for EBV-encoded mRNA (EBER) is __.

Taken together, this is consistent with a peripheral T-cell lymphoma with the differential diagnoses of adult T-cell leukemia/lymphoma and peripheral T-cell
lymphoma, not otherwise specified.

A serum test for anti-HTLV is recommended to exclude the diagnosis of adult T-cell leukemia/lymphoma.

title: PTCL, NOS
content:
Peripheral T-cell lymphoma,
not otherwise specified (please see COMMENTS)

Microscopic description:
Sections show cutaneous tissue with a diffuse atypical
lymphocytic infiltration from ulcerated epidermis to
subcutaneous layer. The infiltrate atypical lymphocytes are
medium-sized with fine chromatin, small nucleoli and high
N/C ratio, resulting a blastic appearance. Frequent mitoses
are seen and geographic necrosis is focally noted. There is
no epidermotropism.

Immunohistochemically, the atypical lymphocytes express CD3
and CD45 but not CD4, CD5, CD8, CD20, CD25, CD30, TdT, CD1a,
CD34, MPO, TIA-1, granzyme B, BF-1 or TCR-gamma. No
follicular dentritic meshwork is highlighted by CD21. The
labeling index by Ki67 is up to 80%. In situ hybridization
for EBV-encoded mRNA (EBER) is negative.

In conclusion, this is PTCL-NOS.

COMMENTS:
Although adult T-cell lymphoma is among the differentials,
it is unlikely as the tumor cells are negative for CD4 and
CD25. Please correlate with the HTLV-1 status. If the
serum is positive for anti-HTLV, this is adult T-cell
leukemia/lymphoma with unsual phenotype. If negative, this
is peripheral T-cell lymphoma, not otherwise specified
PTCL

title: Reactive hyperplasia
content:
Reactive hyperplasia.

Microscopic description:
Section shows lymph nodes revealing reactive follicular hyperplasia with polarized mantle zone and geographic-shaped germinal centers which contain light and dark zones and numerous tingible body macrophages. Paracortical hyperplasia resulting a "moth-eaten" appearance is also discerned. No evidence of granuloma or malignancy is observed.

Immunohistochemically, CD3 and CD20 demonstrate normal distribution of T and B lymphocytes.  The proliferation index as determined by Ki-67 is high in the germinal centers and low in the interfollicular areas.

The lymphoid cells of germinal center are negative for bcl-2.

Dr. Shih-Sung Chuang concurs with this diagnosis.

IgD shows attenuated or absent mantle zone around these follicles.

title: residual acute myeloid leukemia AML
content:
Empty marrow, but excess of blasts (10-20%) in bone marrow
smear (see Note).

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
The bone marrow shows empty marrow without hemopoietic
components. Neither dysplasia, nor evidence of excess of
blasts or myelofibrosis can be evaluated.
CD34 staining shows no excess of     blasts.
However, the BM aspiration reveals hypocellular marrow with
excess of blasts (10-20%).

Note: Taken together the clinical & hematological features,
residual acute myeloid leukemia is considered.

title: Section of the punch biopsy shows a diffuse lymphocytic infiltration in the derm
content:
Section of the punch biopsy shows a diffuse lymphocytic infiltration in the dermis by medium to large atypical lymphocytes, some cells seem to cluster within vascular spaces.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells express CD3, CD4, CD30, and MUM1 but not CD20,
CD56, CD68, CD162 (cutaneous leukocyte-associated antigen), TIA-1, granzyme B or EMA.
Stainings with CD31 and CD34 highlights endothelial cells, but there are no
intravascular tumor cells.

Clinical correlation is mandatory to decide whether this is lymphomatoid papulosis or primary cutaneous anaplatic large cell lymphoma.

title: Small lymphocytic lymphoma (SLL)
content:
Small lymphocytic lymphoma (SLL). See Comment.

Section reveals lymphoid tissue in which the normal nodal
architecture is effaced by diffuse proliferation of
atypical lymphoid cells with proliferation centers.
Prolymphocytes and paraimmunoblasts are present.

By immunohistochemistry, the atypical lymphoid cells are
positive for CD20, CD23, CD5, and bcl2, and negative for
CD10, and cyclin D1. EBER is negative. No follicular
dendritic meshworks are identified by CD21. Bcl6 and MUM1
are focally and weakly positive. The Ki67 proliferation
index is aound 40%.

Comment:
Correlation with clinical findings is recommended to exclude
the possibility of chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL).

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

title: 建議excision [severe crushing artifact]
content: Note: Due to severe crushing artifact, no definite diagnosis can be made. Lymph node excision is recommended if clinical lymphadenopathy is present.

title: Acute and chronic cholecystitis, ruptured.
content:
Acute and chronic cholecystitis, ruptured.

Microscopic description:
Sections reveal partial mucosal destruction and inflamed
wall that is infiltrated by numerous polymorphonuclear as
well as mononuclear cells in its lamina propia as well as
the underlying muscular coat. The wall shows edema,
hyperemia and extravasation of RBCs. Focal transmural
necrosis is seen consistent with rupture.

title: Acute hepatitis;
content:
Sections show liver tissue strips with lobular hepatocellular injury, featuring
lobular inflammation of neutrophils and lymphocytes accompanied by hepatocellular
swelling with presence of acidophilic bodies. Mild portal inflammation is noted.
Masson trichrome stain shows pericellular fibrosis as well as fibrous expansion of
some portal areas.

Immunostainings for CD3 and CD20 show no evidence of malignant lymphoma. The overall
features are consistent with acute hepatitis. Further serology test for viral loads is
suggested.

title: Adenocarcinoma
content:
Liver, echo-guide biopsy --- Adenocarcinoma, moderately to pooly differentiated. See Comment.

Comment:
Combined with the clinical history, image, tumor histopathological feature and IHC stain profile, the
possible tumor origin of pancreatobiliary system and upper gastrointestinal tract should be firstly consisdered.
Metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma can be compatible. Further clinicopathological correlation and study are
suggested.

title: adenocarcinoma (please see description).
content:
adenocarcinoma (please see description).

Microscopic description:
Section shows hepatic tissue infiltrated by tumor cells with hyperchromatic nuclei arranged as irregular glandular structures in a desmoplastic stroma.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for CK7 and CK19, but not CK20 or arginase-1. CDX2 is positive in scattered, individual tumor cells.

Taken together, this is an adenocarcinoma, of either primary or metastatic. It can be consistent with cholangiocarcinoma if the possibility of pancreatobiliary or gastrointestinal tract origin is excluded. Clinico-radiological correlation isrecommended.

title: Adenocarcinoma, primary or metastatic
content:
Adenocarcinoma, primary or metastatic

It shows infiltrative tumor cells with hyperchromatic nuclei arranged as irregular
glandular structures with presence of tumor necrosis.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for CK19, but negative for
arginase-1. Taken together, this is an adenocarcinoma, either primary or metastatic.
Detailed systemic surveys are highly recommended.

title: Adenoma
content: An adenoma with papillary / tubular pattern, lined by intestinal/ biliary / pyloric -type epithelium with low-grade dysplasia is seen.

title: Autolysis (gallbladder)
content: The gallbladder shows mucosal autolysis. No malignancy is noted.

title: C/W metastatic carcinoma
content:
metastatic carcinoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows liver tissue cores infiltrated by neoplastic cells featuring enlarged and pleomorphic nuclei with?out obvious glandular differentiation. 

Immunohistochemically, these tumor cells _.

The overall findings, taken together with clinical information, are compatible with metastatic __ adenocarcinoma. 

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: Cavernous hemangioma.
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Liver, segment ___, ____ --- Cavernous hemangioma.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received is a piece of hepatic tissue measuring __ cm and _____ gm. It had been sectioned partially when received. A tumor is seen measuring ____ cm. It is located ___ cm from surgical margin.

Representative sections are taken and labeled as follow:

Microscopic description:
Section shows hepatic tissue with a demarcated nodule composed of cystically dilated spaces lined by bland-looking
endothelial cells. Immunohistochemically, the endothelial cells are positive for CD31, confirming the diagnosis.
The non-tumor liver parenchyma shows chronic inflammatory cell infiltrate around portal area.

Sections of tumor show prominent hyalinization and sclerosis with some obliterated spaces lined by bland-looking endothelial cells as highlighted by ERG, with associated SMA-positive pericytes.

title: Cholesterosis
content:
Cholesterosis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows aggregate of foamy macrophages in lamina propria. No malignancy is observed.

title: Chronic cholecystitis with adenomyoma
content:
Chronic cholecystitis with adenomyoma

Microscopic description:
The gallbladder shows intact, chronically inflamed mucosa accompanied by formation of Rokitansky sinuses within the underlying muscular coat. Adenomyoma is seen, which is composed of branching and cystically dilated glands glands invaginating into muscular wall, accompanied by hyperplasia of smooth muscle. No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: Chronic cholecystitis with cholelithiasis (with adhered liver tissue)
content:
Chronic cholecystitis with cholelithiasis (with adhered liver tissue)
Microscopic descriotion:
The gallbladder shows intact, chronically inflamed mucosa accompanied by formation of Rokitansky sinuses within the underlying muscular coat. Adhered liver tissue is also seen. No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: Chronic follicular cholecystitis.
content:
Chronic follicular cholecystitis.

Microscopic description:
The gallbladder shows intact, chronically inflamed mucosa accompanied by formation of Rokitansky sinuses within the underlying muscular coat. Prominent lymphoid follicles with germinal centers formation are seen. Pyloric metaplasia is also noted. No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: combined hepatocellular-cholangiocarcinoma
content:
combined hepatocellular-cholangiocarcinoma.

Microscopic description:
Sections show histologic evidence of both hepatocellular and biliary differentiation by morphology, and supported by
immunohistochemistry.

title: Consistent with autoimmune hepatitis
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Consistent with autoimmune hepatitis, liver, right lobe, echo-guided random biopsy

CLINICAL INFORMATION: 
-- 2016-6-25: GOT(88 U/L), bilirubin direct(10.34 mg/dL), alkaline phosphatase(351    
              IU/L), r-GT (116.3 IU/L). 
-- AFP 2.4 ng/mL
-- Antinuclear Ab 160x(+), anto-ds DNA <0.5 IU/mL, anti-smooth muscle Ab 20x(-)
-- Anti-HCV (-), HBsAg <0.05.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
The specimen is composed of two pieces of brown tissue, measuring up to 2 x 0.1 x 0.1 
cm.

All are embedded in one cassette.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
It shows liver tissue cores revealing periportal interface activity and fibrosis as 
well as lobular inflammation accompanied by ductular proliferation and cholestasis. 
The inflammatory infiltrate includes lymphocytes, neutrophils, eosinophils, and 
histiocytes. Hepatocyte swelling and acidophilic bodies are also seen. 

Immunohistochemically, no viral inclusion is demonstrated by anti-CMV immunostain. CK7
highlights the ductular proliferation. CD31 demonstrates the sinusoidal 
capillarization 

No fungus is seen by GMS and PAS. 

The overall features are consistent with an autoimmune hepatitis. However, drug-
induced hepatitis cannot be completely excluded. Please correlate with clinical 
information.

title: Consistent with polycystic disease, liver
content:
Sections show cystic lesions lined by flattened or cuboidal biliary epithelium. 
Mononuclear inflammatory infiltrate is focally seen. The hepatic trabecular cords show
 atrophy adjacent to the cysts. Calcification is noted along the cystic wall.

title: focal nodular hyperplasia. FNH
content:
Focal nodular hyperplasia.

Microscopic description:
Sections show nodules composed of benign-looking hepatocytes arranged in plates no more than 2 cells in thickness. The central scar contains large dystrophic vessels, numerous small arterioles with a lymphocytic infiltrate. At the interface of fibrous regions, a ductular reaction is highlighted by CK19 immunostaining. Focal area shows sinusoidal dilatation with congestion. The sinusoidal endothelium shows patchy CD34 staining. GS is focally positive. Reticulin stain shows no loss of reticulin framework in tumor part.
Taken together, this is a focal nodular hyperplasia.

title: GAL focal abscess.
content:
Acute cholecystitis with focal abscess and cholelithiasis.
Acute cholecystitis with focal abscess.

Microscopic description:
Sections reveal partial mucosal destruction and inflamed wall that is infiltrated by numerous polymorphonuclear as well as mononuclear cells in its lamina propria as well as the underlying muscular coat. The wall shows edema, hyperemia, extravasation of RBCs and myofibroblastic proliferation. Focal abscess formation is seen.
No malignancy is observed.

content: Glutamine synthetase is present in the pericentral hepatocytes.

title: HCC
content:
Hepatocellular carcinoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows tissue cores containing sheets of neoplastic hepatocellular cells arranged in thick trabecular pattern with focally clear cell change. No bile ducts are seen within the tumor area.

Masson's trichrome demonstrats the fibrotic bands in non-tumorous part.
Reticulin stain shows loss of reticulin framework in tumor part.
The reticulin stain shows disruption of normal reticulin framework and thickening of the cell plates in tumorous part.
CD34 stain demonstrates diffusely increased sinusoidal capillarization in tumor, supporting the diagnosis of HCC.
CD34 stain demonstrates sinusoidal capillarization.
Tumor and non-tumor areas both show focal cytoplasmic staining for HbcAg.
The tumor cells are negative for GPC-3 and CK19. Compared with the findings between the tumor and non-tumorous part, the staining results of HSP-70 and GS are not contributory.
CK7demonstrates the normal distribution of bile ducts within portal area.

title: Liver
content:
Clinical information, Lab data
-- Viral test: HBsAg (_IU/mL), Anti-HCV ()

-- Tumor markers: AFP (_) 

-- CT (201_-_): 

HCC, non-tumor太小無法評估 2014071008
The inflammation activity of the non-tumorous liver parenchyma is difficult to be evaluated and will be overestimated, due to the fact that the tumor almost occupied the entire specimen with very little non-tumorous part left for evaluation. The non-tumorous part immediately adjacent to the tumor shows extensive hemorrhage and reactive changes.
A subcapsular wedge biopsy is note recommended to evaluate a diffuse disease such as chronic hepatitis because fibrous septa extending from the capsule into the underlying parenchyma may prompt an overestimation of fibrosis and nonspecific inflammation related to the surgical procedure may influence assessment of the grade of inflammation. Regenerative nodule
Sections show regenerative nodules with reduced and scattered portal tracts. There is focal increased cellularity, but without overt cytologic atypia. Immunohistochemically, the hepatic cells are negative for GPC-3. Staining for CD34 shows no significant increase of sinusoidal capillarization. Staining for HSP-70 shows no difference between the nodules and surrounding hepatic tissue. The reticulin stain shows focally mild disruption of normal reticulin framework with cell plates of 1-3 cells thick in the nodules.

title: Liver cirrhosis with a focus of atypical adenomatous hyperplasia (dysplastic nodule) [biopsy]
content:
Liver cirrhosis with a focus of atypical adenomatous hyperplasia (dysplastic nodule) (Please see description).

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
Section shows liver cirrhosis. A tiny focus shows mildly increased in hepatocytic nuclear size, cellularity and presence of unpaired artery. 
HSP70 and GS stains are not increased in this focus. GPC3 stain is negative. Atypical adenomatous hyperplasia/dysplastic nodule
is favored. The morphology and immunostaining markers do not reach the diagnostic criteria of heptocellular carcinoma by
these specimens. However, sampling problem is possible. Clinical correlation is recommended.

title: Necrotizing cholecystitis
content:
Necrotizing cholecystitis

Microscopic description:
The gallbladder shows hemorrhagic as well as liquefactive
necrosis of the entire wall with intense PMN infiltration
and fibrin exudation.

title: Small cell carcinoma, metastatic
content:
Small cell carcinoma, metastatic.

Microscopic description:
Section shows hepatic tissue infiltrated by sheets and nests
of neoplastic cells revealing medium sized cells with
necrosis and crush artifact. The tumor cells show finely
granular chromatin and scant cytoplasm. Nuclear molding is
also seen.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for
synaptophysin and INSM1 but not TTF-1 or p40. Together with
clinico-radiological findings, metastastic small cell
carcinoma is diagnosed.

title: Small cell carcinoma.
content:
Small cell carcinoma. See Comment.

Microscopic description:
Section reveals liver tissue infiltrated by sheets and nests
of neoplastic cells. The tumor is composed of medium-sized
cells exhibiting tumor necrosis. The neoplastic cells
display finely granular chromatin, inconspicuous nucleoli,
scant cytoplasm, and nuclear molding.
Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for
AE1/AE3, synaptophysin, and show focal positivity for
chromogranin A. The cells are negative for arginase-1.
Ki-67 shows a high proliferative index.

Comment: These findings are consistent with small cell
carcinoma. Given the location, metastatic disease should be
strongly considered.

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

title: Solitary biliary cyst, liver
content: Sections show cystic tissue lined by bland-looking cuboidal epithelium. Liver tissue is seen in its wall. No malignancy is observed.

title: Xanthogranulomatous cholecystitis with cholelithiasis, gallbladder, laparoscopic  cholecystectomy
content:
The gallbladder shows ulcerative mucosa accompanied by heavy collections of mixed  inflammatory cells and abundant foamy histiocytes with fibroblasts/myofibroblasts  proliferation. Formation of Rokitansky sinuses within the underlying muscular coat is  
also seen. No evidence of  malignancy is noted.

title: Atypical + Candida/ stroma scant
content:
a. Atypical squamous proliferative lesion (please see description)
   Candidiasis.

b. Submucous fibrosis: Cannot be evaluated due to scanty
   submucosal tissue.
   Submucosa: Present

Ancillary study for diagnosis:
Deep cut and PAS stain done. Fungal elements,
morphologically consistent with Candida spp., are found.

Microscopic description:
Section reveals proliferation of squamous epithelium with verrucous and endophytic growth patterns and parakeratosis at surface. Mild cellular atypia is noted. Intraepithelial neutrophilic infiltration, and fungal infection are seen, which is highlighted by PAS stain.

Although no definite stromal invasion can be determined, a verrucous carcinoma or well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma cannot be excluded due to scanty submucosal tissue present in this specimen.

title: Atypical squamous epithelial proliferation (Dr Yeh), biopsy
content:
Atypical squamous epithelial proliferation.

Microscopic description:
Section reveals verrucous proliferation of squamous epithelium with exophytic and endophytic growth patterns and parakeratosis at surface.
(Intraepithelial neutrophilic cells infiltration, and fungal infection are seen in them.) 

Although no definite stromal invasion can be determined, a verrucous carcinoma or well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma cannot be excluded. Wide excision of the tumors is recommended.

title: Atypical squamous proliferative lesion
content:
Atypical squamous proliferative lesion

Microscopic description:
The original and the deeper cut sections show tangential cut of mucocutaneous tissue exhibiting squamous hyperplasia with parakeratosis and mild cellular atypia. There is only little stromal element present which is insufficient for proper evaluation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows downward proliferation of squamous epithelium featuring mild cellular atypia and dyskeratosis in a mixed inflammatory background. Although no definite stromal invasion is identified, the overall features are suspicious for squamous cell carcinoma. Total excision is recommended.

title: Atypical verrucous proliferation (please see description)
content:
a. Atypical verrucous proliferation (please see description)
b. Submucous fibrosis: Cannot be evaluated due to scanty
   submucosal tissue.
   Submucosa: Present

Ancillary study for diagnosis:
Deep cut and PAS stain done. Fungal elements,
morphologically consistent with Candida spp., are found.

Microscopic description:
Section reveals verrucous proliferation of squamous
epithelium revealing endophytic growth with blunt
projections of well differentiated squamous epithelium with
deep bulbous processes and a pushing margin.

The level of extension is difficult to be evaluated in this biopsy. A verrucous carcinoma or well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma cannot be excluded. Excision of the lesion may be necessary for a precise diagnosis.

Although no definite stromal invasion can be determined, squamous cell carcinoma cannot be excluded due to scanty submucosal tissue present in this specimen. Total excision of the lesion is recommanded.

Ancillary study for diagnosis:
Deep cut and PAS stain done. No fungal element found.

title: Candida/atypia/Re-biopsy after treatment
content:
Section shows parakeratotic squamous epithelium featuring nuclear atypia and increased
mitotic activity, which may be reactive or neoplastic. There is focal ulceration of
the mucosa with granulation tissue formation and mixed inflammatory infiltrate in the
underlying stroma.

Fungal spores and hyphae within keratin layer are identified by GMS and PAS. 
Re-biopsy after treatment of infection is recommended.

title: Cellular atypia/candida/No stroma
content:
a. Squamous proliferative lesion with cellular atypia and
   candidiasis.
b. Submucous fibrosis: Cannot be evaluated.
   Submucosa: Absent.

Microscopic description:
The section shows a picture of parakeratosis and hyperplasia of squamous epithelium with intraepithelial acute and chronic inflammatory cell infiltration, foci of cellular atypia, and downward extensions, which may be reactive or neoplastic 

No adequate stromal tissue is present for evaluation of invasiveness. Many fungal filaments and yeast forms are seen on the superficial epithelium, consistent with candidiasis. Excision of the lesion may be necessary to exclude malignancy.

granulation tissue.

title: Granulomatous inflammation
content:
Granulomatous inflammation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows buccal mucosal tissue with multiple small aggregates of epithelioid histiocytes and Langerhan type giant cells, accompanied by mixed inflammatory infiltrate in the surrounding tissue. Neither necrosis nor vasculitis is seen.
No mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast and Auramine-Rhodamine stains. No fungus is identified by GMS and PAS.

The differential diagnosis include infection, foreign body reaction, orofascial granulomatosis, sarcoidosis, granulomatosis with polyangiitis, etc. Please correlate with clinical information.

title: Lichen planus
content:

Microscopic description:

Liquefactive degeneration of the basal layer of the

The above histomorphology can be consistent with oral lichen planus. Clinical correlation is recommended.

title: lichenoid lesion (please see description)
content:
lichenoid lesion (please see description)

Microscopic description:
Section shows squamous mucosal tissue with parakeratosis and
irregularlly acanthosis featuring sawtooth appearance.
Liquefactive degeneration of the basal layer of the
epithelium and subpepidermal clefts are present. Marked
subepithelial lymphoplasmacytic bandlike infiltrate is
noted. .

The above histomorphology, together with the findings in
S0800232, is consistent with oral lichenoid lesion, such as
lichen planus, mucous membrane pemphigoid, and lichenoid
reaction to drugs or dental amalgam. Clinical correlation is
recommended.

Mucocele.

title: R/O Sjogran's syndrome (suggestive of chronic sialadenitis)
content:
Focal lymphoid aggregate (please see description).

Microscopic description:
Section reveals multiple salivary tissue fragments measuring 4 mm square or more. One of them show >50 lymphocytes aggregation. There is periductal fibrosis, duct ectasia and acinar atrophy, which are features suggestive of chronic sialadenitis rather than Sjogren's syndrome. Please correlate with clinical findings and serological
autoantibodies.

epithelium, consistent with candidiasis.

title: SCIS
content: Sections show the epithelium replaced by neoplastic squamous cells that show hyperchromatism, enlargement and loss of polarity of nuclei without definite stromal invasion.

title: Sjogren syndrome
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Labial minor salivary gland, __, biopsy --- No evidence of Sjogren's syndrome

Microscopic description:
Section shows lobules of salivary gland focally infiltrated by mild degree of chronic lymphocytic infiltration, with FOCUS SCORE (FS) <1 (defined as aggregate containing at least 50 lymphocytes). The overall morphology reveals mild chronic sialoadenitis without evidence of Sjogren syndrome.
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Labial minor salivary gland, __, biopsy --- No evidence of Sjogren's syndrome

Microscopic description:
Section shows lobules of salivary gland without overt lymphocytic infiltration. The overall morphology reveals no evidence of Sjogren syndrome.

PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Labial minor salivary gland, __, biopsy --- Inadequate for evaluation of Sjogren's syndrom
The initial and deeper cutting sections show only three very tiny lobules of salivary glands, in which no inflammatory cell infitrate is seen. Overall, the specimin is inadequate for a reliable evaluation of Sjogren's syndrome.

PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Labial minor salivary gland, __, biopsy --- Suggestive of Sjogren syndrome.
Microscopic description:
These four salivary tissue fragments measure 4 mm square or more. Three of them show infiltrate of >=50 mononuclear leukocytes in the salivary glandular elements which present no remarkable change. These findings are highly compatible with clinical impression of Sjogren syndrome, with dry eye and dry mouth.

PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Labial minor salivary gland, __, biopsy --- Suggestive of Sjogren syndrome.

Microscopic description:
Section reveals salivary tissue fragments measuring 4 mm square or more. Two of them show >50 lymphocytes
aggregation. The glandular acini show no remarkable change of fibrosis or atrophy. Though the picture may be suggestive of Sjogren's syndrome, correlation with clinical findings and serological autoantibodies is needed for a definite diagnosis.

REFERENCE: Shiboski SC, et al. American College of Rheumatology classification criteria for Sjogren's syndrome: a data-driven, expert consensus approach in the Sjogren's International Collaborative Clinical Alliance Cohort. Arthritis Care Res (Hoboken). 2012 Apr;64(4):475-87.

title: Squamous epithelial hyperplasia (without atypia)
content: Section shows hyperplastic squamous epithelium with parakeratosis. No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: Squamous epithelial hyperplasia with atypia
content:
Squamous epithelial hyperplasia with atypia

Microscopic description:
It shows parakeratotic and hyperplastic squamous epithelium featuring endophytic growth pattern, focal blurring of the lower border and focally nuclear atypia, which may be reactive or neoplasia. Both intraepithelial and subepithelial inflammatory cell infiltration are seen. No fungus is identified by stainings for GMS and PAS. Excision of the lesion may be necessary for precise diagnosis.

title: Squamous papilloma
content:
squamous papilloma.

Microscopic description:
The lesion comprises an arborescence of vascular connective tissue clothed by a multilayered squamous epithelium. The latter is well differentiated and shows an orderly sequence of maturation. There is no evidence of malignancy.

title: suggestive of chronic sialadenitis (please see description).
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Labial minor salivary gland, biopsy ---
suggestive of chronic sialadenitis (please see description).

Microscopic description:
Section reveals multiple salivary tissue fragments measuring 4 mm square or more. There is lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate in a periductal distribution, along with focal fibrosis, duct ectasia and acinar atrophy, which are features suggestive of chronic sialadenitis rather than Sjogren's syndrome. Please correlate with clinical findings and serological autoantibodies.

title: Suspicious for SCC (建議excision)
content:
Suspicious for squamous cell carcinoma.

Microscopic description:
It shows tangentially cut mucocutaneous tissue with hyperkeratosis and hyperplasia of squamous epithelium featuring mild nuclear atypia, endophytic growth pattern and focal blurring of lower border. Underlying invasive carcinoma cannot be excluded. Total excision of lesion should be taken into consideration according clinical picture.

title: Suspicious for SCC (建議re-biopsy)
content:
Suspicious for squamous cell carcinoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows verrucous proliferation of squamous epithelium featuring mild cellular atypia, with endophytic growth into submucosal area. Although no definite stromal invasion is identified, the overall features are suspicious for squamous cell carcinoma. Rebiopsy is recommended.

Suspicious for squamous cell carcinoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows tangential cut of atypical squamous epithelium with dyskeratosis, which is highly suspicious for squamous cell carcinoma. Re-biopsy to obtain a larger specimen is recommeneded.

title: Ulcer with atypia and fungal infection (建議re-biopsy)
content:
Ulcer with cellular atypia and fungal infection.

Microscopic description:
The original and deeper-cut sections show stratified squamous mucosa shows partial destruction with heavy intraepithelial neutrophilic cell infiltrate. Separate fragments of necrotic exudate are also seen, admixed with fungal hyphae and spores.
The squamous epithelium focally features downward proliferation with mild to moderate cellular atypia and increased mitotic activity, which may be reactive or neoplastic. 

Although no definite stromal invasion can be determined, a verrucous carcinoma or well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma cannot be excluded.

title: Ulcer with fungal infestation
content:
Ulcer with fungal infestation

Section shows squamous mucosal tissue with ulceration and abundant neutrophilic infiltrate along with fungal spores and hyphae. No stromal tissue is present for evaluation. Clinical correlation is recommended and re-biopsy may be needed if there is clinical suspicion of malignancy

Microscopic description:

Basis of the histologic findings, differentiation between verrucous hyperplasia, PVL, well-differentiated squamous cell carcinoma and verrucous carcinoma is not possible. Complete excision of the lesion to exclude the presence of submucosal invasion is recommended.

[Addendum] (Issued on July 7, 2024)
Additional immunostaining shows that the nevus cells are diffusely positive for cyclin D1 throughout the entire depth of the tumor, hinting an aggressive nature according to McKee's Pathology of the Skin, 2012 edition. Close follow-up is strongly recommended.

In caseous necrosis (caseating)  histological architecture is  reserved. Acellular pink areas of necrosis surrounded by a granulomatous inflammatory process.
  - foci of tuberculous infections.
liquefactive necrosis: dead cells are completely digested away and normal architecture is obliterated. Usually caused by infection and is associated with inflammatory cells.

Ulceration = neutrophil + necrotic debris + vessel
Angiofibroma growth pattern

Granulative tissue: edematous stroma and small vessel (increased vascularity)

Granuloma = rounded aggregate of histiocytes. Histiocytes in a granuloma have abundant cytoplasm (are “epithelioid”) and indistinct cell borders such that they seem to merge into a 
                     syncytium. Giant cells are usually but not always present. If syncytial aggregates of histiocytes/giant cells are in sheets (rather than in a discreet nodule) the preferred term is                                          “granulomatous inflammation” rather than granuloma.

title: This case had been sent to Dr. Kung-Chao Chang at
content:
This case had been sent to Dr. Kung-Chao Chang at
National Cheng Kung University Hospital for consultation. His comment is described as follows:

PATHOLOGICAL DIAGNOSIS:
Lymph node (?), left axillary, core biopsy --- Anaplastic large cell lymphoma, ALK-negative (See Note).

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Section reveals sheets of atypical lymphoid cells with
prominent necrosis. These atypical lymphoid cells are
medium- or large-sized with irregular nuclei and focal
multinucleation. Focally eccentric kidney-shaped nuclei are
present.

By immunohistochemistry, the tumor cells are diffusely
positive for CD3 and CD30 but not CD20 or AE1/AE3. In situ
hybridization for EBV-encoded mRNA (EBER) is focally
positive.

We also performed 9 immunostains including LCA, CD20, CD43,
EMA, CD4, granzyme B, ALK, TIA1 & bF-1 (18C00200).

Note: The differential is CD30-positive peripheral T-cell lymphoma NOS. In view of morphology and EMA+/betaF-1(-), ALK-negative ALCL is favored.
---------------------------------------------------------
PRELIMINARY DIAGNOSIS:
Lymph node, left axilla, core biopsy --- T-cell lymphoma

Note: This case will be sent to Dr. Kung-Chao Chang at
National Cheng Kung University Hospital for further
consultation.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of 7 pieces of tissue measuring
up to 0.7x0.1x0.1 cm.
All for section.

Microscopic description:
Section reveals sheets of atypical lymphoid cells with
prominent necrosis. These atypical lymphoid cells are
medium- or large-sized with irregular nuclei and focal
multinucleation. Focally eccentric kidney-shaped nuclei are
present.

By immunohistochemistry, the tumor cells are diffusely
positive for CD3 and CD30 but not CD20 or AE1/AE3. In situ
hybridization for EBV-encoded mRNA (EBER) is focally
positive.

Parakeratosis:retention of nuclei in the stratum corneum
infestation: 寄生蟲侵擾
Organoid: 中間有類似紅紅的collagen隔開形成一簌一簌的樣子

bland small round nuclei
Cytologic system
GX Grade cannot be assessed
G1 Well differentiated (Low grade)
G2 Moderately differentiated (Intermediate grade)
G3 Poorly differentiated (High grade)
G4 Undifferentiated (High grade)

In caseous necrosis (caseating)  histological architecture is  reserved. Acellular pink areas of necrosis surrounded by a granulomatous inflammatory process.
  - foci of tuberculous infections.
liquefactive necrosis: dead cells are completely digested away and normal architecture is obliterated. Usually caused by infection and is associated with inflammatory cells.

Ulceration = neutrophil + necrotic debris + vessel
Angiofibroma growth pattern

Granulative tissue: edematous stroma and small vessel (increased vascularity)

Granuloma = rounded aggregate of histiocytes. Histiocytes in a granuloma have abundant cytoplasm (are “epithelioid”) and indistinct cell borders such that they seem to merge into a 

                     syncytium. Giant cells are usually but not always present. If syncytial aggregates of histiocytes/giant cells are in sheets (rather than in a discreet nodule) the preferred term is                                          “granulomatous inflammation” rather than granuloma.

title: 如果都排除了  那可以符合
content:
COMMENT:
Hepatocellular carcinoma, and metastatic colorectal are
excluded by morphology and immunostains.

The immunophenotype suggests metastatic carcinoma, with
possible origins including mammary, urothelial, or salivary
duct carcinoma based on positive immunostains with GATA3 and
TRPS1.

However, a small proportion of primary non-small cell lung
carcinomas also express GATA3 and TRPS1, so a pulmonmary
origin is possible if no tumors are found in the above-
mentioned organs. Clinico-radiological correlation is
recommended for determining the origin of the tumor.
