title: Acquired digital fibrokeratoma.
content:
Acquired digital fibrokeratoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows polypoid skin lesion with variably hyperplastic epidermis and is composed of dense collagen fibers oriented predominantly in vertical direction.

title: acral lentigo.
content:
Acral lentigo.

Microscopic description:
Section shows marked basal cell hyperpigmentation with slight elongation of rete ridges with melanophages in the dermis.

title: Actinic keratosis [SKIAK]
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Actinic keratosis, __, excisional biopsy

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
The specimen is composed of ___ piece_ of ___ tissue, measuring __ x ___ x ___ cm.

All are embedded in ___ cassette.

Actinic keratosis.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Section shows skin tissue revealing parakeratosis and hyperkeratosis of squamous
epithelium, with atypia predominantly affecting the basal layer. The epidermis is
focally proliferating in budlike downgrowths into the papillary dermis. Solar
elastosis and chronic inflammatory infiltrate are seen in the dermis.

 性質: 0 benign

title: AI
content:
acute inflammation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with focal intraepithelial neutrophilic infiltrate.

title: AI + fungus/ acute inflammation with fungal infection
content:
Acute inflammation with fungal infection.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with focal intraepithelial neutrophilic infiltrate, and fungal spores and hyphae within the keratin layer.

title: AI +abscess + bacterial
content:
acute inflammation with abscess.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with neutrophilic infiltrate and focal abscess formation admixed with bacterial colonies.

title: AI +abscess.
content:
acute inflammation with abscess.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with neutrophilic infiltrate and focal abscess formation.

title: AI with gross
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Skin, ____ finger, debridement --- Acute inflammation.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of _ pieces of tissue measuring up to _ cm.
All for section.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with focal intraepithelial neutrophilic infiltrate.

title: Angiokeratoma.
content:
Angiokeratoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows ectasia of superficial blood vessels accompanied by epidermal changes including acanthosis and hyperkeratosis.

title: Angioleiomyoma
content:
Angioleiomyoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows one well-defined nodular lesion composed of proliferative muscle bundles and compressed vessels.

title: Arteriovenous hemangioma.
content:
Arteriovenous hemangioma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with intradermal proliferation of numerous thick-walled vessels lined with plump endothelial cells.

title: Atrophic dermatofibroma
content:
Atrophic dermatofibroma.

Microscopic description:
The dermally located lesion is a poorly circumscribed proliferation of low cellularity, containing fibroblasts and some multinucleated cells in a collagenous background.

title: Basal epithelial proliferative lesion (see microscopic description)
content:
Section shows skin tissue with subepithelial proliferation of basaloid cells featuring  focal cellular atypism, occasional mitosis, and spindling pattern. Scattered melanin
pigmentations are seen within the tumor nests as well as in the superficial dermis. Connection between the lesional cells and the epidermis cannot be determined.
Retraction artifacts are focally seen around tumor nests. Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are negative for S-100, excluding melanocytic lesion. Total excision of
the tumor is recommended for a definitive diagnosis.

title: Basosquamous cell carcinoma
content:
Basosquamous cell carcinoma.

Microscopic description: 
Tumor histological type: Basosquamous cell carcinoma
Tumor grade: I (Well differentiated)
Tumor size: 1.2 cm in maximum dimension.
Tumor thickness: 0.4 cm.
Invasion depth: Invades the Dermis
Margins of excision:
  Peripheral margin: <1 mm
  Deep margin: <1 mm
Additional tumor features:
  Lymphovascular Invasion: Absent
  Perineural Invasion: Absent
  Necrosis: Absent
  Mitotic count / 5 hpf (mm2): 2
Lymph Node: not assessed.
pTNM: pT1.

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

title: BCC (basal cell carcinoma)
content:
Basal cell carcinoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a tumor (__ mm in maximum dimension) composed of lobules of basaloid cells bearing scant cytoplasm and featuring peripheral palisading, retraction artifact, and presence of scattered squamous eddies with dyskeratotic cells. Mucinous change of stroma is noted focally.

Peripheral margin: 
Deep margin: 

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

Basal cell carcinoma

title: BCC (suspect basosquamous carcinoma)
content: Section shows lobules of basaloid cells bearing scant cytoplasm and featuring peripheral palisading, retraction artifact., and focal keratinization. In the deep portion of the tumor, there are infiltrative nests and cords of neoplastic cells that show more abundant cytoplasm with vesicular nuclei, which may represent squamous differentiation. The overall features suggest a basal cell carcinoma, in which a variant of basosquamous carcinoma cannot be excluded. Wide excision is recommended. The peripheral and deep margins are involved.

title: Blue nevus
content:
Common blue nevus.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a picture of blue nevus with heavy melanin pigment deposition in the  dermal spindle cells which are intermingled with many melanophages. The epidermis is unremarkable. There is no evidence of malignancy.

title: Bowen disease
content:
It shows intraepithelial neoplastic growth of squamous epithelial cells devoid of
stromal invasion. Margins are not involved.

title: Bowen disease
content:
Bowen disease

Microscopic description:
Sections show dysplastic squamous cells replacing the full epidermal layer. The margin is free with closest distance of _ mm from peripheral margin.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

[F] Malignant 213

title: Caallus, skin
content: The lesion is characterized by markedly thickened keratin layer and hyperplastic epithelium.

title: Calcinosis cutis
content:
calcinosis cutis

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with dermal and subcutaneous deposition of numerous calcified materials. It may be a cutaneous phenomenon secondary to local tissue degeneration ("dystrophy") or may occur when the final metabolic defects give rise to elevated serum calcium or phosphate. Clinical correlation is suggested.

title: cherry angioma.
content:
cherry angioma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with a small polypoid lesion consisting of multiple lobules of dilated and congested capillaries.

title: chronic dermatitis.
content:
chronic dermatitis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with perivascular lymphoplasmacytic infiltration in the su    perficial dermis. No maligancy is observed.

title: Compound nevus.
content:
Compound nevus.

Microscopic description:
Section shows nevus cells in both junctional and intradermal areas, in which the cells mature with depth. No malignancy is observed.

title: Condyloma acuminatum
content:
Condyloma acuminatum.

Microscopic description:
Section shows the squamous epithelium displaying papillary proliferation in which the individual papillae are supported by a fibrovascular core and the hyperplastic squamous cells exhibit numerous koilocytes (perinuclear halo cells). No malignancy is observed.

title: consistent with accessory tragi.
content:
consistent with accessory tragi.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a nodule covered by squamous epithelium with cutaneous adnexae and central cartilage.

title: consistent with follicular cyst, ruptured and inflamed.
content:
consistent with follicular cyst, ruptured and inflamed.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue featuring marked mixed inflammatory cell infiltration that includes foreign body type multinucleated giant cells. There is identifiable residual hair shaft and squamous epithelium. The overall features are consistent with epidermal inclusion cyst, ruptured and inflamed.

title: consistent with lentigo simplex.
content:
consistent with lentigo simplex.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue revealing epithelial basilar hypermelanosis with slight elongation of rete ridges with melanophages in the dermis.

title: Consistent with ruptured epidermal inclusion cyst
content:
Consistent with ruptured epidermal inclusion cyst
Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue exhibiting dermal infiltrate of mixed inflammatory cells, collections of histiocytes and aggregates of multinucleated giant cells. The features are compatible with a ruptured epidermal inclusion cyst.

title: Consistent with ruptured follicular cyst
content:
Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue exhibiting dermal infiltrate of mixed inflammatory cells, collections of histiocytes and aggregates of multinucleated giant cells. The features are compatible with a ruptured follicular cyst.

title: Corn (callus)
content:
Corn (callus).

Microscopic description:
The lesion is characterized by markedly thickened keratin layer and hyperplastic epithelium.

title: Crush injury_C/W crush injury
content: Section shows hemorrhage in the dermis and subcutaneous fat consistent with crushing  injury.

title: Cutaneous amyloidosiseous amyloidosis
content:
Section shows eosinophilic amorphous material deposition predominantly within subcutaneous layer and focally in the dermis.

Congo red staining demonstrates apple-green birefringence under polarized light. Staining for PAS, desmin and S100 are negative. No malignancy is observed.

title: Dermatofibroma [skiDF]
content:
dermatofibroma.

Microscopic description:
The dermally-located lesion is a poorly circumscribed proliferation of varying cellularity containing fibroblasts, histiocytes and mature collagen that exhibits a vague storiform pattern.

Multinucleated giant cells with hemosiderin
pigment, as well as lipid-laden histiocytes are also
seen. Staining for CD34 is negative. No malignancy is
observed.

title: Dermatofibroma with follicular induction in the overlying epidermis
content:
Dermatofibroma with follicular induction in the overlying epidermis

Microscopic description:
It shows a dermatofibroma in the dermis. The overlying epidermis is hyperplastic and acanthotic. Some of the rete
ridges of the hyperplastic epidermis is made up of basaloid cells. The peripheral row of these basaloid cells exhibits a palisading arrangement and is close contact with mesenchymal cells that reproduce a papillary mesenchymal body like structure of a primitive hair follicle. No evident cleft formation or increase mitoses, suggesting a basal cell
carcinoma, is present.

title: Dermatofibroma, cellular variant.
content:
Dermatofibroma, cellular variant. See Note.

Microscopic description:
The dermal lesion shows proliferation of fibroblasts, histiocytes and mature collagen that exhibits a storiform pattern. There is increased cellularity and mitotic activity with central necrosis. Stainings for CD34 and AE1/AE3 are negative.  Lesional cells at the edge are positive for CD34.
The margin is <1 mm from lesional cells. 

NOTE: The risk of local recurrence is higher with this subtype.

title: dermatofibroma, lipidized ("Ankle Type") variant. [skiDF1]
content:
dermatofibroma, lipidized ("Ankle Type") variant.

Microscopic description:
The dermally-located lesion is a poorly circumscribed
proliferation of varying cellularity containing epithelioid to spindle cells, histiocytes and mature collagen. Some lesional cells show prominent nucleoli.
Multinucleated giant cells with hemosiderin
pigment, as well as numerous lipid-laden histiocytes are seen.

Immunohistochemically, the lesional cells are negative for
SMA, AE1/AE3, S100 and Melan A. CD68 highlights the
histiocytes.

The overall features are consistent with dermatofibroma of
lipidized ("Ankle Type") variant.

title: Dermatofibroma/Aneurysmal fibrous histiocytoma.  [skiDF]
content:
Dermatofibroma, aneurysmal variant. See Note.

Microscopic description:
The dermally-located lesion is a poorly circumscribed proliferation of varying cellularity containing fibroblasts, histiocytes and mature collagen that exhibits a vague storiform pattern. There are large, irregular, blood-filled spaces and hemosiderin-laden macrophages

Staining for CD34 is negative. No malignancy is observed.

Note: The risk of local recurrence is higher with the aneurysmal subtype.

title: Desmoplastic trichilemmoma.
content:
Desmoplastic trichilemmoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows an epithelial tumor composed of clear cell with peripheral palisading nand focal presence of basement membrane. Focal area shows irregular epithelial nests and strands embedded in a desmoplastic stroma.
Immunohistochemically, the lesional cells are focally positive for CD34 and p16.
The overall features and consistent with desmoplastic trichilemmoma.

title: dilated pore.
content:
dilated pore.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a keratin-plugged cystically dilated follicle which is superficially located. It is lined by stratified squamous epithelium which is acanthotic with irregular budding.

title: Dysplastic nevus.
content:
Microscopic description:
The section reveals junctional and dermal melanocytes arranged in irregular nests and as single units. Bridging of the rete ridges is also present. Lamellar fibroplasia and a scattered lymphocytic infiltrate are noted in the dermis. The surgical margin is free of the lesion, with the closest distance measuring 1 mm. Clinical follow-up is recommended.

Ancillary studies: 
HMB45: Focally positive with superficial staining. 
P16: Positive
Ki67: 1%
BRAF(VE1): Positive.
BAP1: Positive.

title: Eccrine poroma
content:
eccrine poroma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows broad anastomosing bands of uniformly small cuboidal cells connected intercellular bridges. Narrow ductal lumen is also seen noted within the tumor bands.

title: Eccrine spiradenoma
content:
eccrine spiradenoma.

Microscopic description:
The tumor consists of a couple of sharply demarcated lobules within the dermis that show no connections with the epidermis.
The lobules are made up of two types of epithelial cells arranged in intertwining cords. Cells with small, dark nuclei lie at the periphery of the bands and cells with large pale nuclei that lie in the center of the bands and around small lumina. The lumina
frequently contain small amounts of eosinophilic material.

title: Epidermal nevus.
content:
Epidermal nevus.

Section shows acanthosis and papillomatosis of squamous epithelium, accompanied by chronic inflammation in the dermis.

title: Epidermoid cyst, keratin substance only [SKI15K]
content:
Epidermoid cyst, keratin substance only

Microscopic description:
Section shows laminated keratin fragments only.

title: Erythema nodosum
content:
The histologic section is mainly confined to the subcutaneous tissue which shows
septal inflammation spread into the adjacent lobule. In addition, there are
conspicuous granulomata composed of histiocytes with multinucleated giant cells, and
occasional eosinophils and neutrophils. Miescher radial granuloma characterized by
aggregations of small histiocytes around a central clef is also seen.
Perivascular lymphocytic infiltrate is seen at deep dermis.

Special stainings (acid fast, Auramine-Rhodamine, GMS and PAS) show no micro-organism
in the granulomas. No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: FB retention
content:
Consistent with foreign body retention

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with epidermial defect and chronic inflammatory infiltrate in the dermis. No malignancy is observed.

title: FB retention 1C [FB}
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
_____, removal of foreign body --- Consistent with foreign body retention.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of one piece of foreign body material measuring _ cm in size.
[gross examination only]

title: fibrosarcomatous dermatofibrosarcoma DFSP
content:
fibrosarcomatous dermatofibrosarcoma
    

Microscopic description:
Tumor type: fibrosarcomatous dermatofibrosarcoma
  protuberans.
Tumor size: __ cm at greatest dimension
Tumor location: chest wall, within dermis and subcutis
Surgical margin: free of tumor, with closest distance of 1
  cm from peripheral margin
FNCLCC grading: grade 1
- Differentiation: score 1
- Mitotic count: score 2, 15/10HPF
- Tumor necrosis: score 0, no necrosis
pTNM: pTlNx.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

title: focal mucinosis.
content:
focal mucinosis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows interstitial mucin deposits in widened spaces
between collagen bundles in the dermis, admixed with bland
fibroblastic cells being focally positive for CD34 but not
S100, EMA, SMA, desmin or ER. The mucin is positive for
mucicarmine but not PAS. The overall features are most
consistent with focal mucinosis.

title: Follicular cyst [SKI12]
content:
follicular cyst.

Microscopic description:
The cyst is lined by stratified squamous epithelium connecting to the overlying skin. Its lumen contains keratin material. No evidence of inflammation or malignancy is noted.

title: Follicular cyst [SKI12FB]
content:
follicular cyst, inflamed.

Microscopic description:
The cyst is lined by stratified squamous epithelium connecting to the overlying skin, associated with a foreign body granulomatous response. Its lumen contains keratin material. No evidence of malignancy is noted.

title: Follicular cyst, inflamed [Ski12in]
content:
follicular cyst, inflamed.

Microscopic description:
The cyst is lined by stratified squamous epithelium connecting to the overlying skin. Its lumen contains keratin material. Inflammatory reaction is present. No evidence of malignancy is noted.

title: Follicular cyst, ruptured and inflamed. [SKI12A]
content:
follicular cyst, ruptured and inflamed.

Microscopic description:
The cyst is lined by stratified squamous epithelium connecting to the overlying skin. At its epithelial-stromal interface, there is marked mixed inflammatory cell infiltration that includes foreign body type multinucleated giant cells. The lumen is partially obliterated by deposition of similar type of inflammatory cells, with identifiable residual keratin material.

title: Folliculitis
content:
The skin tissue shows mild acanthosis of epidermis with elongated rete ridges. Infiltrate of neutrophils and lymphocytes are seen within follicular epithelium.
Associated granulomatous reaction is seen in the adjacent dermis. Perivascular infiltrate of chronic inflammatory cells are seen. No fungus is demonstrated by GMS and PAS.

title: Folliculosebaceous cystic hamartoma.
content:
folliculosebaceous cystic hamartoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows hair follicles with sebaceous differentiation in a fibrous stroma.

folliculosebaceous cystic hamartoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a dermal-based lesion composed of cystic cavities with secretions in the lumen, accompanied by hair follicles, sebaceous glands, in a stroma containing fibrous and adipose tissue.

title: foreign body granuloma [silicone]
content:
Foreign body granuloma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows variable-sized spaces surrounded by histiocytes, resembling lipoblasts. The histiocytes are positive for CD68 but not S100 or CDK4. Foreign body such as silicone should be considered. Please correlate clinically.

title: Gangrene
content:
Gangrene

Microscopic description:
Sections of the skin and soft tissue show liquefactive necrosis with heavy PMN infiltrate as well as lymphoplasmacytic cells infiltrate.

title: Granulation tissue with chronic active inflammation.
content:
Granulation tissue with chronic active inflammation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows dermal and subcutaneous tissue featuring granulation tissue with infiltrate of acute and chronic inflammatory cells admixed with histiocytes and occasional multinucleated giant cells.

title: Haluronic acid fillers
content:
Section shows pools of basophilic amorphous substances within soft tissue with surrounding granulomatous reaction. These deposits are positive for alcian blue
stain. The findings may suggest hyaluronic acid fillers. Clinical correlation is recommended.

title: Hidradenitis suppurativa
content:
Consistent with hidradenitis suppurativa, _ axilla, excision

Section shows epithelial-lined sinus tracts with mixed inflammation and granulation tissue as well as multinucleated giant cells in the underlying stroma, consistent with hidradenitis suppurativa. No malignancy is observed.

title: Hidradenoma.
content:
Hidradenoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a dermal-located lesion with solid and cystic growth. It is comosed of clear or pale cells admixed with squamoid cells in a fibrovascular and hyalinized stroma.

title: Hidrocystoma.
content:
Hidrocystoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with a dermally-located cystic lesion lined by inner columnar
cells and outer myoepithelial cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: Hyperpigmentation
content:
excisional biopsy --- hyperpigmentation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue revealing epithelial basilar hypermelanosis.

title: Hypertrophic scar
content:
hypertrophic scar.

Microscopic description:
Dense and hypertrophic collagenous fibers with perpendicular running vessels are seen in the dermis. Focal hemorrhage and chronic inflammatory infiltrate are also noted. No malignancy is observed.

title: IDN Intradermal nevus
content:
intradermal nevus.

Microscopic description:
It shows intact epidermis with intradermal lobules or nests of mature nevus cells. No junctional activity is noted. No malignancy is observed.

title: IDN+SKI15
content:
-- Intradermal nevus.
-- Epidermoid cyst.

Microscopic description:
It shows intact epidermis with intradermal lobules or nests of mature nevus cells. No junctional activity is noted.
There is also a dermally-located cyst lined by squamous epithelium with keratin fragments in its lumen.

title: inverted follicular keratosisinverted follicular keratosis
content:
inverted follicular keratosis

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue revealing well-circumscribed endophytic growth of squamous cells featuring lobular extension into the dermis, with numerous horn cysts. Solar elastosis is also seen. No malignancy is observed.

old man/head and neck

title: Irritated SK [SkiSK]
content:
irritated seborrheic keratosis.

Microscopic description:
The epidermis is characterized by a sharply demarcated, exophytic lesion which shows hyperkeratosis and papillomatosis with squamous eddies formation. Chronic inflammatory infiltrate is seen in the dermis.

Microscopic description:
The epidermis is characterized by a sharply demarcated, enophytic lesion which shows hyperplastic squamous epithelium with several squamous eddies and scattered horn cyst formation. Neutrophilic scale-crust is noted at the surface. Chronic inflammatory infiltrate is seen in the dermis. Immunohistochemically, the Ki67 shows increased nuclear staining confined within basal layer.

title: junctional nevus [JCN]
content:
Junctional nevus

Section shows nevus cells in junctional area with a low Ki-67 proliferative index. No malignancy is observed.

title: Juvenile Xanthogranuloma, JXG

Juvenile xanthogranuloma.

The section shows a demarcated, dense dermal infiltrate of foamy histiocytes, with scattered Touton giant cells.

title: Keloid
content:
Keloid

title: large cell acanthoma.
content:
large cell acanthoma.

Section shows flat featureless epidermis containing enlarged keratinocytes with hyperpigmentation, which are devoid of atypia. Solar elastosis is seen. No malignancy is observed.

title: lipogranuloma.
content:
lipogranuloma

Microscopic description: 
Section shows variable-sized spaces in the dermis, surrounded by histiocytes and multinucleated giant cells.
This is probably due to injection or topical application of oil based substances. Please correlate.

title: Lobular capillary hemangioma
content:
Lobular capillary hemangioma

Microscopic description:
Section shows a demarcated lesion composed of lobules of capillary channels lined by bland endothelial cells. Mitos    es are occasionally seen. No malignancy is observed.

title: Lymphoid hyperplasia with eosinophilia
content:
Skin, left chest, excision --- Lymphoid hyperplasia with eosinophilia (See Note)
Note: This case has sent to National Cheng Kung University Hospital for diagnosis.

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
Section shows mixed inflammatory infiltrate composed of abundant eosinophils, lymphocytes and histiocytes in
superficial and deep dermis with occasional lymphoid follicle formation.

No epidermotropism is identified but aggregates of Langerhans cells within epidermis are noted.
Immunohistochemically, these lymphocytes are composed a mixed population of cells positive for CD3, CD20 and
bcl-2. CD4+ and CD8+ cells show a normal distribution and PD-1 stains only 10-15% of cells with weak staining
intensity.
CD10 immunostain highlights the germinal centers. EBER in situ hybridization is negative.

Note: Eczematous dermatitis is favored.

title: Melaning bleaching method
content:
No removal of pigment is noted after melanin bleaching method.

Then pigments are removed after melanin bleaching method.

title: mixed tumor (chondroid syringoma).
content:
mixed tumor (chondroid syringoma).

Microscopic description:
Section shows a well-circumscribed tumor with epithelial strands in a myxoid stroma.

title: Molluscum contagiosum
content:
Molluscum contagiosum.

Microscopic description:
The section shows an indented dermis as a result of sharply
delimited and lobulated mass of proliferating epidermis,
associated with prominent molluscum bodies.

title: Neurofibroma [NF]
content:
neurofibroma.

Microscopic description:
Sections show a demarcated nodule composed of loosely arranged spindle cells in haphazard arrangement. The lesional cells feature small, wavy or buckled nuclei in a variable collagenous matrix. 

Degenerative atypia is present.
By immunohistochemistry, the lesional cells are positive for S100, consistent with the diagnosis.

title: neurofibroma with vague plexiform growth [NF]
content:
neurofibroma with vague plexiform growth.

Microscopic description:
Sections show demarcated nodules composed of loosely arranged spindle cells in vague plexiform growth. The lesional cells feature small, hyperchromatic, wavy or buckled nuclei in a variable myxoid to collagenous matrix.
By immunohistochemistry, scattered positive cells for S100, EMA and CD34 are seen. No malignancy is observed.

title: Nevus lipomatosus superficialis
content:
nevus lipomatosus superficialis.

Microscopic description:
It shows skin tissue with wide spread infiltrate of the dermis by mature adipocytes.
No malignancy is observed.

title: Nevus sebaceus
content:
nevus sebaceus.

Microscopic description:
The section shows an exophytic lesion revealing papillomatosis and sebaceous gland hyperplasia that open directly into epidermis. It also shows apocrine glands and paucity of hair follicles underneath the lesion. The above-mentioned features are consistent with nevus sebaceus.

title: Nevus sebaceus
content:
Nevus sebaceus.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
The epidermis exhibits papillomatous hyperplasia with hyperkeratosis. Numerous mature sebaceous glands lie in the
dermis. The hair structures remain small except for occasional dilated keratin-filled infundibula.

title: Nevus sebaceus with syringocystadenoma papilliferum, skin, scalp
content:
The section shows an exophytic lesion revealing papillomatosis and sebaceous gland hyperplasia that open directly into epidermis. It also shows apocrine glands and
paucity of hair follicles underneath the lesion. The above-mentioned features are consistent with nevus sebaceus.

Syringocystadenoma papilliferum is also present in the background of nevus sebaceus,
which shows papillary projections with intense lymphoplasmacytic infiltration in the
stroma. The papillae is covered superficially by squamous epithelium and deeply by
double-layered epithelium, consisting of inner tall columnar cells and outer cuboidal
myoepithelila cells. No evidence of malignancy is observed.

content: Oak 5雙先不用

title: Onychomycosis (nail)
content:
onychomycosis.

Section shows skin and nail tissue with focal intraepithelial neutrophilic infiltrate. Fungal spores and hyphae within the keratin layer are highlighted by PAS.

title: osteoma cutis.
content:
osteoma cutis.

Microscopic description:
Sections show a well-circumscribed nodule of mature lamellar bone focally rimmed by osteoblasts and containing fatty marrow spaces.

title: Palisaded granulomatous inflammation. (see description)
content:
Palisaded granulomatous inflammation. (see description)             

Microscopic description:
Section shows necrobiotic center with peripheral palisading of histiocytes and scattered lymphocytes. No mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast stain. No fungus is identified by PAS. The histiocytes are highlighted by CD68. Desmin is negative.

The differential diagnoses include rheumatoid nodule, granuloma annulare or granulomatous inflammation induced by other causes. Please correlate with clinical features.

title: papillary hidradenoma.
content:
papillary hidradenoma.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Section shows elongated tubules and papillary strucutures lined by luminal columnar cells with decapitation secretion, and basal cuboidal myoepithelial cells.
P16 is negative. No malignancy is observed.

title: papillary lesion (see description).
content:
papillary lesion (see description).

Microscopic description:
Section shows the squamous epithelium displaying focally and mildly papillary proliferation with hyperkeratotic surface. No obvious koilocytes (perinuclear halo cells) or dysplasia are discerned.
However, condyloma acuminatum cannot be excluded.
Please follow-up and correlate clinically.
No malignancy is observed.

Ancillary studies: deeper-cut.

Microscopic description:
The specimen consists of a tiny fragment of vulval tissue, in which there is parakeratosis of the squamous epithelium.
Early papillary structure and equivocal koilocytosis are present but not enough to classify it as condyloma acuminatum. Staining for p16 is focally positive.
Clinical correlation and follow-up is recommended.

debridement, removal of partial nail and excision of pyogenic granuloma --- consistent with paronychia.

title: Penetrating injury
content:
Chronic inflammation, consistent with penetrating injury

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with epidermial defect and chronic inflammatory infiltrate in the dermis. No malignancy is observed.

title: Pigmented fungus
content:
Microscopic description: 
Section shows granulomatous inflammation with large round pigmented fungal forms, which may represent an agent of chromoblastomycosis or phaeohyphomycosis. Correlation with culture is needed for diagnosis.

title: Pilomatricoma
content:
Pilomatricoma

Microscopic description:
The lesion is a pilomatrixoma, composed of nests of benign squamous cells with calcifying ghost cells more evident from the center toward periphery. No evidence of malignancy is seen.

Pilomatricoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows a well demarcated lesion located at deep dermis to superficial subcutis, composed of ghost cells and basaloid cells accompanied by calcification and mixed inflammatory infiltrate as well as occasional multinucleated giant cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: Pilonidal sinus
content:
Pilonidal sinus

Microscopic description:
Section shows hair shaft fragment in the dermis associated with foreign body reaction and mixed inflammatory infiltrate. Sinus lined by squamous epithelium is seen.

title: porokeratosis.
content:
cornoid lamella formation, suggestive of porokeratosis. 
see NOTE.

Microscopic description:
Section shows keratin-filled epidermal invagination with cornoid lamella. Dyskeratosis is seen. Chronic inflammatory cell infiltrate is seen in the dermis. 

NOTE: These findings may be suggestive of porokeratosis.
However, cornoid lamella formation may also occur as a non-specific finding in a variety of conditions. Clinical correlation is mandatory for the diagnosis.

title: Proliferating epidermal cyst.
content:
Proliferating epidermal cyst.

Microscopic description:
The cyst is lined by stratified squamous epithelium connecting to the overlying skin. Its lumen contains keratin material. Focal epithelial proliferation with horn cysts and squamous eddies formation is seen. No evidence of inflammation or malignancy is noted.

title: Proliferating trichilemmal tumor (PTT). See Note.
content:
Proliferating trichilemmal tumor (PTT). See Note.

Microscopic description:
Sections show a well-circumscribed solid and cystic dermal
tumor focally connecting to the overlying skin. It is composed of squamous epithelium with prominent
infolding and containing homogeneous eosinophilic keratin.
The neoplastic cells are monomorphous without significant
cytological atypia. Extending to subcutaneous fat is also
seen. Focal and limited irregular outlines and growth are seen, which is present adjacent to inflammatory and forein body reaction, and is in favor of reactive rather than malignant change. The surgical margin is free, with closest distance of 2 mm from deep margin.

Note: Most cases of PTT have shown benign biological
behavior. In rare instances, malignant transformation may
occur. Follow-up is suggested.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

Ref. S0902598

title: Proliferative actinic keratosis
content: Section shows dysplastic squamous epithelium focally exhibiting proliferating in budlike down growths into the papillary dermis. The dermis underlying the keratosis shows solar elastosis and vascular ectasia.

title: Pyogenic granuloma
content:
Pyogenic granuloma.

No malignancy is observed.

content:
Reticulohistiocytosis.

Section shows a dermal nodule composed of histiocytoid cells revealing ground glass cytoplasm, vesicular nuclei, and prominent nucleoli, and including multinucleated forms.
An admixure of lymphocytes and occasional eosinophils is seen.

By immunohistochemistry, the lesional cells are positive for CD68 but not S100 or SMA, consistent with the diagnosis.

sebaceoma.

expression.

title: sebaceous adenoma (please see description).
content:
sebaceous adenoma (please see description).

Microscopic description:
Section shows lobules of mature sebaceous cells with a
minority of basaloid germinative cells at the periphery.

Stainings for MLH1 and MSH2 show intact nuclear
expression. However, most of the tumor cells show no MSH6 expression.

Sebaceous adenoma forms part of the spectrum of the Muir-Torre syndrome. Please correlate clinically.

content:
Sebaceous hyperplasia.

content:
Section shows a subcutaneous lesion composed of proliferation of fibroblasts and myofibroblasts admixed with some lymphocytes, suggesting fibrosis after a prior infection/inflammation. No malignancy is observed.

The skin tissue shows mild acanthosis with multinodular dermal proliferation of fibroblasts and myofibroblasts admixed with some lymphocytes in the underlying dermis.

title: Section shows well-demarcated tumor composed of lobules of mature sebaceous cell
content: Section shows well-demarcated tumor composed of lobules of mature sebaceous cells with a minority of basaloid germinative cells at the periphery. The surface is ulcerative.No malignancy is observed.

content: Sections show the epidermis with full-thickness replacement by neoplastic squamous cells that show hyperchromatism, enlargement and loss of polarity of nuclei without stromal invasion.

title: SKI16
content:
Epidermoid cyst, ruptured and inflamed.

content:
consistent with epidermoid cyst, ruptured and inflamed.

content:

The dermally-located cyst shows partial destruction of its lining squamous epithelium. There is marked mixed inflammatory cell infiltrate that includes foreign body type multinucleated giant cells, with focal abscess formation. No malignancy is observed.

title: Skin
content:
Calcinosis cutis
Section shows skin tissue with dermal and subcutaneous deposition of numerous calcified materials. It may be a cutaneous phenomenon secondary to local tissue
degeneration ("dystrophy") or may occur when the final metabolic defects give rise to elevated serum calcium or phosphate. Clinical correlation is suggested.

Dermatofibroma
Section shows skin tissue with acanthosis of epidermis and a dermal proliferation of bland-looking spindle cells featuring oval or spindle nuclei admixed with some foamy histiocytes. Tumor extension to the subcutis is noted. No cellular atypia or increased mitotic activity is observed. Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are negative for CD34, excluding dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans. No frank malignancy is observed.

Hidrocystoma
It shows skin tissue with a dermally-located cystic lesion lined by inner columnar
cells and outer myoepithelial cells. No malignancy is observed.

Squamous epithelial proliferative lesion (建議total excision)
It shows hyperkeratic and acanthotic squamous epithelium with mild cellular atypia and focally dyskeratosis. No adequate stromal tissue is present for evaluation of invasiveness. Underlying malignancy cannot be excluded. Total excision may be necessary for precise diagnosis.

Irritated serrheic keratosis
Section shows hyperkeratosis and papillomatosis of squamous epithelium with proliferation of variably pigmented basal cells and presence of horn cysts. Marked subepithelial chronic inflammatory infiltrate is noted.
Necrosis and suppurative inflammation
Section shows skin tissue with ulceration accompanied by extensive necrosis admixed with dense PMNs infiltration in the underlying dermis and superficial subcutis. No malignancy is observed.

Congo red
Staining for Congo red shows apple-green birefringence under polarized light.

title: Skin tag (eyelid)
content:
fibroepithelial polyp (skin tag)

Microscopic description:
Section shows acanthotic epithelium with hyperkeratosis, papillary projections with inner fibrovascular core lined by stratified squamous epithelium. No malignancy is observed.

title: Skin tag (Fibroepithelial polyp)
content:
fibroepithelial polyp (skin tag).

The polypoid skin tissue is lined by an intact stratified squamous epithelium and its stroma is made up of loose collagen.

title: Skin tags and intradermal nevus IDN
content:
skin tags and intradermal nevus.

Section shows small polypoid lesions lined by an intact stratified squamous epithelium
and supported by fibrous connective tissue stroma. There is also one small intra-
dermal nevus, featuring an intact epidermis with intradermal depositions of lobules or
nests of mature nevus cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: SKISK  Seborrheic keratosis, adenoid type

seborrheic keratosis.

content:
seborrheic keratosis.

title: SKISK Seborrheic keratosis
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Seborrheic keratosis

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
The specimen is composed of     piece(s) of        tissue,
measuring

Representative sections are submitted in one cassette.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
The epidermis is characterized by a sharply demarcated, exophytic lesion which
exhibits hyperplasia of variably pigmented basaloid cells with exuberant keratin
production (hyperkeratosis) and several horn cyst formation.

title: SKISK+FUNGUS
content:
Seborrheic keratosis with fungal infection.

Microscopic description:
The epidermis is characterized by a sharply demarcated, exophytic lesion which exhibits hyperplasia of variably pigmented basaloid cells with exuberant keratin production (hyperkeratosis) and several horn cyst formation. Some fungal spores are seen within the superficial keratin layer.

title: Spitz nevus.
content:
Spitz nevus.

Microscopic description:
Section shows nests of large epithelioid cells with abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli, extending from the epidermis into the reticular dermis in a wedge-shaped configuration. The Ki-67 index is low (2%). The lesional cells are positive for melan-A, consistent with the diagnosis.

Ki67, HMB-45, P16, Cyclin D1

title: steatocystoma.
content:
steatocystoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows cystic lesion with serpiginous wall lined by thin squamous epithelium. Sebaceous glands are seen adjacent to or contiguous with cyst wall.

title: storiform collagenoma.
content:
storiform collagenoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a well-circumscribed dermal nodule composed of hypocellular hyalinized collagen bundles separated by clefts and arranged in a storiform pattern. Bland-looking spindle-shaped cells are seen. 

Scattered multinucleated giant cells are seen.
giant cell collagenoma.

title: Suggestive of Chromoblastomycosis.
content:
Suggestive of Chromoblastomycosis.

Microscopic Description:
The section shows skin tissue revealing pseudoepitheliomatous hyperplasia, mixed inflammatory
infiltrate, including multinucleated giant cells, along with abscess formation in the dermis. There are large, round,
pigmented fungal forms, which may represent an agent of chromoblastomycosis. Correlation with culture is needed for
diagnosis.

Ancillary Studies:
PAS: Fungi highlighted.
AE1/AE3: Pseudoepitheliomatous hyperplasia without
malignancy.

title: suggestive of Herpes simplex / zoster infection.
content:
suggestive of Herpes simplex / zoster infection.

Microscopic description:
Section shows intraepidermal blisters associated with intracellular edema. Several epithelial cells reveals nuclear changes of nuclear moulding, chromatin margination and multinucleation. Involvement of folliculosebaceous unit is seen. Focal epidermal necrosis is seen.
The features are suggestive of Herpes simplex / zoster infection. Please correlate.

title: suppurative granulomatous inflammation with fungal infection.
content:
suppurative granulomatous inflammation with fungal infection.

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue with dermal granulomatous inflammation with central collections of necrotic debris and neutrophils, surrounded by multinucleated giant cells, histiocytes and lymphoplasmacytic infiltration. Some fungal hyphae and yeast form are identified by PAS. No mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast stain.

title: Suppurative inflammation with fungal infection [Supp2]
content:
Suppurative inflammation with fungal infection

Microscopic description:
Section shows suppurative inflammation with collections of neutrophils, liquified debris of necrotic cells and edema fluid.   Fungal spores and hyphae are identified within necrotic keratin layer.

title: Syringocystadenoma papilliferum
content:
Syringocystadenoma papilliferum.

Microscopic description:
The section shows cystic invaginations with papillae lined by a double-layered epithelium, composed of inner tall columnar cells and outer cuboidal cells.
The stroma exhibits mononuclear inflammatory infiltrate.
No evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: Syringoma
content: Section shows a syringoma, which is composed of clusters of small ducts lined by two cells thick epithelium with comma-shaped extensions in a fibrotic stroma.

title: Thrombosed vessel
content:
thrombosed vessel (capillary aneurysm).

Microscopic description:
The slide shows a single greatly dilated vessel that lacks a
discernible wall or is surrounded by a delicate fibrous layer.
The lumen contains a partially organized thrombus.

thrombosed vessel (capillary aneurysm).

Microscopic description:
Section shows a single greatly dilated vessel with its lumen containing a partially organized thrombus.

title: traumatic neuroma.
content:
Traumatic neuroma.

Section shows irregular haphazardly arranged proliferation of regenerating nerve fascicles of various sizes and shaped embeddded in a fibrous stroma.

title: trichilemmal cyst (pilar cyst)
content:
trichilemmal cyst (pilar cyst)

Microscopic description:
Section shows an intradermal cyst lined by squamous epithelium containing homogeneous eosinophilic keratin.
Focal forein body giant cell reaction and cholesterol crystals are seen.

There is focal epithelial infolding into the cyst lumen, but is not enough to classify it as proliferating trichilemmal cyst/tumor. There is no cellular atypia nor invasive growth. However, follow-up of the lesion is still recommanded.

The margin is free of tumor (__ mm in distance).

title: Trichilemmoma
content:
Trichilemmoma.

Section shows proliferation of uniform cells with peripheral palisading and focal  thickened eosinophilic basement membrane as demonstrated by PAS.

title: Trichoadenoma
content:
Trichoadenoma

Sections show numerous keratocysts lined by stratified squamous epithelium which manifests epidermoid keratinization in the dermis. Some of the cysts rupture with inflammatory reaction.

title: Trichoblastoma
content:
Trichoblastoma

basaloid cells with conspicuous peripheral palisading in a
dense fibrous stroma. Keratocyst formation, stromal mucin
deposition are focally seen.

title: Trichoepithelioma
content:
Trichoepithelioma.

peripheral palisading, in fibrous stroma.

title: trichofolliculoma.
content:
trichofolliculoma.

Section shows a cystic cavity lined by stratified squamous epithelium containing keratinous debris and hair shaft fragments. Arising from its wall are numerous hair follicles. Stromal granulomatous inflammation are present.

Tumoral calcinosis

calcinosis cutis.

title: ulceration.

ulceration.

content:
verruca plantaris.

palmoplantar wart.

verruca vulgaris.

verruca vulgaris.

title: Warty dyskeratoma.
content:
Warty dyskeratoma.                                             
Microscopic description:
Section shows a well-circumscribed cup-shaped lesion composed of a proliferation of keratinocytes, with prominent acantholysis, with suprabasal clefting, dyskeratosis, corps ronds, corps grains, and an overlying parakeratotic plug. Mitoses are common.The surrounding stroma contains chronic inflammatory cell infiltrate.

title: Xanthelasma
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Skin, ___ eyelid, excision --- xanthelasma.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
The specimen is composed of one piece of skin tissue, measuring ____ cm.

All are embedded in one cassette.

Microscopic description:
Section shows collection of formy histiocytes in the dermis. No evidence of malignancy
is observed.

title: 有綁線的Skin/SCC
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Squamous cell carcinoma.
    
GROSS DESCRIPTION:
The specimen consists of one fragment of skin, measuring _ x _ x _ cm with two _long strands indicating the 12 o'clock and two _short strands indicating the 6 o'clock.
A ___tumor is seen, measuring _ x _ x _ cm.
It measures _ cm to 12 o'clock, _ cm to 3 o'clock, _ cm to 6 o'clock and _ to 9 o'clock surgical margins, respectively.
Representative sections are taken and labeled as follows:
          
MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
Tumor histological type: Squamous cell carcinoma
Tumor grade: II (moderately differentiated)
Tumor size: _ cm in maximum dimension.
Tumor thickness: _ cm.
Invasion depth: Invades the dermis / subcutis
Margins of excision:
  Peripheral margin: Uninvolved by tumor, _ cm in distance. Involved by tumor.
  Deep margin: involved by tumor
Additional tumor features:
  Lymphovascular Invasion: Absent
  Perineural Invasion: Absent
  Necrosis: Absent
  Mitotic count / 5 hpf (mm2): __
Lymph Node: Not assessed.
Additional finding:
  Solar elastosis.
  Hemorrhage and suppurative inflammation are seen.
Immunohistochemical stain:
  p40: positive.

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

                              

適用cutaneous carcinoma (CC), basal cell carcinoma (BCC) of head and neck 
cutaneous carcinoma (CC), basal cell carcinoma (BCC)
*Deep invasion is defined as invasion beyond the subcutaneous fat
or>6 mm (as from the granular layer of adjacent normal epidermis
to the base ofthe tumor); perineural invasion for T3 classification
is as tumor cells within the nerve sheath of a nerve lying
deeper than the dermis or measuring >=0.1 mm in caliber, or presenting
with clinical or radiographic involvement of named nerves without skull
base invasion or transgression.

title: Angiolipoma [angiolipo]
content:
angiolipoma.

Microscopic description:
Sections show mature fat cells incorporated with numerous proliferating capillaries.

title: Arteriovenous malformation. [AVM]
content:
Arteriovenous malformation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows vessels in different sizes of the vascular wall. One dilated vessel with abrupt changes in thickness of vascular wall is seen. Thrombosis is seen in the lumen. The overall features are consistent with arteriovenous malformation. By immunohistochemistry, the endothelial cells are highlighted by ERG and CD31.
Arteriovenous malformation.

Microscopic description:
Section shows arterioles, capillaries, and venules intermixed with larger-calibre, tortuous vein. By immunohistochemistry, the endothelial cells are highlighted by CD31. The overall features are consistent with arteriovenous malformation.

Arteriovenous malformation

Microscopic description:
Section shows vessels in different sizes of the vascular wall. One dilated vessel with abrupt changes in thickness of vascular wall is seen. Thrombosis is seen in the lumen. The overall features are consistent with arteriovenous malformation. No malignancy is
observed.

title: Atypical lipomatous tumor ALT
content:
Atypical lipomatous tumor

Microscopic description:
Sections show adipocytes with variation in size and presence of atypical hyperchromatic stromal cells in a myxoid stroma. The surgical margin is involved.

Immunohistochemically, the atypical cells are positive for CDK4.

title: Benign notochordal cell tumor (BNCT).
content: The biopsy shows sheets of large polyhedral cells with abundant clear to pale pink cytoplasm with round nuclei containing fine or homogeneously dense chromatin. There is no myxoid extracellular matrix. Immunohistochemical studies show that the tumor cells are positive for S-100, keratin and brachyury.

title: Bursitis
content:
bursitis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a cystic lesion surrounded by fibrous tissue with chronic inflammatory infiltrate and capillary proliferation.

PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Soft tissue,___, bursectomy --- bursitis.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of _ pieces of tissue measuring up to _ cm.

All for section.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a cystic lesion surrounded by fibrous tissue with chronic inflammatory infiltrate and capillary proliferation.

title: Cavernous hemangioma
content:
Cavernous hemangioma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a demarcated nodule composed of cystically dilated vessels filled with blood.

title: Confirmed, _ carpal ligament, transverse ligament release
content: Section shows dense connective tissue consistent with ligament.

title: consistent with calcific tendonitis.
content:
consistent with calcific tendonitis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows extensive calcific deposits focally surrounded by histiocytes, consistent with calcific tendonitis.

title: consistent with synovial cyst.
content:
consistent with synovial cyst.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a cyst formed by a fibrous connective tissue
wall focally lined by histiocytes and containing blood clots
and fibrinoid deposition in its lumen.

title: De-Quervain's disease
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Soft tissue, ____wrist, tenovaginectomy ---
Consistent with De-Quervain's disease. See description.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of 3 pieces of grayish tissue measuring 0.7x0.3x0.3 cm in size.
All for section.

Microscopic description:
Section shows thickening and myxoid degeneration of tendon consistent with a chronic degenerative process, as opposed to inflammation. These findings are consistent but not pathognomonic for De-Quervain's disease.

title: dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans.
content:
dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans.

Microscopic description:
Tumor type: dermatofibrosarcoma protuberans.
Tumor size: 2.6 cm at greatest dimension
Tumor location: lower abdominal wall, within dermis and
  subcutis
Mitotic rate: 1/10 high-power fields (HPF).
Necrosis: absent.
Surgical margin: involved at peripheral and deep margins.
Immunohistochemical studies:
  SMA: weakly positive.
  Desmin, S100: negative
  CD34: positive
  Beta-catenin: no nuclear expression.

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

title: desmoplastic fibroblastoma.
content:
desmoplastic fibroblastoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows well-circumscribed, lobulated lesions which shows patternless proliferation of bland spindle or stellate cells in a hypovascular fibrous stroma.

title: digital mucous cyst.
content:
digital mucous cyst.                 
                   
Microscopic description:
The cyst is formed by a fibrous connective tissue wall without epithelial lining.

This is a digital mucous cyst, as known as benign ganglion cysts of the digits.

title: Endometriosis
content:
Endometriosis.

Microscopic description:
Sections show soft tissue with embedded endometrial glands and stromal cells,
accompanied by aggregate of hemosiderin-laden macrophages. No malignancy is observed.

title: Epithelioid hemangioma (angiolymphoid hyperplasia with eosinophilia)
content:
Epithelioid hemangioma (angiolymphoid hyperplasia with eosinophilia)

Microscopic description:
Section shows skin tissue revealing lobular proliferation of capillaries surrounding central vessel in the subcutaneous layer. The capillaries are lined by plump, epithelioid endothelial cells without nuclear pleomorphism. There is abundant infiltrate of eosinophils and lymphocytes, accompanied by focal lymphoid follicle formation with reactive germinal centers at periphery of lesion. Staining for HHV-8 is negative. No malignancy is observed.

title: Fibrolipoma
content:
lipoma.

Microscopic description:
The mass is composed of closely-packed mature fat cells admixed with fibrous tissue component.

title: Fibroma of tendon sheath
content:
Fibroma of tendon sheath.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a demarcated nodule composed of hyalinized, collagenous matrix
associated with widely dispersed spindle cells and thin-walled blood vessels. The
spindled cells are devoid of nuclear atypia and mitotic activity.

title: GAN benign fibroadipose tissue, clinically ganglion
content:
Benign fibroadipose tissue, clinically ganglion.

Microscopic description:
Section shows fibroadipose tissue including some thick-walled blood vessels. No obvious cystic wall is included.

title: GAN Ganglion cyst
content:
Ganglion cyst.

Microscopic description:
The cyst is formed by a fibrous connective tissue wall without epithelial lining.

title: Glomus tumor
content:
Glomus tumor.

Microscopic description:
Section shows capillary sized vessels lined by endothelial
cells surrounded by uniform glomus cells forming nests and
sheets in a hyalinized stroma. Glomus cell has a round shape
with indistinct borders with eosinophilic cytoplasm
Immunohistochemically, CD34 and SMA are positive, consistent
with the diagnosis.

title: Gouty tophi
content:
Gouty tophi

Microscopic description:
The section reveals collection of amorphous urate crystal deposits in irregular
islands partly surrounded by granulomatous inflammatory infiltrates consisting of
lymphocytes, plasma cells, epithelioid histiocytes and multinucleated giant cells.

title: Gouty tophi with suppurative inflammation
content:
Gouty tophi with suppurative inflammation

Microscopic description:
The section reveals collection of amorphous urate crystal deposits in irregular
islands partly surrounded by mild granulomatous inflammatory infiltrates consisting of
lymphocytes, plasma cells, epithelioid histiocytes and multinucleated giant cells.
Besides, suppurative inflam    mation is also noted.

title: Graft infection / ESRD
content:
Suppurative inflammation and foreign body reaction.

Microscopic description:
Section shows suppurative inflammation with collections of
neutrophils, liquified debris of necrotic cells and edema
fluid. Foreign body-like materials consistent with graft, along with aggregates of histiocytes and multinucleated giant cells are seen.

title: HG sarcoma, DDx DDLPS, osteosarcoma
content:
Section shows a high-grade sarcoma which is composed of fascicles of neoplastic spindle cells with marked cellular atypia and tumor necrosis. Focal deposition of lace-like osteoid is identified.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are diffusely and strongly positive for CDK4
but not AE1/AE3, desmin, or CD117. S100 shows a few scattered positive cells.

title: Intramuscular angioma.
content:
Intramuscular angioma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows cystically dilated vessels with focal thrombosis and lined by bland endothelial cells admixed with mature adipose tissue within skeletal muscle tissue.

title: Intravascular fasciitis
content:
Section shows a well-demarcated subcutaneous nodule composed of plump and bland  spindle-shaped myofibroblasts arising from a vessel. Mitoses are seen.

Immunohistochemically, the spindle cells are positive for smooth muscle actin, but negative for CD34, S-100, CD68, EMA or desmin. CD34 highlights the
intravascular growth of lesional cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: Intravascular lobular capillary hemangioma
content:
Section shows intravascular proliferation of capillary-sized lobules of capillary 
channels lined by bland endothelial cells. Mitoses are occasionally seen. No 
malignancy is observed.

title: Kaposi sarcoma.
content:
Kaposi sarcoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows dermal cellular proliferation comprising fascicles of spindle cells with small slit-like spaces containing erythrocytes. These cells are uniform and contain minimally atypical elongated vesicular nuclei. Epidermal ulceration is seen. An infiltrate of neutrophils, lymphocytes and some plasma cells is seen.
Immunohistochemically, the spindle cells express CD31 and CD34 with focal loss of associated SMA-positive pericytes. Nuclear expression of HHV in spindle cells is seen, confirming the diagnosis.

Peripheral margin: _ mm.
Deep margin :__ mm.

title: Lipoma
content:
Lipoma.

Microscopic description:
The mass is composed of closely-packed mature fat cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: Lipoma_form
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Soft tissue, _____, excision --- Lipoma.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of a piece of tissue measuring ____ cm. On cut section, it shows soft and yellowish cut surface without necrosis.

Representative sections are taken and submitted in one cassette.

Microscopic description:
The mass is composed of closely-packed mature fat cells. No malignancy is observed.

title: Lobular capillary hemangioma
content:
lobular capillary hemangioma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a demarcated lesion composed of lobules of capillary channels lined by bland endothelial cells. Mitoses are occasionally seen. No malignancy is observed.

title: Multiple nodules of amorphous substance are encased by granulomatous response wi
content: Multiple nodules of amorphous substance are encased by granulomatous response with histiocytes and foreign body giant cells.

title: Myopericytoma
content:
Microscopic description:

Section shows a well-circumscribed tumor composed of uniform oval-to spindle-shaped tumor cells that display a multilayered, perivascular, concentric growth. The tumor cells contain eosinophilic cytoplasm and plump spindled nuclei. There are increased blood vessels which are
variable in size.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells express SMA  but not S-100, CD34 or desmin.

Taken together, this is a myopericytoma, a benign neoplasm. There is no evidence of malilgnancy.

title: Necrotizing inflammation
content: Section shows extensive and widespread tissue necrosis of the soft tissues accompanied by diffuse inflammatory cells infiltration. No malignancy is observed.

title: Neurofibroma
content:
neurofibroma

Microscopic description:
Sections show skin tissue with a demarcated nodule composed of loosely arranged spindle cells in haphazard arrangement. The lesional cells feature small, hyperchromatic, wavy or buckled nuclei in a variable myxoid to collagenous matrix.
No malignancy is observed.

title: Nodular fasciitis
content:
Section shows a subcutaneous nodule composed of plump and regular spindle-shaped  fibroblasts, arranged in fascicles and tissue culture-like pattern with focal
lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate. Collagen bundles are focally seen.

title: Nodular fasciitis
content:
Nodular fasciitis

Microscopic description:
Section shows a subcutaneous nodule composed of plump and regular spindle-shaped  fibroblasts, arranged in fascicles and tissue culture-like pattern with focal
lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate. Collagen bundles are focally seen.

Immunohistochemically, the spindle cells are positive for smooth muscle actin, but negative for CD34 and S-100. No malignancy is observed.
    
Nodular fasciitis

title: Pericytes SMA
content: Immunohistochemically, the endothelial cells are highlighted by ERG with associated SMA-positive pericytes.

title: Plantar fibromatosis
content:
Plantar fibromatosis

Microscopic description:
Section shows nodules and fascicles of uniform spindle cells with small nuclei in a collagenous stroma. Compressed vascular spaces are seen. The surgical margin is free but very close to the tumor (<1mm).

Immunohistochemically, the spindle cells are positive for SMA, but not S100, CD34 or beta-catenin. Desmin is positive in scattered, individual cells.

NOTE: The tumor shows a significant rate of local recurrence.

Palmar fibromatosis

title: reactive myofibroblastic proliferation
content:
Microscopic description:
Section shows haphazard proliferation of reactive myofibroblasts.

title: Schwannoma
content:
Sections show a well-encapsulated partially cystic and partially solid tumor featuring
alternating hypocellular and hypercellualr areas composed of spindle cells with
indistinct cytoplasmic borders and elongated, wavy nuclei. Hyalinized blood vessels
are seen throughout the lesion. Striking nuclear atypia, favoring degenerative atypia,
with no increased mitotic activity is occasionally seen. Calcifications, hemosiderin-
laden cells and lymphoid aggregates are seen in the fibrous capsule.

title: Schwannoma
content:
Schwannoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a demarcated and encapsulated tumor featuring alternating hypocellular and hypercellualr areas, composed of spindle cells with focal degenerative atypia. No malignancy is observed.

Sections show a picture of schwannoma, which is composed of a biphasic tumor with compact areas of spindle cells (Antoni A pattern) showing occasional palisading, alternating with loosely arranged foci (Antoni B pattern) containing collections of lipid-laden histiocytes. Hemorrhage and cystic degeneration is present.

By immunohistochemistry, the lesional cells are diffusely positive for S100, consistent with the diagnosis.

Schwannoma.
Microscopic description:
Section shows alternating hypocellular and hypercellualr areas composed of spindle cells featuring indistinct cytoplasmic borders and elongated, wavy nuclei. Focal degenerative atypia, with no increased mitotic activity is also seen. Clusters of irregular vascular channels with hyalinized wall and calcification are also seen. No malignancy is observed.

 Large vessels with thick hyalinized walls and luminal thrombi are also seen.
Immunohistochemically, the spindle cells show strong and diffuse S-100 expression with both nuclear and cytoplasmic staining. No malignancy is observed.

title: Solitary fibrous tumor SFT
content:
Solitary fibrous tumor.

Microscopic description:
Sections shows a well circumscribed tumor composed of alternating hypo- and hypercellular areas of bland-looking spindle cells, in a collagenous and vascularized stroma. Perivascular hyalinization is seen. Myxoid change is seen within hypocellular area.

Immunohistochemically,

title: spindle cell hemangioma
content:
spindle cell hemangioma.

Microscopic description:
Sections show a well-circumscribed subcutaneous tumor composed of dilated cavernous-like vascular channels intermixed with solid areas composed of epithelioid and spindle endothelial cells. Vacuolated epithelioid endothelial cells are seen.

Immunohistochemically, the lesional cells are positive for ERG. Associated pericytes are highlighted by SMA.
HHV8 is negative.

title: Spindle cell lipoma
content:
Spindle cell lipoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows a well-circumscribed mass composed of mature adipose tissue admixed
with bland spindle cells and "ropey" collagen.

Immunohistochemically, the spindle cells are positive for CD34 but not CDK4.
No malignancy is observed.

title: Synovial metaplasia
content:
Synovial metaplasia

Microscopic description:
Section shows cystic space with a lining identical to synovial tissue.

title: Tenolysis, tendon
content:
confirmed.

Microscopic description:
Setion shows dense connective tissue consistent with tendon.

title: Tenosynovial giant cell tumor, diffuse type
content:
Tenosynovial giant cell tumor, diffuse type

Microscopic description:
Section shows villous or papillary structures lined by synoviocytes, with multinodular growth of bland-looking histocytoid cells, admixed with scattered osteoclast-like giant cells. There are focal collections of foamy histiocytes. Hemosiderin deposits are seen. No malignancy is observed.

Tenosynovial giant cell tumor, diffuse type. See note.

Microscopic description:
Sections show villous structure lined by synoviocytes, with multinodular or solid growth of bland-looking histocytoid cells, admixed with scattered osteoclast-like
giant cells and prominent foamy histiocytes. Hemosiderin deposits are seen. Focal necrosis is seen. However, no overt atypia is noted. The mitotic activity is around 4 per 10 HPFs.
No malignant transformation is observed.

Note: Diffuse-type tenosynovial giant cell tumor is locally aggressive but non-metastasizing, with a recurrent rate of around 20-50%.

title: Tenosynovial giant cell tumor, localized type TSGCT
content:
tenosynovial giant cell tumor, localized type.

Microscopic description:
Section shows a well circumscribed tumor composed predominantly of bland-looking histiocytoid cells, accompanied by scattered osteoclast-like giant cells. There are focal collections of foamy histiocytes. No malignancy is observed.

title: Thrombectomy
content:
Consistent with thrombus

Microscopic description:
Section shows fibrinoid deposition admixed with some neutrophils and eosinophils.

title: Varicose vein
content:
Varicose vein

Microscopic description:
The vessels show fibrotic wall with marked variation in thickness with thinning at
points of maximal dilatation.

Section shows dilated blood vessels of varying sizes with irregular thin to thick walls. No atypical change of the endothelial cells is noted.

title: 00. TB AR GMS PAS, W-S
content:
Necrotizing granulomatous inflammation.

Microscopic description:
Sections show a granuloma with central caseating necrosis, surrounded by multinucleated giant cells, histiocytes and lymphoplasmacytic infiltration. No fungus is identified by stainings for GMS and PAS. Although no mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast stain and Auramine-Rhodamine stain, mycobaterial infection is highly suspected.

是TB
Necrotizing granulomatous inflammation with mycobacterial infection.

Microscopic description:
Sections show __ tissue exhibiting prominent granulomatous inflammation featuring central caseating necrosis, surrounded by multinucleated giant cells, histiocytes and lymphoplasmacytic infiltration as well as scattered neutrophils. Mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast and Auramine-Rhodamine stains. No fungus is identified by stainings for GMS and PAS.
No mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast stain. No fungus is identified by PAS.

No invasive carcinoma is demonstrated by AE1/AE3.     

No mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast and Auramine-Rhodamine stains.

No mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast stain or TB immunostain. 

No fungus is identified by GMS and PAS.

 The GMS and PAS stains are negative for fungal organism.

No fungus is identified by stainings for GMS and PAS.

No viral inclusion is demonstrated by anti-CMV immunostain. 

Although no mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast stain. Mycobaterial infection cannot be excluded. 

Dr曾:  No mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast stain or Auramine-Rhodamine stain, nor is fungus identified with GM stain.

Stainings for GMS and PAS highlight the fungal hyphae and spores.

title: A small leiomyoma is identified in adjacent pulmonary
content:
A small leiomyoma is identified in adjacent pulmonary
  parenchyma, which is composed of bland spindle
  cells with an extremely low cellularity, with
  lymphoplasmacytic infiltrate at the periphery.
  By immunohistochemistry, the spindle cells are positive
  for desmin, H-caldesmon but not CD34 and HMB45. ER and PR 
  show few positive cells with weak staining. 
  Comment: This finding is most consistent with a leiomyoma.
  However, correlation with the patient's clinical history,
  including any prior surgical resection of uterine
  leiomyomas, is recommended to exclude the possibility of
  benign metastasizing leiomyoma.

title: Abscess, LUNG
content:
Abscess.
Microscopic description:

The lung tissue shows extensive necrosis with mixed inflammatory cells infiltration and foci of abscess formation. No evidence of granuloma or malignancy is observed.

title: Acute and chronic bronchiolitis
content:
Acute and chronic bronchiolitis

Microscopic description:
Sections show peribronchiolar infiltration of mixed
inflammatory cells, accompanied by intraluminal collections
of neutrophils as well as mucus. Mild fibrosis is seen.

title: ADENOCA (should r/o meta)
content:
Adenocarcinoma. see Comment.

Microscopic description:
Section shows lung tissue infiltrated by neoplastic columnar
cells with pleomorphic nuclei arranged in glandular pattern
and in small clusters.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for CK7,
CK20, CDX2 but not TTF-1, Napsin A, GATA3, and PAX8.

Comment:
Metastases must be ruled out first, particularly from the
gastrointestinal system, before rendering a diagnosis of
primary pulmonary enteric-type adenocarcinoma.
Adenocarcinoma; metastasis from colorectal primary should be
excluded. See comment.

Microscopic Findings:
Section shows infiltrative tubuloglandular structures lined
by neoplastic columnar cells with necrosis in a
desmoplastic stroma.

By immunohistochemistry, the tumor cells are positive for
CK20 and CDX-2, and negative for TTF-1, CK7, and p40.

COMMENT: Metastasis, particularly from the lower
gastrointestinal tract, should first be ruled out; in the
absence of such findings, primary pulmonary enteric-type
adenocarcinoma may be considered.

title: ADENOCA invasive mucinous type, LUNG, biopsy
content:
Invasive mucinous adenocarcinoma

Section shows lung tissue infiltrated by columnar cells with abundant intracytoplasmic mucin and basally oriented nuclei, arranged in acinar pattern.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells express CK7 and CK20 but not TTF-1. The overall features can be consistent with a primary pulmonary invasive mucinous adenocarcinoma. Clinical and radiological correlation should be made to exclude metastasis.

title: ADENOCA, LUNG, BIOPSY
content:
Adenocarcinoma.

Microscopic description:
The section shows lung tissue infiltrated by large cells with pleomorphic nuclei and abundant eosinophilic cytoplasm with ____ patterns.
Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells express TTF-1, confirming the diagnosis of adenocarcinoma.

(This case has been reviewed by two pathologists.)

Complex glands 

NOTE: The specimen is not adequate for molecular testing.

title: ADENOCA, mucinous with lepidic pattern, LUNG, BIOPSY
content:
Mucinous adenocarcinoma with lepidic pattern

Section shows lung tissue infiltrated by columnar cells with abundant intracytoplasmic mucin and basally oriented nuclei, arranged in lepidic pattern.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells express CK7 and CK20 but not TTF-1. The overall features can be consistent with a primary pulmonary invasive mucinous adenocarcinoma. Clinical and radiological correlation should be made to exclude metastasis.

title: AIS
content:
Adenocarcinoma in situ.

Microscopic description:
Sections taken from two tumors show atypical pneumocyte proliferation without invasive growth.

adenocarcinoma in situ.

Microscopic description:
Sections taken from nodule show a subpleural tumor
   composed of atypical pneumocyte proliferation in purely
   lepidic pattern without invasive growth. The size is __
   mm. The surgical margin is free of tumor.

title: Anthracosis (LN)
content:
Anthracosis with granuloma like aggregates of 
  histiocytes and nodal hyaline scars is noted.

title: Aortic dissection 2014111730, 2014122706, 2015010808
content:
consistent with aortic dissection.

Microscopic description:
Section shows blood and fibrin clots within the splitted aortic wall, which reveals myxoid degeneration. There is no evidence of malignancy.

title: apical cap
content:
apical cap

Microscopic description:
Sections taken from the nodule show subpleural
fibrosis which features an admixture of eosinophilic
mature collagen and basophilic elastin fiber. The elastin
fiber is highlighted by EVG stain. No invasive carcinoma is
demonstrated by AE1/AE3. Staining for HMB45 is negative.
CD31 shows no vascular lesion. No malignancy is observed.

title: atheroma
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Coronary artery, endarterectomy ---
Consistent with atheroma.

Gross description:
The specimen consists of _ pieces of tissue up to __ cm.
All for section (decal).

Microscopic description:
Section shows atherosclerotic plaque with a few inflammatory
cells (lymphocytes) scattered within the atheroma.

title: atherosclerosis
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Ascending aorta, repair --- atherosclerosis.

Microscopic description:
Section shows aorta with atherosclerotic plaque admixed with
some lymphocytes, and foam cells scattered within the
atheroma. Cholesterol crystals and cell debris are seen.

title: Atypical adenomatous hyperplasia, AAH
content:
Atypical adenomatous hyperplasia.

Microscopic description:
Section shows lung tissue cores with mildly enlarged and hyperchromatic of alveolar lining cells.

title: Atypical cells (biopsy)
content:
Section shows bronchoalveolar tissue focally revealing enlargement and hyperchromatism
 of alveolar lining cells, which can be reactive or neoplastic. Aggregates of foamy 
histiocytes are seen focally in the alveolar space. Although these atypical cells are 

imunopositive for TTF-1, the finding is not reliable to confirm a cancerous lesion in 
this situation. Together with image finding, re-biopsy is mandatory to reach a firm 
diagnosis.

title: Bronchiolar smooth muscle hyperplasia
content:
Bronchiolar smooth muscle hyperplasia.

Microscopic description:
Section shows thickening ot the smooth muscle around bronchiole.

title: Carcinoid tumorlets
content:
Carcinoid tumorlets

_ foci of carcinoid tumorlets are seen on section _, measuring up to _ mm at greatest dimension. It is characterized by proliferation of spindle cells revealing finely stippled chromatin and inconspicuous nucleoli. Mitosis is
hardly identified. 

By immunohistochemistry, the carcinoid tumorlets are immunoreactive to CD56, chromogranin A and synaptophysin.

title: Chronic inflammation, bronchus
content: Section shows bronchoalveolar/ bronchial tissue with chronic inflammatory cells infiltrate in subepithelial region. Neither granuloma nor malignancy is observed.

title: Cryptococcosis
content:
Cryptococcal Infection.

Microscopic description:
The section shows lung tissue cores infiltrated by chronic inflammatory cells and rounded fungal microorganism forming small groups in clear gelatinous capsule, as highlighted by PAS and GMS staining. The overall pictures are compatible with

cryptococcal infection. There is no evidence of malignancy.

SPECILA STAINING: GMS and PAS stains.

title: Degeneration, mitral valve, left, replacement Aortic dissection 2014111730, 2014122706, 2015010808
content:
Degeneration, mitral valve, left, replacement
Section shows valvular tissue with myxoid degeneration.

title: Empyema
content:
Empyema

Microscopic description:
The section taken from the pleura shows soft tissues with extensive necrosis, fibrin clots and infiltration of mixed inflammatory cells.
No granuloma nor evidence of malignancy is observed.

title: Fibrosis with dystrophic calcification, aortic valve, replacement 2015032646
content:
Fibrosis with dystrophic calcification, aortic valve, replacement 2015032646
Section of the aortic valve shows degenerative changes with fibrosis and dystrophic calcification. No malignancy is observed.

title: granulomatous inflammation, consistent with sarcoidosis.
content:
granulomatous inflammation, consistent with sarcoidosis.

Microscopic description:
Sections show nodal tissue revealing small
aggregate of noncaseating epithelioid granulomas with
tightly packed epithelioid cells, admixed with
occasional Langhans giant cells and lymphocytes.
No mycobacterial microorganisms are identified by acid fast
stain. No fungus is identified by PAS.
The features are consistent with sarcoidosis.

title: Lung_ Bullae
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Lung, ___ lobe, VATS wedge resection --- Bullae.

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
The specimen is composed of one piece of lung tissue
measuring _cm and _ gm. Bullae measuring up to _
cm are seen.

Representative sections are submitted in one cassette.

Microscopic description:
The sections show bullae with fibrotic wall and chronic
inflammatory reaction. Mesothelial cell hyperplasia is
focally seen.

title: Metastatic adenocarcinoma, consistent with colorectal origin.  [Lung]
content:
PATHOLOGIC DIAGNOSIS:
Lung, left upper lobe, VATS wedge resection --- 
Metastatic adenocarcinoma, consistent with colorectal origin. 

Gross description:
The specimen consists of a piece of lung tissue measuring
_ x _ x _ cm and weighing _ g. 
On cut section, a tumor measuring _ x _ x _ cm is seen
It is located _ cm to stapled margin.

Representative sections are taken and submitted in 3 cassettes. 
A1: Tumor
A2: Non-tumor
A3: Margin

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
Section shows lung tissue infiltrated by complex tubuloglandular structures lined by neoplastic columnar cells with necrosis. 

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for CDX-2, consistent with metastatic adenocarcinoma of colorectal origin.

title: metastatic adenocarcinoma, consistent with pulmonary origin
content:
metastatic adenocarcinoma, consistent with pulmonary origin.

Microscopic description:
Sections show bone tissue infiltrated by clusters of tumor cells bearing pleomorphic nuclei with eosinophilic cytoplasm. By immunohistochemistry, the tumor cells are positive for TTF-1 and AE1/AE3 but not GATA3, consistent with metastatic carcinoma of pulmonary origin.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)
