title: Minute meningothelial-like nodules.
content:
Minute meningothelial-like nodules.

Microscopic description:
Section shows lung tissue with an interstitial proliferation of cytologically bland oval to blunted spindle cells. Immunohistochemical analysis is performed, and these cells are positive for PR but not AE1/AE3, synaptophysin, chromogranin A or HMB45.

title: Mucoid impaction of bronchi (MIB).
content:
Mucoid impaction of bronchi (MIB).

Microscopic description:
Sections show extensive mucous plugging of the airways accompanied by air-way dilatation.
The mucus has laminated appearance with eosinophlic debris and Charcot-Leyden crystals (allergic mucin)

title: NSCC adenosquamous
content:
Non-small cell carcinoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows lung tissue infiltrated by tumor cells in solid nests, individual cells and tubuloglandular structure.
Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells in tubuloglandular stucture are positive for TTF-1 and CK7; while the ones in
solid nests or as individual cells are positive for p40 and CK7. Staining for calretinin is negative.

COMMENT: This is a non-small cell carcinoma with immunohistochemical stains favor separate adenocarcinoma and squamous components, which indicate the possibility of adenosquamous carcinoma, although this diagnosis can only be made based on a resection specimen.

title: NSCC NOS
content:
Non-small cell carcinoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows tumor cells in solid nests featuring necrosis,
marked cellular atypism and prominent nucleoli in a
lymphoid backgound. 

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for
AE1/AE3 but not p40, TTF-1, synaptophysin, chromogranin A,
PAX8, CD5 or CD117. Calretinin and WT-1 are negative,
excluding mesothelioma.

Taken together, this is a non-small cell carcinoma, not
otherwise specified, with no clear squamous, adenocarcinoma
or neuroendocrine morphology or staining pattern.

Note: This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.

title: NSCC, favor adenocarcinoma.
content:
Non-small cell carcinoma, favor adenocarcinoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows lung tissue infiltrated by tumor cells in solid nests, and individual cells. These tumor cells show enlarged and pleomorphic nuclei with prominent nucleoli and eosinophilic cytoplasm. Brisk mitotic activity is seen.
Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for TTF-1 and negative for p40.

This is a non-small cell carcinoma with immunohistochemical stains favor adenocarcinoma.

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

註: PDCA with a solid pattern:
TTF1 and/or mucin+/ p40 - 
TTF1 and p40 -/ mucin + 
features of adenocarcinoma on cytology specimens, even if the biopsy shows a solid NSCC that is negative for TTF1, p40, and mucin stains.

title: NSCC, favor SQCC
content:
non-small cell carcinoma, favor squamous cell carcinoma

Microscopic description:
Section shows infiltrative nests of tumor cells bearing large, vesicular nuclei with distinct nucleoli
and lightly eosinophilic cytoplasm. Some intermingled lymphocytes are seen. Lymphoid infiltrate is also seen in
adjacent tissue. 

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are strongly positive for p40 and CK5/6 but not TTF-1, or synaptophysin. Staining
for mucincarmine is negative. Weak intensity of nuclear staining of EBER is seen in some tumor cells.

Morphological squamous cell patterns are not present, but supported by stains.
註: In poorly differentiated carcinomas with a solid pattern, if the tumour is p40-positive but TTF1-negative,

title: Organizing pneumonia
content:
Organizing pneumonia.

Microscopic description:
Section shows benign lung tissue exhibiting fibroblastic plugs filling air spaces. No
evidence of granuloma or malignancy is observed.

title: Penumatocele
content: Sections show several thin-walled cysts with rupture. Focal fresh hemorrhage and dilated vessels are noted in lung parenchyma. Some neutrophils are seen in the bronchial space.

title: Pleural nodules; anthracosis; hyalin
content:
The pleural nodules show anthracotic
  pigment in a background of hyaline collagenized tissue.

title: Pneumoconiosis
content:
Section shows lung tissue exhibiting numerous hypocellular, hyalinized collagenous nodules surrouded by histiocytes and lymphocytes in lung parenchyma and subpleural
region. Some spicules are present under polarized light. The histomorphology is   
consistent with pneumoconiosis. No micro-organism is identified by PAS, GMS,

title: primary pulmonary enteric-type adenocarcinoma r/o metastasis first
content:
adenocarcinoma.
see Comment.

Microscopic description:
Section shows lung tissue infiltrated by neoplastic columnar
cells with pleomorphic nuclei arranged in glandular pattern
and in small clusters.

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for CK7,
CK20, CDX2 but not TTF-1, Napsin A, GATA3, and PAX8.

Comment:
Metastases must be ruled out first, particularly from the
gastrointestinal system, before rendering a diagnosis of
primary pulmonary enteric-type adenocarcinoma

title: Pulmonary hamartoma
content:
Pulmonary hamartoma 

Microscopic description: 
The histologic sections reveal the presence of a well circumscribed benign pulmonary lesion composed of hyaline cartilage, fibroadipose tissue, smooth muscle and bland spindle cells set in fibromyxoid stroma. The mesenchymal elements appear to entrap benign respiratory epithelium. These findings support the above diagnosis.

title: SCC, primary or metastatic
content:
Squamous cell carcinoma, primary or metastatic

Microscopic description:
Section shows tissue cores infiltrated by nests of
squamoid cells with cellular atypia and increased mitotic
activity in a desmoplastic stroma. Keratinization is focally
seen.

By immunohistochemistry, the tumor cells are positive for
p40 but not TTF-1. Some residual pneumocytes are highlighted
by TTF-1.

The overall features suggest a squamous cell carcinoma,
either primary or metastatic, which cannot be differentiated
by morphology or immunohistochemical studies. 
Clinico-radiological correlation is recommended.

title: Small cell carcinoma [SmCC]
content:
Small cell carcinoma.

Microscopic description:
Section shows sheets and nests of neoplastic cells revealing medium sized cells with focal tumor necrosis and crush artifact. The tumor cells show finely granular chromatin, inconspicuous nucleoli and scant cytoplasm. Nuclear molding is also seen. 

Immunohistochemically, the tumor cells are positive for AE1/AE3, synaptophysin but not chromogranin A or p40.

title: Thoracis
content:
Adenocarcinoma with lepidic pattern
Section shows lung tissue cores revealing adenocarcinoma with lepidic pattern, lined
by atypical pneumocytes with mildly enlarged and hyperchromatic nuclei.
Immunohistochemically, the atypical pneumocytes are highlighted by TTF-1. Staining for
Ki-67 shows no significant increase in proliferation index.

Comment: An invasive component cannot be excluded in this limited specimen.

 
The lung tissue shows fibrosis with fibrinopurulent exudates coating over the pleural side. The alveolar spaces are filled with fibrin deposition and proliferation of fibroblasts. Reactive hyperplasia of type II pneumocytes and interstitial fibrosis are also discerned. Emphysematous change is present. There is no evidence of malignancy.

title: Thymic cyst (thymus)
content:
Sections show a unilocular cyst lined by ciliated columnar cells. Adjacent thymic 

tissue is seen. No malignancy is observed.

title: TNM 須 clinical imaging correlation
content:
Pathologic Stage Classification (pTNM, AJCC 8th Edition)
Combining the previous report (S07-00539), it shows multifocal lung adenocarcinoma of different histopathologic types in bilateral lobes. Clinical-radiological correlation is mandatory for a proper staging.

title: Adrenal gland_Weiss Criteris
source: 病理報告範本與格式 template
content:
Histopathologic criteria proposed by Weiss

-- High nuclear grade, Fuhrman criteria: absent (grade 2)

-- >5 mitoses per 50 high -power fields: absent (<1 mitosis per 50 high-power fields)

-- Atypical mitotic figures: absent

-- <25% of tumor cells are clear cells: absent

-- Diffuse architecture (>33% of tumor): present

-- Necrosis: absent 

-- Venous invasion (smooth muscle in wall): absent

-- Sinusoidal invasion (no smooth muscle in wall): absent

-- Capsular invasion: absent

The overall features are insufficient to diagnosed as malignancy based on criteria 

proposed by Weiss. 

Modified Weiss (Weiss Revisited) System
Mitotic rate >5/50HPF
Atypical mitotic figures
Clear cells comprising<25% of the tumor
Necrosis
Capsular invasion

*Five criteria, each 0 or 1 (2"mitotic rate+2"cytoplasm +abnormal mitoses+necrosis+capsular invasion) for total of 7 possible points with 3 points diagnostic of malignancy

title: Colon, intestine 憩室破掉
source: 病理報告範本與格式 template
content:
DIAGNOSIS:
Diverticulitis with perforation, _,
*Harmann procedure and drainage of intra-abdominal abscess

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received is a segment of intestine measuring _ cm in length and _ cm in circumference. There is perforation, located _ cm from the nearest surgical cut end, with induration of wall. The reciprocal external surface shows fibrous adhesion and is coated with purulent exudates. Diverticulae are seen, measuring _ cm at greatest dimension.

Representative sections are taken and labeled as follows:

Microscopic description:

    Transmural necrosis and architecturedestruction with replacement by granulation tissue are seen. The adjacent subserosal tissue also reveals fibrosis and aucte and chronic inflammatory cell infiltration.

Sections show a diverticulum with marked inflammatory infiltrate, destruction of the architecture and granulation tissue formation with scattered multinucleated giant cells, extending to the pericolonic fat along with fibrinopurulent coating at subserosal areas. Other diverticulae revealing mixed inflammatory infiltrate near base
of diverticulae are also seen. No malignancy is observed.

小腸破掉
Sections taken from the perforative site shows hemorrhage throughout the intestinal wall with edematous change and congestion of the lamina propria, admixed with scattered neutrophils. No malignancy is observed.

Primary Tumor (pT)
pT0: No evidence of primary tumor
pTis: Carcinoma in situ, intramucosal carcinoma (involvement of lamina propria with no extension through muscularis mucosae)
pT1: Tumor invades the submucosa (through the muscularis mucosa but not into the muscularis propria)
pT2: Tumor invades the muscularis propria
pT3: Tumor invades through the muscularis propria into pericolorectal tissues
pT4: Tumor invades the visceral peritoneum or invades or adheres to adjacent organ or structure
pT4a: Tumor invades through the visceral peritoneum (including gross perforation of the bowel through tumor and continuous invasion of tumor through areas of inflammation to the surface of the visceral peritoneum)
pT4b: Tumor directly invades or adheres to adjacent organs or structures
Regional Lymph Nodes (pN)
pN0: No regional lymph node metastasis
pN1: One to three regional lymph nodes are positive (tumor in lymph nodes measuring ≥0.2 mm), or any number of tumor deposits are present and all identifiable lymph nodes are negative
pN1a: One regional lymph node is positive
pN1b: Two or three regional lymph nodes are positive
pN1c: No regional lymph nodes are positive, but there are tumor deposits in the subserosa, mesentery, or nonperitonealized pericolic, or perirectal/mesorectal tissues.
pN2: Four or more regional lymph nodes are positive
pN2a: Four to six regional lymph nodes are positive
pN2b: Seven or more regional lymph nodes are positive
ITCs (Isolated tumor cells):0.2mm or less
Micrometastasis:0.2mm~2mm (當Meta不用特別寫是micrometastasis)
Distant Metastasis (pM)
M1:  Metastasis to one or more distant sites or organs or peritoneal metastasis is identified   
pM1a: Metastasis to one site or organ is identified without peritoneal metastasis
pM1b: Metastasis to two or more sites or organs is identified without peritoneal metastasis
pM1c: Metastasis to the peritoneal surface is identified alone or with other site or organ metastases

title: John 的 Gross 2015/10
source: 病理報告範本與格式 template
content:
GROSS FINDINGS
The specimen consists of three wedge resections of lung, each with a stapled edge, arbitrarily designated wedges 1, 2 and 3.
Wedge 1: Measures 28 x 14 x 5 mm, weight 1.0g, pleura intact. Cut surface is variegated, brown to black without distinct lesions.
Wedge 2: Measures 33 x 7 x 7 mm, weight 0.5g, pleura intact. Cut surface is variegated, brown to black without distinct lesions.
Wedge 3: Measures 39 x 10 x 5 mm, weight 1.5g, pleura intact. Cut surface shows a solitary black nodule 5 mm in maximum dimension.

The specimen is all embedded.
AA: Wedge 1.
AB: Wedge 2.
AC: Wedge 3 nodule.
AD: Wedge 3 remaining tissue.

559 

GROSS FINDINGS
The specimen consists of a piece of mucosa with submucosa measuring 19 x 15 x 6 mm. The mucosal surface is normal without discrete lesions. The cut surface shows a poorly defined grey to brown, spongy area measuring 9 x 9 x 6 mm which extends to the margin.

The specimen is all embedded.
AA-AB: All tissue embedded. 

561

GROSS FINDINGS
A. Liver specimen (wedge resection of segments VI and VII) 
Segment VI and VII composite: 84 x 73 x 53 mm and 140.0 g 
It had been incised partially when received.
B. Tumor: Solitary 
Segment VI and VII composite: White to cream, 55 x 50 x 47 mm; capsule intact; necrosis 5% 
C. Tumor-free resection margin (in the nearest tumor): 7 mm 
D. Large vessel (venous) invasion: Not identified 
E. Non-tumorous liver: Non-cirrhotic

Representative sections are taken.
RA-RB: Tumour to resection margin.
RC-RD: Tumour to overlying capsule, including necrotic area.
RE: Additional section of tumour to capsule.
RF: Uninvolved liver.

563

GROSS FINDINGS
1. [Esophago-gastric junction] The specimen consists of a segment of distal oesophagus and a segment of proximal stomach, submitted as separate pieces and are described separately.
Esophagus: 123 mm long x 37 mm in diameter. On opening, a poorly defined erythematous, possibly ulcerated area is seen at the mid section, measuring 35 x 32 mm. This is 49 mm from the proximal (true) margin and 40 mm from the distal (false) margin. There is no obvious tumour seen and on sectioning through the wall there are no lesions identified. 50% of this area is embedded. No lymph nodes are identified within the paraesophageal soft tissue. The proximal (true) margin is inked green and the distal (false) margin is left uninked.
Stomach: 109 x 47 x 24 mm. On opening, no lesions are seen on the mucosal surface. A small slip of normal esophageal squamous mucosa is noted at the proximal aspect. Several lymph nodes are identified in the lesser curve fat, measuring up to 8 mm in diameter. One lymph node is suspicious for tumour involvement. The proximal (false) margin is left uninked and the distal (true) margin is inked green.
2. [Paraesophageal LN] The specimen consists of multiple fragments of lymph node tissue and fat measuring from 1-8 mm in maximum dimension. 
3. [Subcarinal LN] The specimen consists of four fragments of lymph node tissue and fat measuring from 5-9 mm in maximum dimension. 
4. [Pulmonary ligament LN] The specimen consists of three fragments of lymph node tissue and fat measuring from 2-11 mm in maximum dimension. 
5. [Cervical LN] The specimen consists of three fragments of lymph node tissue and fat measuring from 3-12 mm in maximum dimension. 
6. [Lesser curve LN] The specimen consists of a piece of fat measuring 26 x 14 x 3 mm. No obvious lymph node is seen.
7. [Celiac axis LN] The specimen consists of a single lymph node measuring 31 x 27 x 23 mm. The cut surface shows the node has been replaced by necrotic tumour. 

Representative sections are taken.
RA: True proximal (esophageal) and distal (gastric) margins.
RB-RE: Representative sections of erythematous area (50% embedded).
RF: Gastroesophageal junction.
RG: 1x lesser curve lymph node with possible tumour.
RH: 3x lesser curve lymph nodes.
RI: 2x lesser curve lymph nodes.
AJ: Paraesophageal LN.
AK: Subcarinal LN.
AL: Pulmonary ligament LN.
AM: Cervical LN.
AN: Lesser curve LN.
RO: Celiac axis LN, representative section.

566

GROSS FINDINGS
1. [Left ovary] The specimen consists of a disrupted cystic ovary with attached fallopian tube, weighing 132.5 g in aggregate. The ovary measures 127 x 78 x 63 mm, and the fallopian tube measures 47 mm in length and 7 mm in diameter. The cut surface of the ovary has a simple, oligocystic appearance with abundant attached blood clot and fibrin debris. The wall measures up to 6 mm in thickness and on sectioning shows a small intramural region with a mucinous appearance; there are otherwise no suspicious areas. The cut surface of the fallopian tube shows no obvious lesions.
2. [Right ovary] The specimen consists of a heavily calcified ovary measuring 37 x 28 x 24 mm and weighing 16 g. The calcification precludes sectioning and the tissue is submitted for decalcification prior to sampling.

Representative sections are taken.
RA-RE: Left ovary, with C including mucinous appearing area.
RF: Left fallopian tube.
RG-RH: Right ovary (post decalcification).

4. Activity grade of necroinflammation (of the Ishak system, 1995): ___/18
I. Periportal or periseptal interface hepatitis (piecemeal necrosis):
(0) Absent
(1) Mild (focal, few portal areas)
(2) Mild/moderate (focal, most portal areas)
(3) Moderate (continuous, around <50% of tracts or septa)
(4) Severe (continuous, around >50% of tracts or septa)
II. Confluent necrosis
(0) Absent
(1) Focal confluent necrosis
(2) Zone 3 necrosis in some areas
(3) Zone 3 necrosis in most areas
(4) Zone 3 necrosis + occasional portal-central (P-C) bridging
(5) Zone 3 necrosis + multiple P-C bridging
(6) Panacinar or multiacinar necrosis

(0)Absent
(1) 1 focus or less per ×10 objective
(2) 2–4 foci per ×10 objective
(3) 5–10 foci per ×10 objective
(4) More than 10 foci per ×10 objective

(0) Absent
(1) Mild, some or all portal areas
(2) Moderate, some or all portal areas
(3) Moderate/marked, all portal areas
(4) Marked, all portal areas

(0) No fibrosis
(1) Fibrous expansion of some portal areas, with or without short fibrous septa
(2) Fibrous expansion of most portal areas, with or without short fibrous septa
(3) Fibrous expansion of most portal areas with occasional portal to portal (p-p)
bridging
(4) Fibrous expansion of portal areas with marked bridging (portal to portal
(p-p) as well as portal to central (p-c)
(5) Marked bridging (p-p and/or p-c) with occasional nodules (incomplete
cirrhosis)
(6) Cirrhosis (probable or definite)
Simple ideogram for comparison between the Ishak (1995), Metavir (1994) and
Scheuer (1991) systems is available in the reference: Duido M, et al. Dig Liver Dis (2011) 43 Suppl 4:S331-43.
H.Special staining studies (specify the findings when applied) 

Reasons for inclusion of Item-G (additional findings in the non-tumorous liver):
The HBV infection, cirrhosis, hepatolithiasis and hepatic schistosomiasis have been repeatedly demonstrated to be potential risk factors for intrahepatic cholagniocarcinoma (ICC). HBV-associated ICC shares many clinicopathological similarities with HBV-associated HCC.
References: (1) Lee CH, et al. Viral hepatitis-associated intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma shares common disease processes with hepatocellular carcinoma. Br J Cancer. 2009; 100(11):1765-70. (2) Zhou H, et al. Hepatitis B virus-associated intrahepatic cholangiocarcinoma and hepatocellular carcinoma may hold common disease process for carcinogenesis. Eur J Cancer. 2010; 46(6):1056-61.

*Grade: 
The classic categories that are still graded using three tier schemes include mucoepidermoid carcinoma, and carcinoma, not otherwise specified. While adenoid cystic carcinoma was historically stratified into three tiers, current classification no longer advocates for this.
Additionally, several tumor types can at least be stratified into low and high grade. High grade transformation (historically designated as dedifferentiation)
refers to the phenomenon of progression from a conventional, usually indolent phenotype, to a pleomorphic aggressive morphology

title: myxoid adrenal cortical adenoma.
source: 病理報告範本與格式 template
content:
myxoid adrenal cortical adenoma.

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:
Sections show a well-circumscribed tumor composed of lipid-
laden clear cells and small-medium size cells with
eosinophilic cytoplasm arranged in cords and pseudoglandular
pattern in a myxoid stroma. No overt atypia is seen.
Mitosis is hardly identified. Focal lymphocytic infiltrate
is noted. Immunohistochemically, the lesional cells are
positive for Melan-A but not AE1/AE3, consistent with the
diagnosis.

Note. Although the tumor lacks morphologic signs of
malignancy according to criteria proposed by Weiss,
there is no final conclusion whether or not the myxoid
subtype have a distinct biologic behavior from conventional
adrenocortical adenomas, which needs to be further
elucidated. Follow-up is recommended.

Reference:
Adrenocortical tumors with myxoid features: a distinct
morphologic and phenotypical variant exhibiting malignant
behavior. Am J Surg Pathol. 2010 Jul;34(7):973-83.

It shows adrenal gland with one demarcated tumor composed of neuroblastic cells that

focally.

1. Histologic Type: Neuroblastoma

title: non-invasive follicular thyroid neoplasm with papillary like nuclear features. (NIFTP) see COMMENT.
source: 病理報告範本與格式 template
content:
non-invasive follicular thyroid neoplasm with papillary
  like nuclear features. (NIFTP) see COMMENT.

Microscopic description:
Sections taken from left lobe tumor show a demarcated tumor
with capsule, composed of microfollicles
lined by follicular cells revealing nuclear enlargement,
chromatin clearing and focal presence of nuclear
pseudoinclusions. Hurthle cell change is seen.

Tumor site: left lobe
Histologic type: non-invasive follicular thyroid neoplasm
  with papillary like nuclear features. (NIFTP)
Tumor Size: 1.4x1x0.9 cm
Surgical margin: Free of tumor, <1 mm from margin.
Ancillary studies: CK19(positive); HBME1(negative); Ki67
  (5%); thyroglobulin(positive); synaptophysin(negative).
Additional findings:
  Right lobe: a small nodular goiter is seen.

COMMENT:
The tumor is completely removed and careful examination of
the tumor capsule interface reveals no capsular or
lymphovascular invasion. The risk of recurrence or other
adverse events is extremely low (<1%) within the first 15
years after resection. NIFTP can be treated with lobectomy,
allowing patients to avoid complete thyroidectomy and
radioactive iodine therapy.

source: 病理報告範本與格式 template
content:
Papillary microcarcinoma (pT1a)

MICROSCOPIC FINDINGS:
The thyroid gland shows features of nodular goiter and a tiny focus of atypical follicular cells with ground glass nuclei, nuclear grooves and pseudoinclusions in a fibrotic stroma.

Lymphatic invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Absent

Parathyroid gland: Absent / right/ left, upper/ lower or ectopic

(This case has been peer reviewed by two doctors.)

Papillary microcarcinomas (also historically referred to as papillary microtumor, occult, latent, or small papillary carcinoma) are not technically a specific variant but refer to papillary carcinomas that are found incidentally measuring 1 cm or less. In spite of their rather common identification in thyroid gland resections, and apparent indolent biologic behavior, it is the recommendation to issue a protocol for all cases in which papillary thyroid carcinoma is found, including subcentimeter carcinomas, whether incidentally found in a thyroid gland removed for other reasons (eg, multinodular goiter), discovered clinically (palpable, visible nodule), and/or discovered by imaging. Given the more sophisticated diagnostic (eg, imaging) modalities currently available, small (ie, less than 1 cm) lesions are being identified and resected.

title: Phyllodes tumor
source: 病理報告範本與格式 template
content:
Microscopic description:
Histologic Type: 
___ Phyllodes tumor, benign

___ Phyllodes tumor, borderline
___ Phyllodes tumor, malignant
Tumor Size: 
Stromal Cellularity: 
___ Mild (stromal nuclei are non-overlapping)
___ Moderate (some overlapping stromal nuclei)
___ Marked (many overlapping stromal nuclei)
Stromal Atypia:
___ None
___ Mild (minimal variation in nuclear size, even chromatin, and smooth nuclear contours)
___ Moderate (more variation in nuclear size and irregular nuclear membranes)
___ Marked (marked nuclear pleomorphism, hyperchromasia, and irregular nuclear contours)
Stromal Overgrowth:
Stromal overgrowth is present when there is at least one low-power microscopic field (4x objective and 10x eyepiece or 22.9 mm2)
that contains stroma only without epithelial elements.
___ Absent
___ Present
___ Cannot be determined
Mitotic Rate:
Malignant phyllodes tumors have greater than or equal to 10 mitoses per 10 high-power fields (40x objective and 10x eyepiece) or
greater than or equal to 5 mitoses / mm2. Benign phyllodes tumors have less than 5 mitoses per 10 HPFs (less than 2.5 mitoses /
mm2).
___ None identified: _________________
___ Specify number of mitoses per 10 high power fields: _________________ mitoses per 10 High
Power Fields (HPFs)
OR
___ Specify number of mitoses per square Millimeter (mm): _________________ mitoses per mm2
___ Cannot be determined
Histologic Tumor Border: 

A circumscribed border is smooth and well-defined or shows a minimally irregular tumor interface with adjacent stroma. Infiltrative
(permeative) tumor borders can be focally infiltrative (unequivocal invasion into adjacent stroma in one low power field) or
extensively infiltrative (unequivocal invasion in a wide area or in multiple foci along the tumor periphery).
___ Circumscribed (well-defined; pushing)
___ Infiltrative (permeative)
+___ Focal
+___ Extensive
___ Cannot be determined
Malignant Heterologous Elements: 
A phyllodes tumor is regarded as malignant when there are malignant heterologous elements, even when not all of the other
histological features of malignancy are present. This rule does not apply if the only heterologous element is well differentiated
liposarcoma. In the breast, those tumors usually lack MDM2 and CDK4 amplifications and have a low metastatic risk. A diagnosis of
malignant phyllodes tumor should therefore not be based solely on the presence of well-differentiated liposarcoma without all of the

Representative sections are taken and labeled as follows:

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTIONS:
1&2. Sections show hyperplastic follicles with papillary infolding, lined by cuboidal epithelium with uniform nuclei and prominent scalloping of the colloid. The stroma contains aggregates of lymphoid tissue with focal germinal center formation. The clinical and pathologic features are consistent with Graves disease. One lymph node is identified over right specimen with preserved nodal architecture. No malignancy is observed.

title: Thyroid_subacute granulomatous thyroiditis
source: 病理報告範本與格式 template
content:
DIAGNOSIS:

Subacute granulomatous thyroiditis (de Quervain thyroiditis), thyroid, bilateral 
lobes, ___

GROSS DESCRIPTION:

#1. [Thyroid, right lobe] measuring _ x _ x _ cm and _ gm. It is vaguely nodular and firm in consistency. 

#2. [Thyroid, left lobe,] measuring _ x _ x _ cm and _ gm. It is  
vaguely nodular and firm in consistency. 

The entire specimen is taken and submitted in _ cassettes.  

MICROSCOPIC FINDING:

Sections show focally destroyed thyroid follicles by marked inflammatory infiltrate 
which is composed of lymphocytes, plasma cells, neutrophils, and epithelioid 
histiocytes. Granulomas containing multinucleated giant cells are seen both within the

thyroid follicles and interfollicular regions. There is also interfollicular fibrosis.

Immunohistochemically, IgG highlights scattered plasma cells without increased 
IgG4/IgG ratio as determined by immunostains, excluding IgG4 related disease. 
There are no mycobacterial microorganisms as demonstrated by acid fast, and 

Auramine-Rhodamine stains. No fungus is demonstrated by GMS and PAS. 

Taken together, the overall features are compatible with subacute granulomatous 
thyroiditis (de Quervain thyroiditis). _ lymph nodes are identified with preserved 
nodal architecture.
